posts.author: mlaflamm

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

Sorry for the long delay before receiving feedbacks. I 
thought I could solve this problem before this date.

After thinking hard about this issue, my conclusion is that 
adding case insensitivity to all dataset implementations is 
far too much trouble for the benefit. I&amp;\#8217;ll follow your 
suggestion and I will improve documentation about this 
issue.

I also created a decorator that adds case insensitivity to 
existing datasets. 

Thanks for having submitted this problem.
posts.subject: #4 Identifiers are case sensitive
posts.author: mlaflamm

posts.text: - **status**: open --> closed
posts.subject: #4 Identifiers are case sensitive
posts.author: mlaflamm

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

Since version 1.5, DbUnit is case insensitive. The 
CaseInsensitiveDataSet is now deprecated.
posts.subject: #4 Identifiers are case sensitive
posts.author: mlaflamm

tickets.description:
Identifiers in the dataset XML file are treated as 
case-sensitive, even if the database is case-
insensitive.

If the database returns the table name as &quot;COMPANY&quot; 
and the dataset uses &quot;Company&quot;, DbUnit will throw a 
NoSuchTableException.  The same basic problem occurs 
with column names.

If DbUnit can't be modified to be smart about case-
insensitive databases, it would be nice to at least 
point this out in the documentation.  It took me quite 
a while to understand why my table names weren't being 
recognized.

tickets.description:
Message: 8284985 
FROM: Brian BrooksDATE: 04/03/2002 11:54:37SUBJECT:  
\[dbunit-user\] Null Values Syntax FlatXmlDataSet 
junit.framework.AssertionFailedError: column count 
\(table=SERVICE\) expected:&lt;2&gt; but was:&lt;4&gt;

With Dbunit 1.2.4, what is the FlatXmlDataSet syntax 
for columns containing NULL values?

I'm trying to use the FlatXmlDataSet like

// Fetch database data after executing your code 
String\[\] tableNames = \{&quot;SERVICE&quot;\};
IDatabaseConnection connection = getConnection\(\);
IDataSet partialDataSet = 
connection.createDataSet\(tableNames\);
ITable databaseData = 
partialDataSet.getTable\(&quot;SERVICE&quot;\);

// Load expected data from an xml dataset
IDataSet expectedDataSet = new FlatXmlDataSet\( 
new FileInputStream\(&quot;expectedDataSetService.xml&quot;\) \);
ITable expectedData = 
expectedDataSet.getTable\(&quot;SERVICE&quot;\);

// Verify if database data match expected data
DataSetUtils.assertEquals\(expectedData, databaseData\);

There are several entries in the SERVICE table that 
correctly contain NULL values.  However, I can't 
figure out the correct syntax for the 
FlatXmlDataSet .xml file.

Right now when I try the syntax from the extract,

&lt;dataset&gt;
&lt;SERVICE CUSTOMERID='1000' SERVICECODE='PCEW'/&gt;
&lt;SERVICE CUSTOMERID='1000' SERVICECODE='PSW'/&gt;
&lt;SERVICE CUSTOMERID='1000' OPERATORID='DG1' 
SERVICECODE='GMTS' SUBSERVICE='APP'/&gt;
&lt;SERVICE CUSTOMERID='1000' OPERATORID='DG1'
SERVICECODE='GMTS' SUBSERVICE='REL'/&gt;
&lt;SERVICE CUSTOMERID='1000' OPERATORID='DG1'
SERVICECODE='GMTS' SUBSERVICE='APP'/&gt;  
&lt;/dataset&gt;

Note that for the first and second row, I want to 
express that the OPERATORID and SUBSERVICE columns 
contain NULL values.

When I try to use this syntax to indicate these 
entries contain NULL values, I get this error

junit.framework.AssertionFailedError: column count
\(table=SERVICE\) expected:&lt;2&gt; but was:&lt;4&gt;
at org.dbunit.dataset.DataSetUtils.assertEquals
\(DataSetUtils.java:114\)
at mytest.testBillyBob\(mytest.java:55\)

which seems to indicate that the assertEquals needs a 
more explicit statement that the OPERATORID and 
SUBSERVICE columns for the first and second rows 
contain NULL values.

Message: 8286691
FROM: Manuel LaflammeDATE: 04/03/2002 14:33:57SUBJECT: 
RE:  \[dbunit-user\] Null Values Syntax The problem is 
that the current implementation uses the first table 
row to
generate the table metadata. Ideally DbUnit should use 
metadata from the DTD
if one exists.
Please log this problem in the bug database. As a 
temporary workaround, you
need to provide the first row without null values or 
to use the XmlDataSet
class.
Manuel Laflamme, Software Architect
Freeborders Canada

Finally, for some reason on my home computer the 
behavior has changed slightly, I get this trace
org.dbunit.dataset.NoSuchColumnException:
&lt;OPERATORID&gt; not a column of table &lt;SERVICE&gt;
at org.dbunit.dataset.AbstractTable.assertValidColumn
\(AbstractTable.java:51\)
at org.dbunit.dataset.xml.FlatXmlTable.getValue
\(FlatXmlTable.java:94\)
at org.dbunit.operation.AbstractBatchOperation.
execute\(AbstractBatchOperation.java:93\)
at org.dbunit.operation.CompositeOperation.
execute\(CompositeOperation.java:68\)
at org.dbunit.DatabaseTestCase.executeOperation
\(DatabaseTestCase.java:84\)
at org.dbunit.DatabaseTestCase.setUp
\(DatabaseTestCase.java:101\)
at mytest.main\(mytest.java:21\)

posts.subject: #1 Detect ambiguous table names
posts.author: mlaflamm

tickets.description:
When no schema is specified, it's possible for DbUnit 
to get column information from multiple tables \(from 
different schemas\).  In this case, the SQL that is 
created won't work.

DbUnit could detect the case where it is getting 
columns from multiple schemas.  It could give some 
sort of &quot;Ambiguous Table Name&quot; error to alert the user 
to the root problem.  When a method like 
DatabaseTableMetaData.getColumns retrieves a list of 
column names, it could look at the schema name for 
each to make sure they're all the same.

This feature is currently available from CVS repository. I 
added system 
property &quot;dbunit.database.supportBatchStatement&quot; 
to disable batch statement usage even if database report 
supporting it. On the other side, you can't force the usage 
of batch statement if the database do not support it.

Usage:
dbunit.database.supportBatchStatement = true/false

posts.subject: #2 UseBatch system property
posts.author: mlaflamm

tickets.description:
Please add a system property that that indicates that 
batch inserts should NOT be performed, even if the 
driver says that it &quot;supports&quot; them.

This flag is necessary to work around a defect in the 
DB2 7.1 JDBC driver. The driver blows up with an 
access violation if you attempt to perform batch 
inserts on a column that contains binary data. For 
example, if you have a simple table like:

CREATE TABLE test \(
id CHAR\(16\) FOR BIT DATA NOT NULL,
scope VARCHAR\(255\) NOT NULL,
CONSTRAINT pk\_test PRIMARY KEY \(id,scope\)\)

Everything works fine if individual inserts are 
performed using a PreparedStatement. Only batch 
inserts fail. Batch inserts work fine on non binary 
data.

dbunit will use batch updates if the driver supports 
it. This is a problem for me due the DB2 bug.

tickets.description:
On line 67 of Assertion.java \(CVS version 1.3, Mar 22, 2002\), in the function
Assertion.assertEquals\(IDataSet expectedDataSet, IDataSet actualDataSet\)
it should read:

if \(actualNames\[i\].equals\(expectedNames\[i\]\) == false\)

instead of

if \(actualNames\[i\].equals\(expectedNames\[i\]\)\)

otherwise an error will be returned if the table names are
the same, instead of when they are different.

tickets.description:
From: Jeremy\_Stein@elementk.com
Sent: Wednesday, April 10, 2002 2:10 PM
To: dbunit-user@lists.sourceforge.net
Subject: \[dbunit-user\] clearParameters on batch 
statement causing error withOracle
\----

Is it necessary for PreparedBatchStatement.clearBatch 
to call
\_statement.clearParameters\(\)?  I'm using Oracle 8i, 
and the thin driver is
giving me an internal error:

java.sql.SQLException: internal error
at 
oracle.jdbc.dbaccess.DBError.throwSqlException
\(DBError.java:114\)
at 
oracle.jdbc.dbaccess.DBError.throwSqlException
\(DBError.java:156\)
at 
oracle.jdbc.dbaccess.DBError.throwSqlException
\(DBError.java:219\)
at oracle.jdbc.dbaccess.DBDataSet.setDBItem
\(DBDataSet.java:310\)
at
oracle.jdbc.driver.OraclePreparedStatement.clearParamet
ers\(OraclePreparedStatement.java:2502\)
at
org.dbunit.database.statement.PreparedBatchStatement.cl
earBatch\(PreparedBatchStatement.java:74\)
at
org.dbunit.operation.RefreshOperation.executeRowOperati
on\(RefreshOperation.java:126\)
at
org.dbunit.operation.RefreshOperation.execute
\(RefreshOperation.java:183\)

If I comment out that line, it seems to work fine... 
but am I breaking
something else?

\(For reference, I've appended an easy way to reproduce 
the error without
using DbUnit.\)

Thank you\!

Jeremy


\-------------------------------------------------------
\---------------
import java.sql.\*;

public class Test \{

public static void main\(String\[\] args\) \{
try \{
Class.forName
\(&quot;oracle.jdbc.driver.OracleDriver&quot;\);
Connection conn = DriverManager.getConnection\(
&quot;jdbc:oracle:thin:@server:1521:db&quot;, &quot;user&quot;,
&quot;pwd&quot;\);
PreparedStatement pstmt = 
conn.prepareStatement\(
&quot;update ORGANIZATION set ORG\_NAME = ? 
where ORG\_ID = ?&quot;\);
pstmt.setString\(1, &quot;Org Name&quot;\);
pstmt.setInt\(2, 123\);
pstmt.addBatch\(\);
pstmt.executeBatch\(\);
pstmt.clearParameters\(\);
\}
catch \(Exception e\) \{
e.printStackTrace\(System.err\);
\}
\}
\}
\-------------------------------------------------------
\---------------

tickets.summary: Allow duplicate table name in dataset
tickets.description:
Please allow multiple tables with the same name to 
coexist in a dataset.  Currently, DbUnit calls 
getTableNames to get the list of tables and then calls 
getTable for each one.  Instead, there should be a 
method getTables that returns the array of tables.


Message from Manuel:

I now fully understand what you intend to do. I agree 
with you, this is necessary to add a method 
like &quot;ITable\[\] getTables\(\)&quot; to allows multiple tables 
with same the same name to coexist in a dataset.

Please submit a feature request for this; this is 
definitely something useful to add.

names name feature\!

to use a special string \(like &quot;\[NULL\]&quot;\) to designate a 
NULL value.  This would allow users to explicitely set 
a column to null, without having to worry about 
whether the column will be treated as a &quot;none column 
value&quot;.

tickets.description:
IdentityInsert.execute has a logic error that causes it to skip 
the call to commit when the table has no identity column.  If 
this is the last table, the transaction is left open \(and 
automatically rolled back when the application closes\).

The fix is attached.  \(The only change is to move the call to 
commit out of the if block.\)

posts.author: oman002

tickets.description:
Please change InsertIdentityOperation to allow it to share 
an existing transaction rather than assume that autocommit 
is on.

One database testing technique is to do the following:
1\) Begin a transaction
2\) Set up test data \(with DbUnit\!\)
3\) Call the tested method
4\) Verify the results \(with DbUnit\!\)
5\) Rollback the transaction

A rollback ensures that all data changes are correctly 
removed even if the tested method made unexpected data 
changes.  And it can be faster than deleting the records.  
\(For example, when a table has many unindexed foreign 
keys pointing to it.\)

This technique cannot currently be used with 
InsertIdentityOperation because this operation expects to 
control the transaction itself.  It would be nice if there was a 
switch of some sort that would cause it to use the existing 
transaction without comitting or changing the autocommit 
flag.

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

This is not a bug but a feature\! See feature request 542034 at 
http://sourceforge.net/tracker/index.php?
func=detail&amp;aid=542034&amp;group\_id=47439&amp;atid=449494.

This situation occurs when no schema is specified and that 
DbUnit detect that it is getting columns information from 
multiple tables having the same name and located in different 
schemas.

You can solve this problem in three different ways:
1\.	Provide the schema name when creating the 
database connection.
2\.	Ensure that the connection is restricted to access 
only one schema.
3\.	Use fully qualified table names 
\(http://www.dbunit.org/howto.html\#multipleschema\).

tickets.description:
This is a follow-up to my simple test based on:

&lt;dataset&gt;
&lt;table name=&quot;NAME&quot;&gt;
&lt;column&gt;TXT&lt;/column&gt;
&lt;row&gt;
&lt;value&gt;Jane Smith&lt;/value&gt;
&lt;/row&gt;
&lt;/table&gt;
&lt;/dataset&gt;

As you already know, this causes clearParameters 
problems in the 1.3 release that have been fixed in the 
current source in CVS.  However, it also breaks the 
current source \(at least with an Oracle 8.1.6 thin driver\), 
just in a different way.  I hunted through and found what 
appeared to be the two key files that contained changes 
related to clearParameters:

PreparedBatchStatement, SimplePreparedStatement

If I take the original 1.3 source, and only comment out 
the three places that were clearing the parameters on a 
batch, then my own test case works.  If I take the entire 
batch of source that I yanked today via Ludovic's \(way 
cool\) cvsgrab, then during the setup for my test \(which 
simply reads the database that was set up, and 
compares the read value to the expected value\) the 
following error is generated:

org.dbunit.database.AmbiguousTableNameException: 
KEYTABLE
at org.dbunit.database.DatabaseDataSet.getTableMap
\(DatabaseDataSet.java:132\)
at 
org.dbunit.database.DatabaseDataSet.getTableMetaData
\(DatabaseDataSet.java:161\)
at 
org.dbunit.operation.AbstractBatchOperation.getOperatio
nMetaData\(AbstractBatchOperation.java:57\)
at org.dbunit.operation.AbstractBatchOperation.execute
\(AbstractBatchOperation.java:111\)
at org.dbunit.operation.CompositeOperation.execute
\(CompositeOperation.java:67\)
at org.dbunit.DatabaseTestCase.executeOperation
\(DatabaseTestCase.java:84\)
at org.dbunit.DatabaseTestCase.setUp
\(DatabaseTestCase.java:101\)
\(etc.\)

Since I've found a workaround for the problem I originally 
encountered, I'm all set, but I thought you might want to 
know about this new one too.

tickets.description:
Group:  v1.4  \(can't select that from drop down\)

A co-worker of mine discovered this:

The second argument to the DatabaseConnection
constructor is the Schema name.  It seems like this
field is case sensetive, because there is an exception
if case is incorrect.  This fact would btw be helpful
if noted somewhere -- maybe FAQ?  We weren't used to
that, our SQLPlus is case-insensetive.

The wired thing now is that no matter what case you
use, Connection.getRowCount\(...\) will work either way.
This makes the problem more difficult to detect.

I guess this isn't a bug, but it's somewhat confusing.
Made the &quot;case-sensetive&quot; issue more difficult to catch.

thanks a bunch -- great project,
Philip

tickets.description:
Create a table with columns that allow the null value.
Export data from this table to a FlatXmlDataSet. and
write to a file. During testing, obtain a new
FlatXmlDataSet from the same table, which is
theoretically equal. Assertion.assertEquals\(\) fails
claiming that the column counts are wrong. This is
because the version of the FlatXmlDataSet that was
written to disk lacks the columns where the value is null.

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

You can do that using the CompositeTable class like this: 

Assertion.assertEquals\(expected, 
new CompositeTable\(expected.getTableMetaData\(\), actual\)\);

This is very important to not invert Actual and Expected 
tables. The Actual table is the database table snapshot you 
want to verify against the Expected table. As its name imply, 
the Expected table contains the expectation values that 
usually comes from an XML dataset or can be created in 
memory. 

The Actual table MUST contain at least ALL the Expected 
table columns. Extra columns can exist in the Actual table 
but not in the Expected one.

tickets.description:
If an invalid xml file is given to dbunit, this is ignored.
dbunit looks on DTD and assumes that the xml file
contains correct columns.

I had a problem with that when my DTD has fewer columns
than the actual XML. This meant that those columns
values were not inserted into the table, and no error
reported.
It took a lot of time until I realized what is happening.

The problems seems to be because Electric XML is a
non-validating parser.
The solution is to use a validating parser \(like Xerces
or whatever\).

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=86901

I had this problem, too.

The clue is, you must always provide a DTD file.

This is described here:
http://dbunit.sourceforge.net/components.html\#FlatXmlDataSet

&quot;If the xml document does not contain a document type
declaration, the metadata is generated from the first row of
each table. This is highly recommended to always provide a
document type declaration since DbUnit will think your table
is missing some columns if the first row has one or more
null values.&quot;

tickets.summary: allow different encoding when exporting database in xml file
tickets.description:
In order to export correctly a database with a special 
encoding, you should add a new method in 
FlatXmlDataSet :

public static void write\(IDataSet dataSet, Writer out\);

with exactly the same body that write\(IDataSet dataSet, 
OutputStream out\) has.

Example :
FlatXmlDataSet.write\(partialDataSet, new 
OutputStreamWriter\(new FileOutputStream
\(&quot;partial.xml&quot;\), &quot;ISO-8859-1&quot;\)\);

tickets.summary: allow to ignore specified columns in Assertions class
tickets.description:
There are some scenarios when data is generated by 
some custom code \(i.e. JDBC or EJB BMP code\) and to 
validate such data with DBUnit it is necessary to ignore 
some columns with autogenerated numbers. The 
simplest implementation is to use FlatXMLDataSet, but 
values for the generated columns \(usually primary keys\) 
should be ignored.

I suggest to introduce a new method in the Assertion 
class which will ignore data in the specified columns.

Attaching the patch to DatabaseSequenceFilter.java that I
made for my own personal use to solve this bug.  Changes
basically boil down to:

\- Removed TableSequenceComparator static nested class, and
moved its getDependentTableNames\(\) method out into
DatabaseSequenceFilter class
\- Moved TableSequenceComparator's &quot;\_dependentMap&quot; private
field out into DatabaseSequenceFilter
\- Changed code in getSortedTableNames to use Lists: the code
loops over the String\[\] tableNames parameter, building a
LinkedList in which the table names are placed into the
proper order.

Possible improvements \(?\):
\- Using DatabaseMetaData.getImportedTableNames\(\) might offer
better performance
\- Might be worth performing &quot;extract method&quot; refactoring
against code in getSortedTableNames\(\)
\- Might make more sense to set the IDatabaseConnection
\(argument to constructor\) as a field, but doing so would
require undesirable changes to the DatasetFilter strategy
currently in use.  I don't think that changing this pattern
is a good idea.

Sure.
What is meant with &quot;having table names that 
contain keys&quot;?
Easiest is to show by an example.
Just look in class org.dbunit.database.DatabaseDataSet.
There is a method 
private Map getTableMap\(\) throws DataSetException \{..\}
In this method there are the statements:
// prevent table name conflict
if \(tableMap.containsKey\(tableName\)\)
\{
throw new AmbiguousTableNameException
\(tableName\); //THIS LINE HAS BEEN COMMENTED OUT\!
\}

The passus &quot;that contains keys&quot; is an offspring of the method 
name containsKey\(..\) used here. So a database table that 
contains a substring in its name, that is equal to a keyword of 
the database is seen as invalid right now.

As remarked in the above shown lines of code, I just 
commented out one line to fit our needs. More elegent would 
be to allow configuration in this matter.

Best regards

Klaus

PS: I do like your framework and have several ideas of 
improvements. Our project is at a point where I could make 
some extensions to dbUnit. What do you think?
posts.subject: #17 Allow table names with conflict in DatabaseDataSet
posts.author: klausikm

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

You seem to believe that your problem is caused by a table 
name that is a substring of another table name. This cannot 
be the case as the table name must be \*exactly\* equals 
\(even by case\). 

Your problem is more likely caused by a table that exists in 
more than one schema. This is a FAQ. You can get more info 
at:
http://www.dbunit.org/faq.html\#AmbiguousTableNameExceptio
n

I think this is unsafe to allow DbUnit accessing multiple tables 
with the same name. Import and export becomes 
unpredictable in such environment.

\---

Don't hesitate to post your DbUnit extension suggestions. I 
cannot guarantee they will end up in DbUnit exactly as you 
thought or coded them. Also be aware that the current CVS 
version is a lot different from the 1.5.1 distribution. The best 
place to discuss about this is in the developer \(or even in the 
user\) mailing list.

want to read/write to a table if it has access to multiple 
ones. In this situation you must restrict DbUnit to only one 
schema or use qualified table names. Sorry but DbUnit is not 
able to perform mind reading magic\! ;\)

tickets.description:
Most projects start like this:
1\. Create a database
2\. Sometimes notice dbUnit and use it

This raises a problem when having table names that 
contain keys.

DatabaseDataSet.getTableMap\(\) the

is no possibility of configuration I had to manually edit 
the source, compile it and use it modifiedly.

Wouldn't it be senseful to allow the developer to 
configure the named functionality?

CVS repository version \(using SAX instead of the Electric XML 
parser\). These datasets can only be accessed in a forward 
only fashion thus preventing to create the metadata from 
another row than the first. 

This \*feature\* allows DbUnit to import and export very large 
XML dataset without getting the OutOfMemoryError.

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

Reply to Tim McAuley:

Effectively your problem is different and mostly related to the 
usage of PreparedStatment by DbUnit. Each 
PreparedStatement is generated from the metadata of the 
processed table. Since all PreparedStatement parameters 
require to be bind to a value, this is impossible to let the 
database set default values.

A few months ago, I started to work on a special dataset 
decorator that exposes one table per set of consecutive row 
having the same non-null columns. Null columns are omitted 
from each table metadata. 

For example, the following table would be exposed as two 
different tables \(having the same name\). The first table 
comprises the first two rows \(metadata columns=c1, c2, c3\) 
and the second being the last row \(metadata columns=c1, 
c2\).

&lt;table c1=&quot;1&quot; c2=&quot;2&quot; c3=&quot;3&quot;&gt;
&lt;table c1=&quot;4&quot; c2=&quot;5&quot; c3=&quot;6&quot;&gt;
&lt;table c1=&quot;7&quot; c2=&quot;8&quot;&gt;

Unfortunately, I stopped to work on this class and never 
committed it in CVS. I'm just hoping there is still a copy of it 
somewhere on my system.

Reply to Tim McAuley:

I dropped the idea of the special dataset decorator and 
modified the InsertOperation and RefreshOperation directly. 
These operations do not override database default values 
anymore when inserting null. Null values are now omitted from 
the insert statement. These operations use multiple 
statements for the same table when null values distribution 
varies from row to row.

This modification is now available from the CVS repository.

posts.subject: #36 FlatXMLDataSet column issue
posts.author: mlaflamm

tickets.description:
When reading from an xml dataset without a metadata 
dtd, columns are ignored if the first row of a table in the 
xml does not include all the columns of the table. 

return new FlatXmlDataSet\(new InputStreamReader
\(stream\)\);

This problem occurred when using xml generated from a 
database using FlatXmlDataSet.write\(\). \[The first row 
had three null columns in the database\]

Looking through the code, the problem stems from the 
FlatXmlDataSet.createTable\(\) method: the first row 
encountered is used as the metadata for the following 
rows for the table:

Element sampleRow = rows\[0\];
...
metaData = FlatXmlTable.createMetaData\(sampleRow\);

This can be simply resolved by the following:

metaData = FlatXmlTable.createMetaData\(getLargestRow
\(rows\)\);

where getLargestRow\(\) returns the row with the most 
columns. \[I realise that there could still be a problem 
with disparate columns, however\]

I realise that this solution isn't ideal as incorrect columns 
may be included if the xml is invalid, but it works for xml 
files generated using the same class.

The fix is available from the CVS repository

tickets.summary: REFRESH and FlatXmlDataSet NULL not compatible
tickets.description:
The DatabaseOperation.REFRESH doesn't seem to be 
compatible with FlatXmlDataSet's NULL representation.  
If I have a unit test whose setup expects NULL in a 
particular column, and some tests modify that value, 
then I cannot use REFRESH as the setupOperation.

Instead, I have to delete only those items in the XML 
dataset, and then insert them.  Otherwise the non-NULL 
value created by a previous test invalidates the setup of 
new tests.

Perhaps either:

\(1\) you can create a new representation of NULL for the 
XML datasets

or 

\(2\) you can perform a DELETE, then INSERT for the 
REFRESH operation

\(1\) seems better since \(2\) may have bad side effects in 
some environments \(unwanted cascading deletes or 
something\).

posts.author: rlogiacco

tickets.description:
Encountered an issue with FlatXmlDataSet's and NULL 
column values.  To indicate a column is NULL you simply 
leave off the name of the column off your xml record.  
Doing so on the first record being inserted causes all 
other future columns to also be null.

For example:
&lt;TBLFRUIT NAME='ORANGE' COLOR='ORANGE'/&gt;
&lt;TBLFRUIT NAME='APPLE'/&gt;

would cause the table FRUIT to have 2 entries in it:
ORANGE      ORANGE
APPLE

But if you reversed it:
&lt;TBLFRUIT NAME='APPLE'/&gt;
&lt;TBLFRUIT NAME='ORANGE' COLOR='ORANGE'/&gt;

would cause the table FUIT to have the following:
APPLE
ORANGE

tickets.description:
I am trying to run the same unit tests against both
Oracle 9i and MS SQL 2000, and have run into a problem
how dbUnit handles bit values.  It seems with MS SQL
likes values of 'true' and 'false' while Oracle likes
zeros and ones.  

Typically, my test cases consists of setting up the
database for testing using XML flat files, running the
test, and then comparing the resulting database with
another set of XML flat files.  Ideally, it would be
nice to have one set of test files for either database,
but because of the variations in how BIT values are
displayed between databases that doesn't work.  So, I
have been forced to maintain two sets of files with the
resulting uglyness.

Generally, though I like dbUnit.  It's been very handy
at testing complex database applications, that in my
case is a combination of EBJ and stored procedures.  I
have been recommending it.  Keep up the good work.

Dan

tickets.summary: Generate DTDs with choice instead of sequence
tickets.description:
FlatDtdDataSet generates a rule for the element
'dataset' that looks like:

<\!ELEMENT dataset \(table\_a\*, table\_b\*, table\_c\*, ...\)>

This imposes a strict order in which the actual rows
must appear \(actually, lexicographical order\).

I think it would be more correct if it was:

<\!ELEMENT dataset \(\(table\_a|table\_b|table\_c|...\)\*\)>

task to generate an XML file from the old database.

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

Is the problematic column having a database default value? 

DbUnit 2.0 does not include columns with null value in Insert 
statements anymore to order to support database default 
values. Update statements work as before and affect all 
dataset columns including those with null values. The 
REFRESH operation uses either Insert or Update depending if 
row to refresh exists or not in your database.

I plan to make this feature configurable in DbUnit 2.1 since 
this can cause side effect problems.

tickets.description:
\(I'm using dbUnit 2.0...\)

Read the docs, know that I should be using dtd when
repopulating a DB will null values in some of it's
columns...

I have my dataset: full.xml, and my schema / metadata:
full.dtd

I tried this first:

1\) FlatXmlDataSet readDataSet = new FlatXmlDataSet\(new
FileInputStream\("full.xml"\), new
FileInputStream\("full.dtd"\)\);

But some of the columns were still being missed when
reading it in... 

Stepping through with a debugger, Line 109 of
FlatXMLProducer:

if \(\_metaDataSet \!= null\)
\{
return
\_metaDataSet.getTableMetaData\(tableName\);
\}

\_metaDataSet is null \(but the entity resolver is set to
my dtd..\)

The workaround / fix is to use:

2\) FlatDtdDataSet metaData = new FlatDtdDataSet\(new
FileInputStream\("full.dtd"\)\);
3\) FlatXmlDataSet readDataSet = new FlatXmlDataSet\(new
FileInputStream\("full.xml"\), metaData\);

\(which \!= \(1\) above...\)

This might just be a documentation issue... nothing in
the API docs seemed to indicate that \(1\) wouldn't use
the dtd provided for table metadata...

Thanks\!  Great Lib\!

Chris

Your could use ant's property file feature
you need to write a file like

db.driver=com.sybase.jdbc3.jdbc.SybDriver
db.userid=myuserid
db.password=mypassword
db.schema=myschema
db.url=jdbc:sybase:Tds:VRC840:50000/test\_db
and put this line into your build.xml

<property file="yourdatabase.properties"/>

then you can use there properties in your dbunit task:

<dbunit 
driver="$\{db.driver\}"
	schema="$\{db.schema\}"
	password="$\{db.password\}"
	userid="$\{db.userid\}"
	url="$\{db.url\}"
	...	> 
... </dbunit

tickets.summary: ant: connection properties with properties file
tickets.description:
It should be possible to define the connection
properties with a properties file.

So:

<dbunit driver="driver" url="url" userid="u" password="p">
...
</dbunit>

can also be written as

<dbunit properties="propertiesfile">
...
</dbunit>

with a properties file containing the following:

dbunit.connection.username=u
dbunit.connection.password=p
dbunit.connection.url=url
dbunit.connection.driver=driver

you how to ignore some columns in the comparison. To works, 
the actual \(database\) table MUST contain at least ALL the 
columns from the expected \(xml\) table. Extra columns can 
exist in the actual table but not in the expected one. 

Assertion.assertEquals\(expected, 
new CompositeTable\(expected.getTableMetaData\(\), 
actual\)\);

tickets.description:
This is based on the 2004-05-03 cvs snapshot.

Assertion.assertEqualls\(ITable, ITable\) doesn't ignore 
primary keys as the javadocs claim.  There's no code 
support for it.

/\*\*
\* Asserts that the two specified tables are equals. 
This method ignore the
\* table names, the columns order, the columns data 
type and the primary
\* keys.
\*/
public static void assertEquals\(ITable expectedTable, 
ITable actualTable\)

posts.text: ColumnsExcludedTable and ColumnsExcludedTableMetaData
posts.subject: #23 SOLUTION: Table Decorator to ignore columns
posts.author: mkienenb

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

Like I wrote in \[947909\], I created the IColumnFilter interface 
a few weeks ago having in mind to use it in a decorator 
similar to your proposal. 
http://cvs.sourceforge.net/viewcvs.py/dbunit/dbunit/src/java
/org/dbunit/dataset/filter/IColumnFilter.java

I now have created a first decorator, FilteredTableMetaData, 
using this interface. Here is an example that excludes all 
columns with prefix "PK" and suffix "TIME":

DefaultColumnFilter columnFilter = new DefaultColumnFilter\(\);
columnFilter.excludeColumn\("PK\*"\);
columnFilter.excludeColumn\("\*TIME"\);

FilteredTableMetaData metaData = new FilteredTableMetaData
\(
table.getTableMetaData\(\), columnFilter\);
table = new CompositeTable\(metaData, table\);

posts.subject: #23 SOLUTION: Table Decorator to ignore columns
posts.author: mlaflamm

tickets.summary: SOLUTION: Table Decorator to ignore columns
tickets.description:
One of the most requested items is the ability to 
selectively ignore certain table columns, like primary 
keys and record creation dates.

554249 None column values
602171 Ability to ignore columns 
726366 allow to ignore specified columns in Assertions 
class 

There are no easy ways to do this in standard DBUnit.

Here are two classes that handles this situation by 
creating a table decorator that hides specified columns 
from visibility.  They are fully documented and are ready 
to drop in as-is to DBUnit or into any project that uses 
DBUnit.

Example usage:

String\[\] excludedColumnNameArray = new String\[\] 
\{"TIMESTAMP", "PRIMARY\_KEY\_ID"\};
ITable actualTable = new ColumnExcludingTable
\(actualDataSet.getTable\("DATABASE\_TABLE"\), 
excludedColumnNameArray\);
ITable expectedTable = new ColumnExcludingTable
\(sendDataSet.getTable\("DATABASE\_TABLE"\), 
excludedColumnNameArray\);

Assertion.assertEquals\(expectedTable, 
actualTable\);


\[Note:  I would have appended this to issue 602171, but 
I don't have the ability to attach files to that case.\]

posts.subject: #55 Values with newlines cannot be compared with assertEquals
posts.author: mlaflamm

posts.subject: #55 Values with newlines cannot be compared with assertEquals
posts.author: mlaflamm

posts.subject: #55 Values with newlines cannot be compared with assertEquals
posts.author: mlaflamm

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=351929

Actually, linefeeds and carriage returns are not legal in
the values of attributes in XML \(see
http://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-20040204/\#AVNormalize\). I
discovered this because I noticed that when you export data
that has newlines, those newlines are lost upon import. If
you replace those newlines with &\#xA, they are imported
properly.

Actually, if I understand the XML spec correctly, all
whitespace other than the space character needs to be
escaped. So I think escapeXML actually needs these lines added:
str = replace\(str, "\t", "&\#x9;"\); // tab
str = replace\(str, "\n", "&\#xA;"\); // linefeed
str = replace\(str, "\r", "&\#xD;"\); // carriage return

-b

tickets.description:
Task: using FlatXmlDataSet to set Sybase Database to
known state for unit tests.

Condition:
1\. Database contains exisitng data that should not be 
touched. 
2\. Database is an exisiting developped DB. 
Identity-column did not have sql\_data\_type as
udt\_identity, or DatabaseMetaData.getPrimaryKeys\(\)
could not return any data. 

Problem: 
1\. Because the current implementation of determining of
identity column is based on sql\_data\_type contains
"\_identity", \( see
org.dbunit.ext.mssql.InsertIdentityOperation \), 
indentity\_insert cannot be turned on. 

2\. Because DeleteOperation \( and UpdateOperation \) get
primary Key column by using
DatabaseMetaData.getPrimaryKeys\(\), which returns null,
the two operation could not succeed. 

3\. Tried to use subclass of
DeleteOperatioin/UpdateOperation/AbstractOperation, but
constructors or other methods require sub class in the
same package, it did not work. 

Question:
1\. Is there any implementation for sybase in terms of
inserting identity columns and deleting them for tear down?
2\. If there is no such implementation, is there anyone
working on it?

Chris

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1048232

Sorry, my mistake. There is no problem with the code it is 
how you define your rows in Excel. 

If you change a format of a cell or clear a format in an empty 
row \(eg Edit - Clear - Format or All\) it will include this row in 
reading the dataset even though your row is completely 
empty. 

To fix this I added junk data in the suspect rows and then 
selected the rows and performed a delete. There is probably a 
better way than this but at least this worked for me.

posts.subject: #60 dbunit don't use oracle's synonym
posts.author: mlaflamm

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

This should be possible to use synonyms with DbUnit by 
modifying the table type property like for views. 
http://www.dbunit.org/faq.html\#views

I do not recommend using synonyms since the data is shared 
among multiple schemas. So CLEAN\_INSERT and REFRESH 
operations will modify data for all shared schemas and affect 
multiple users. I strongly suggest you to use one schema per 
developer without synonyms. This is the safest way to run 
your tests without affecting other users.

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=466344

I initially thought you were getting an exception or an error 
message. So if I understand well all other values are correctly 
inserted excepting values of the VARCHAR2 \(3000\) column. 

Table's metadata is deduced from the first row of each table. 
DbUnit may think that a column does not exist if no value is 
defined for it on the first row. 
http://www.dbunit.org/components.html\#flatxmldataset

I must admit that you haven't got a NoSuchColumnException 
as stated in the doc. The doc is probably not accurate 
anymore and I will update it. 

Existing workarounds are to use a DTD or the 
ReplacementDataSet decorator.
http://www.dbunit.org/faq.html\#generatedtd
http://www.dbunit.org/components.html\#replacementdataset

Let me know if this helps

tickets.summary: Row count error comparing empty expected table data
tickets.description:
Source code check out from cvs 13/5/2004 at 9.54 GMT.

Line 112 of org.dbunit.Assertion.assertEquals\(ITable,
ITable\).

The error occurs when comparing expected data with
actual data, when there are no rows. Hence, the
expected data file is empty, for that table, i.e.
contains no info, or metadata.

When the method compares the two tables it complains
that there were no table columns expected, but that the
actual table has columns.

Easily fixed if the both the actual and expected table
data objects are compared for a rowcount == 0, near the
beginning, in which case you can just return.

Basically, the row count check needs to be performed first.

tickets.description:
I am running an ant build.xml file using Eclipse
V3.0.0. I am using a DB2 database backend. I am using
JDK 1.4.2.

I need to provide the following xml to ant to have
things work:

		<taskdef name="dbunit"
classname="org.dbunit.ant.DbUnitTask"/>
		<dbunit driver="$\{jdbc.driver\}" url="$\{jdbc.url\}" 
			userid="$\{jdbc.test.user\}"
password="$\{jdbc.test.password\}"
useQualifiedTableNames="1" schema="FOO">
			<operation type="CLEAN\_INSERT" src="/tmp/result.xml">
			</operation>
		</dbunit>

Note 'useQualifiedTableNames="1"'. If this attribute is
set to "true", an exception is thrown:

BUILD FAILED: \(...\)/build.xml:176:
org.dbunit.dataset.NoSuchTableException: RESULT

This is consistent with a non-qualified table name
error returned from DB2. No warning about a
non-recognized attribute value is returned from ant or
the ant extensions.

According to http://dbunit.sourceforge.net/anttask.html
, the default values for these attributes are "true"
and "false" values. I assumed these were literal
values, which they turn out not to be.

I think a FAQ entry \(to deal with this issue in the
current version\), a warning when parsing the attribute
if the provided value is not recognized, and a rewrite
of the documentation and/or the attribute parser would
be in order.

posts.subject: #25 Xml data set support for Oracle sequence values
posts.author: svandenbussche

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=920526

I've extended FlatXmlDataSet and FlatXmlProducer to allow 
this type of syntax in the XML: 

<dataset>
<SOME\_TABLE ROW\_ID="id:=SEQ\(SOME\_SEQUENCE\)" 
COL1="value1" COL2="value2/>
<SOME\_CHILD\_TABLE FK\_COL="$\{id\}" COL3="value3"/>
</dataset>

The above will generate the next sequence value and assign it 
to ROW\_ID in the first row and at the same time, creates a 
variable named "id" that can be used in subsequent rows.

If you are interested in getting this enhancement, it may help 
you with your request.

tickets.description:
It would be nice if src/java/org/dbunit/ant/Export.java
could be changed to accomodate the
DatabaseSequenceFilter. This would allow me to automate
my exports let DBUnit figure out what needs to be
exported first, second, etc. all from my ant task.  I'm
suggesting that the code be changed something similar
to the following, or for an option to be added to
"switch" into these lines of code.

ORIGINAL CODE:
IDataSet dataset = getDatabaseDataSet\(connection,
\_tables, true\);

REVISED CODE:
ITableFilter filter = new
DatabaseSequenceFilter\(connection\);
IDataSet dataset = new FilteredDataSet\(filter,
connection.createDataSet\(\)\);

Thanks for your help,
Matthew McCullough
matthewm AT ambientideas.com

tickets.summary: Values not set up if 1st record val. is null
tickets.description:
I have set up a table where some values can be null. 
Then I exported it to XML to further use it to get the 
original data before each test run.

I noticed that some data was not imported into the 
Oracle DB during the setup. I tried to isolate the 
problem and came up with the following:

If the first record in a table does not contain a value in 
a certain column, the column is omitted as a whole. So 
if any following recored does contain a value for that 
column, it's just ignored.

But if I insert a value into the first record into that 
column, all following values for that column appear, too.

mat

tickets.summary: Feature to accept trim data values for Character Data Types
tickets.description:
I am exporting the data from Database to XML file using 
DBUnit. DBUnit pads extra spaces for Character 
DataTypes when the data is less than defined column 
length. 
I am using these Exported XML Files for assertions. 
Whole Scheme of things works great. However, When 
XML data is created by user or edited by user, It 
becomes a tedious process to pad extra spaces and 
most of time is spent in correcting this trivial activity or 
correcting errors.

It would be great if a extra flag is provided in assertion 
method or some appropriate place to indicate to DBUnit 
to ignore the space pads for comparision\(assertion\)

The feature might look stupid, but saves us from lot of 
frustration.

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=492677
Originator: NO

This issue is caused by stream-based inputSource used with a CompositeOperation : the first operation reads the stream and closes it. The second one gets a closed stream.

You have to use a CachedDataSet instance, like FlatXmlDataSet, not a StreamingDataSet
posts.subject: #70 CLEAN_INSERT Throws an Exception

posts.text: Closing this issue since it was a usage problem. It could be discussed whether or not the CompositeOperation should check whether a StreamingDataSet is passed and if so it could wrap this one by a CachedDataSet. This way we could avoid the exception.

This is not a simple change now though I agree that this is
a good idea. Its not a simple change because ITableIterator
already has a method

public boolean next\(\) throws DataSetException;

which is the equivalent of java.util.Iterator.hasNext\(\). So,
making ITableIterator extend java.util.Iterator will break
backwards compatibility.

posts.subject: #73 Infinite wait in StreamingIterator
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
StreamingIterator stars an AsynchronousConsumer 
thread running and then waits for the data to arrive.
If the AsynchronousConsumer has an exception then the 
StreamingIterator is not awoken, and thus will wait 
indefinitely.

tickets.summary: NullPointer when FLatXmlDataSet w/DTD has but one table
tickets.description:
A NullPointerException occurs in the endDTD\(\) method 
on line 224, when the FlatXmlDataSet contains only one 
table.

My DTD was generated by dbunit.  Here it is:

<\!ELEMENT dataset \(
SYSTEM\_SETTING\*\)>

<\!ELEMENT SYSTEM\_SETTING EMPTY>
<\!ATTLIST SYSTEM\_SETTING
PK\_SETTING\_ID CDATA \#REQUIRED
CATEGORY CDATA \#IMPLIED
DESCR CDATA \#IMPLIED
DISP\_VALUE CDATA \#IMPLIED
DATA\_VALUE CDATA \#IMPLIED
RANGE\_CMNT CDATA \#IMPLIED
UNITS\_CMNT CDATA \#IMPLIED
AUD\_USER CDATA \#REQUIRED
AUD\_REC\_ID CDATA \#REQUIRED
AUD\_DT CDATA \#REQUIRED
>


On entry to the endDTD\(\) method, the \_rootModel 
is "\(SYSTEM\_SETTING\)\*" which is weird because that 
exact string does not even exist in the DTD.  I'm 
guessing the XML parser \(or something in DBUnit\) has 
munged the string into something different?  Since the 
parens do not enclose the entire string, the substring\(\) 
call on line 207 goes haywire, resulting 
in "SYSTEM\_SETTING\)" being used as the key to lookup 
the table name.  The lookup fails and the null pointer 
ensures.

tickets.summary: Patch to 2.1 for zip, Oracle XMLType and trigger Support
tickets.description:
Hallo Dear dbunit developers,

I made the following changes to dbunit

\- Oracle XMLType support by extending CLOBs
\- Zip support for XML-Fileformat
\- Oracle foreign key and trigger enable / disable 
operations.

and would be happy if this changes would be included in 
the next dbunit Release.

Also I want to thank for Your time and good work with 
dbunit.

The mail.zip-File contains the following files:

data.exe	    Utility to export/import Oracle 9i database 
schema, JDK 1.3 needed\!
dbunit21p1.doc
Dokumentation of the Sourcechanges to 
support ZIP-Files and other
dbunit21p1.pdf
Same Dokumenation as dbunit21p1.doc
but as pdf if You don't like MS Word.
dbunit21src\_patch.zip
The patched files and new files
Data.java    Example to use new ZIP-Files support.

metadata if there are rows to add.  Out of the box, the API
doesn't really support this, but for my purposes I think it
will work.

for a EMP table \(former Oracle sample table\) becomes a
DataSet with 14 tables of 1 row, instead of 1 table of

lines 205 and 213, better test equality of string
ignoring character case.

tickets.summary: Filtering and ordering through XML feeds (sequential columns
tickets.description:
In a project Im working on we have tweaked the DBUnit
XML expected feeds slightly to address two issues with
the database comparison:

1\. Some columns have incremental \(id\) values and should
be excluded from the expected comparison.
2\. For DB tables that do not define primary keys, the
XML expected data set has to define virtual keys for
sorting.

Our current solution uses a modified XML dataset DTD:

<code>
<\!ELEMENT dataset \(table+\)>
<\!ELEMENT table \(column\*, row\*\)>
<\!ATTLIST table
	name CDATA \#REQUIRED
>
<\!ELEMENT column \(\#PCDATA\)>
<\!ATTLIST column
	filter \(true | false\) \#IMPLIED
	key \(true | false\) \#IMPLIED
>
<\!ELEMENT row \(value | null | none\)\*>
<\!ELEMENT value \(\#PCDATA\)>
<\!ELEMENT null EMPTY>
</code>

Here the column element has optional attributes filter
and key to address two issues above. The expected data
feeds are then fed through a custom made reader class
which stores the columns. Finally these columns are
used by the DBUnit mechanisms and
DefaultColumnFilter.excludedColumnsTable and
DatabaseConnection.getConfig\(\).setProperty\(DatabaseConfig.PROPERTY\_PRIMARY\_KEY\_FILTER,
\_ \).

Obviously, this has taken quite a bit of custom tweaks
of the DBUnit framework, and it would be neat if some
of could be taken care in the package. Our solution
might not be the best, however the issues are clearly
not uncommon. 

For a different approach to more or less the same, see
Steven Caswells post <href
a=http://sourceforge.net/tracker/index.php?func=detail&aid=979764&group\_id=47439&atid=449494>979764</a>.
It should be noted, that although with this solution
the sequential column does not have to be ignored, it
does not address how to specify at what number the
sequence starts. In some cases, as we have found, it is
almost impossible to know before the output data is
generated.

posts.subject: #34 Change private fields to protected
posts.author: rlogiacco

tickets.description:
I have just extended the Excel and XML data set
functionality so that bold and italic fonts in XLS
input data can control the attributes mentioned in the
1077598
\(http://sourceforge.net/tracker/index.php?func=detail&aid=1077598&group\_id=47439&atid=449494\)
request in the XML output data. \(Should anybody find
this interesting, feel free to ask for guidance\).

The implementation was straight forward, including an
extension of the org.dbunit.dataset.Column class, and
modifications to the XlsDataSet, XlsTable and
XmlDataSetWriter.

The problem is that all these classes \(and any of the
other DBUnit classes Ive looked at\) have several
private fields without any access methods. This
includes constants and writer and meta data objects in
the XmlDataSetWriter, and similar fields in other
DBUnit classes.

My request is that these fields are either set to have
the protected access scope, or that get-methods are
provided for extending the framework. Considering the
nature of the DBUnit project, it seems only natural to
allow for easy and flexible extension, rather than
restricting users.

tickets.summary: Support CSV files from a URL (e.g. jar file) + CSV fixes
tickets.description:
I've added support for CSV files coming from a URL.

I've also fixed the existing implementation of
CsvDataSet/CsvProducer/CsvDataSetWriter where:

1\)CsvProducer didn't honour the table ordering file.
2\)CsvProducer didn't honour the 'null' string.

These caused ant tasks that needed the ordering file or
null support to fail.

3\) CsvDataSet was an unordered dataset by default.
4\) CsvDataSet was responsible for writing the
table-ordering.txt file, something CsvDataSetWriter
should have done.

There are tests for the new/changed classes.

In the zip file are three files:
csv-url-support.txt - a unified diff patch file.
mm.mysql-2.0.11-bin.jar - a file missing from /lib
orders.jar - a test file which should be placed in
src/csv/orders.

posts.subject: #84 Autocommit required
posts.author: scooper

tickets.description:
It would appear as though DBUnit requires autocommit to
be set to true.  This is not the default behavior for
the new DB2 JDBC type 4 driver.
This causes several problems when using the
maven-dbunit-plugin and DB2.  Noteably, the data is
loaded, but never commited so all the inserts are
rolled back.

DBUnit should either set autocommit to true before
starting any of its work or it should perform a commit
at regular intervals.

tickets.summary: BigDecimal values read by XlsDataSet have wrong precision
tickets.description:
XlsTable creates BigDecimals using BigDecimal\(double\).
This will create a BigDecimal with an unexpected scale
\(see
http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.4.2/docs/api/java/math/BigDecimal.html\#BigDecimal\(double\)\)

It should use the BigDecimal\(String\) constructor instead.

The fix \(against version 2.1\) is to change
XlsTable.java line 111 from 

return new BigDecimal\(ell.getNumericCellValue\(\)\);

to

return new
BigDecimal\(String.valueOf\(cell.getNumericCellValue\(\)\)\);

posts.subject: #87 Bad comparison in SortedTable
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
Hi.

In org.dbunit.dataset.SortedTable class, the comparison 
is done by comparing the string value of the values, not 
the values themselves.

I have changed it \(on my machine\) to use 
Comparable.compareTo\(\) interface, and it now works 
fine. It now can sort numeric data as numeric, not as 
strings.

Please make the fix: Use casting to Comparable instead 
of DataType.asString\(\) \(line 165\).


In addition, in line 146, check that \_columns\[i\] is not 
null. It could be null if user sends columns list that 
contains column name that doest not exists in the meta 
data.

Thanks.

posts.text:
Hi there,

I refactored the method. The original one invokes now the new 

protected IDatabaseConnection createDatabaseConnection\(Connection jdbcConnection, String dbSchema\)

which is responsible for the creation and initialization of the dbunit "DatabaseConnection".

I also changed the visibility of "\#createConnection\(\)" to protected which should be sufficient.

posts.subject: #88 IDENTITY_INSERT  : Check for identity column incorrect
posts.author: jamapor

posts.text:
Which RDBMS do you use?
Did you know that you can set a custom filter to find your identity columns? It works as follows:

dbunitConnection.getConfig\(\).setProperty\(InsertIdentityOperation.PROPERTY\_IDENTITY\_COLUMN\_FILTER, InsertIdentityOperation.IDENTITY\_FILTER\_EXTENDED\);


I found a good compendium for the topic auto-generated keys at http://troels.arvin.dk/db/rdbms/ \(section "Automatic key generation"\).

I added a new Filter that checks the "remarks" and "autoIncrement" fields provided by the JDBC driver \(it is the filter mentioned in the example above - InsertIdentityOperation.IDENTITY\_FILTER\_EXTENDED\). I suppose that in addition to check the "isAutoIncrement" property it probably should also be checked whether the column is a primary-key column?

I committed the changes to SVN rev. 933. Please let me know if this works for you \(retrieve the latest build from the parabuild server at http://parabuild.viewtier.com:8080/parabuild/index.htm?view=detailed&buildid=30\). I set this item to pending waiting for a response.

Regards,
mat

posts.author: sf-robot

tickets.summary: IDENTITY_INSERT  : Check for identity column incorrect
tickets.description:
I have found a bug in the dbUnit code, or an incorrect
way of coding if you may. To check whether a column is
an identity column or not, the following method checks
the SQL Type Name to check whether it ends with
"identity". However, a column does not end with
"identity" if it is an identity column. I believe that
this was assumed when this method was coded, but was
not actually looked into. I am going to make a revision
to correctly check if a column is an identity column,
rebuild dbUnit, and post my findings. 


From InsertIdentityOperation.java:

public boolean accept\(String tableName, Column column\)
\{
return column.getSqlTypeName\(\).endsWith\("identity"\);
\}



\---------------
Jacob Portnoy
Checkerboard, Ltd.
www.eInvite.com
JPortnoy@Checkernet.com

posts.subject: #91 problem because tableName is case insensitive
posts.author: sycorob24

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=129356

Hi all,

I think it's dangerous to change such behaviour, as it might break backwards 
compatibility. Besides, there must be a reason the class use it - maybe it's an 
issue with some databases.

On the other hand, we could add something like a isCaseSensitive\(\) method in 
the IDataSet interface \(and the respective setCaseSensitive\(\) method in the 
implementations\). 

And if we add such methods, we have 2 decisions to make:

\- keep the current behaviour \(to keep compabitibility\) or the new one
\- whether this flag should be explicitely set by the end user or implicitly by 
the database factories.

If we change the behaviour and the current tests pass, I'm favorable to set it 
as default and leave the change to the end user - eventually, we will identify 
databases where that is a problem \(in the form of bug requests and/or 
complaints in the lists :-\) and then we could delegate the decision to the 
factory.

Anyway, if someone can come with the proper patch \(including the changes, 
documentation and test cases\), I would be glad to review and apply it.

\-- Felipe
posts.subject: #91 problem because tableName is case insensitive
posts.author: felipeal

posts.text:
Hi there,

I introduced some changes related to this on the current trunk rev. 841 and tried to maintain backwards compatibility \(i.e. default is case-insensitive\). I also added a constructor to the DatabaseDataSet taking a boolean "caseSensitiveTableNames" which should solve this issue.

I deliberately did not add the "isCaseSensitive\(\)" method to the IDataSet interface since I believe it should not be there. I prefer the pre-1.5 way where an IDataSet can be decorated with a CaseInsensitiveDataSet. In my eyes this is more elegant.

Another important item to me is <b>not</b> to add a method "setCaseSensitive\(\)" since in my eyes an IDataSet instance should be immutable after its creation \(it also was before I think\). Instead I added a constructor to the AbstractDataSet taking a boolean arg "caseSensitiveTableNames".

Moreover I heavily thought about how to easily set the case sensitivity via a factory but nothing sensible came to my mind. The DatabaseConfiguration object is only available to DatabaseDataSet and QueryDataSet. All other datasets would not be able to access it. So this was not an option.

The advantage of the current implementation is that the only location where the case sensitivity is handled is in class OrderedTableNameMap. If you have a more detailed look at this you will find several locations where it is used - unfortunately at two of those locations I had to invoke the default constructor \(which means that caseSensitiveTableNames is set to false\). These are SequenceTableFilter and FlatXmlProducer. If anyone has an idea on how to handle/fix this I would be very grateful.

So please drop your comments on this quite big change so that I know how to proceed with this.

Thanks in advance and regards,
mat
posts.subject: #91 problem because tableName is case insensitive
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
I've just upgraded from 2.3.0 to 2.4.1.  This fixed a problem having to do with running DbUnit inside Ant inside CruiseControl \(thanks\!\), but created a new one having to do with the case of table names.  The code that tries to do a clean insert now receives a NoSuchTableException from DataBaseDataSet.getTableMetaData\(String tablename\).

The XML dataset that I'm trying to insert was originally extracted from a MySql DB on Linux, where MySql is case sensitive.  It contains table names with mixed case \(poor form I know but it isn't my DB\).  The exception is thrown when I try to do the clean insert on a MySql DB on Windows, where MySql is not case sensative.  This worked on 2.3.0 --- that is, a dataset extracted from a case-sensitive DB could be inserted into a case-insensitive one.

One way of fixing this might be to have DataBaseDataSet.getTableMetaData\(String tablename\) not use \_caseSensitiveTableNames to determine whether to convert the case of the tablenames but to use the "case" properties of the metadata object of the DB being inserted into to  --- e.g., if DatabaseMetaDate.supportsMixedCaseIdentifiers\(\) is true just use the table name as stored in the extracted dataset.

/Paul
posts.subject: #91 problem because tableName is case insensitive
posts.author: runyan

posts.text:
Hi Paul,

thanks a lot for your suggestion\! I like the idea of calling "DatabaseMetaData.supportsMixedCaseIdentifiers\(\)" for identifying the behaviour of the DB. On the other hand I want to avoid putting a quick hack into the DatabaseDataSet to fix this problem. I can imagine change the "CASE\_SENSITIVE\_TABLE\_NAMES" feature to a property which might allow the following values: "true", "false", "native" \(which would be the "supportsMixedCaseIdentifiers"\). Do you have any ideas on how to consistently implement this?

Regards,
mat

posts.subject: #91 problem because tableName is case insensitive
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Hi Mat,

Thanks for all your work on DbUnit.  

I've only had a small look at how DbUnit works internally but it seems to me a good way to go would be to remove the concept of case-sensitivity from DataSets entirely.  When a DataSet is extracted from a DB it would get whatever case the DB presents itself as all upper case, all lower case, or mixed.  When one goes to insert a DataSet into a DB some logic would use the properties of the DatabaseMetaData to figure out how best find the corresponding tables in the DB.  E.g., if DatabaseMetaData.storesLowerCaseIdentifiers\(\) is true it would convert the keys from the tableNameMap to lower case before comparing them with the metadata.  If DatabaseMetaData.storesUpperCaseIdentifiers\(\) it would first convert the keys to upper case before comparing them with the metadata.

I'm not sure whether there are cases where one needs a DataSet not to be case-impartial.  If there are, using a decorator to wrap the DataSet would seem to be a good solution.
posts.subject: #91 problem because tableName is case insensitive
posts.author: runyan

posts.text:
You're welcome - I actually enjoy working on dbunit :-\)

I agree that removing the case sensitivity from the DataSet would be a nice thing. There is one case I am not sure about how it will work in the future. Imagine you have a FlatXmlDataSet having one table name with different cases \(for examle "CUSTOMER" and "Customer"\). If the database \(e.g. oracle\) has both of the tables as well how should dbunit decide which one should be populated during a DatabaseOperation.INSERT? During the creation of the FlatXmlDataSet dbunit must somehow - without having database access at this time - figure out whether the both "CUSTOMER" and "Customer" represent the same ITable \(adding all rows to the same dbunit object\) or whether they are two different tables. I do not yet see a neat solution for this.

BTW, I appreciate your input very much\! Thanks a lot for your participation.

Best regards,
mat
posts.subject: #91 problem because tableName is case insensitive
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Hi Mat -

It's very possible that I'm missing something becuase I'm not nearly as familiar with DbUnit as you are, but the the INSERT case doesn't seem to present a problem to me.  If the FlatXmlDataSet where to be case-impartial then it would create a separate table entry for each distinct table name for which data is added to it.  So if somehow data were added to it for "sometable", "SomeTable" and "SoMeTaBle" it would store these as three separate table entries.  When the FlatXmlDataSet is INSERTed into a DB it would use the case properties of the DB it's writing to to determine how to map its table names to DB table names.  If the DB uses case sensitive table names then it would try to map them as three different tables, if the DB stores all tables names as uppercase it would try to map them all as "SOMETABLE", . . .

In the case where the DB is case sensitive it works correctly.  In the case where the DB is not case sensitive it results in three sets of INSERTS to the same table instead of one, but the final result will still be correct too.

The CLEAN\_INSERT case would require a bit of extra logic however.  Doing three separate CLEAN\_INSERTS will produce a different result to doing a single CLEAN\_INSERT with all the same data.  

One way to make this work would be to have the DeleteAllOperation used by CLEAN\_INSERT store the names \(in their DB format\) of all the tables it has deleted and throw an exception if it is ever called to delete the same table more than once.  This would not only catch this case but would also catch cases where someone accidentally adds more than one set of data the same table to a FlatXmlDataSet file.

/Paul

or the unsupport DATETIME

Hi marney,

If you have problems with oracle dates I firstly recommend to use the OracleDataTypeFactory which maps the SQL type "DATE" to a java.sql.Timestamp which should work for you.

You can do this as follows:
...
IDatabaseConnection dbunitConnection = new DatabaseConnection\(jdbcConnection, "myschema"\);
dbunitConnection.getConfig\(\).setProperty\(DatabaseConfig.PROPERTY\_DATATYPE\_FACTORY, new OracleDataTypeFactory\(\)\);
...

Nevertheless I think the data type handling \(mapping of SQL-types to java types\) is still an ugly thing in dbunit which can/should be made more transparent for users \(even if everybody is able to provide an own IDataTypeFactory implementation\). To be discussed for some future release.

Regards,
mat

tickets.summary: Time in DATE column is ignored
tickets.description:
In my prepare-xml I have the following line:

<value>2005-02-28 01:30:00.000000000</value>

According to the dbunit FAQ this should be the format 
for date and time.

After loading the XML into the database \(column has 
type DATE\) via

IDataSet dataSet = createXmlDataSet\(p\_fileName\);
DatabaseOperation.CLEAN\_INSERT.execute
\(getConnection\(\), dataSet\);

the time part is ignored and set to 00:00:00.

Is this a bug or missing feature or am I doing something 
wrong here?

\(When comparing with expected-xml and using the very 
same value-line as stated above, the time part is taken 
into the query table.\)

I've extended FlatXmlDataSet and FlatXmlProducer to 
allow SELECT statements and variable substitution in 
the xml. However, there is additional functionality that I 
cannot add to these extensions because certain 
aspects of the CachedDataSet, FlatXmlDataSet and 
FlatXmlProducer are private.

I'd like to request that these private methods and 
attributes be made protected to allow for extension.

Thank You for your time considering this enhancement.

tickets.summary: Column count for empty XlsDataSet returns 0
tickets.description:
For an XlsDataSet that is empty \(minus the first row
which has the column names\), XlsTable will not create
metadata for the sheet because the condition is
rowCount > 0. Changing this to rowCount >= 0 will
correctly createMetaData for the sheet.

From XML initialise database sequences with know values.

Use 1:

Initialize sequence to be above data loaded by dbunit to avoid key 
conflicts during load or subsequence testing.

Use case 2:

Initialise sequence to know state for further testing.

posts.subject: #40 Allow SQL functions and executes them via xml import in db.
posts.author: kunugiyama

tickets.summary: Allow SQL functions and executes them via xml import in db.
tickets.description:
Not sure if this has been raised or addressed already
\(I looked through the RFE / Bugs section and didn't see
it\).
It would be nice to be able to auto-generate particular
data when loading the XML data into the database. For
example, when performing date sensitive tests, the test
may succeed today or this month only, since the XML
data is hard-coded. Running the test may fail tomorrow
or the next month depending on the type of the test.
For what its worth, the solution I came up with is to
allow SQL functions such as MONTH\(NOW\(\)\), YEAR\(NOW\(\)\),
etc. to be executed during the data upload into the
database.
I have attached a diff file on how I addressed this
functionality.
Quick instructions on the use of this functionality is
simple. Simply pad your SQL function with the delimeter
\(I have chosen "\#\#\#"\) and dbunit SQL will take care of
the rest. For example, \#\#\#MONTH\(NOW\(\)\)\#\#\# = the int
value of the current month.

tickets.summary: Patch to Ant Task to Wrap Operation in a Transaction
tickets.description:
This patch modfies the Operation tag in the Ant task to
allow a new parameter: transaction="true"

This will cause the Operation to be decorated into a
TransactionOperation and thus cause the operation to be
performed as a single transaction.  This allows the
operation to be atomic and to complete much faster on
many databases.

tickets.description:
On Saturday, Oct 2, 2004, at 11:12 America/New\_York, Lenny 
Marks wrote:

Hi Federico,

I have a few more things related to the queryset stuff from last week 
which should be attached as a patch below. First, I added 
documentation and examples to the anttask.xml xdoc for usage of 
queryset. Also, I did a bit of re-factoring on the QuerySet stuff.

I was able remove manual propagation of the Project by making 
AbstractStep extend from org.apache.tools.ant.ProjectComponent. 
This way Ant automatically takes care of setting up the context 
using reflection.

I added an antlib definition for the dbunit ant tasks. \(http://
ant.apache.org/manual/CoreTypes/antlib.html \) This can be used 
with ant version > 1.6. It just simplifies things a bit.

<typedef classpath="$\{dbunit.jar\}"  resource="org/dbunit/ant/
antlib.xml">

instead of:

<taskdef name="dbunit" classpath="$\{dbunit.jar\}"
classname="org.dbunit.ant.DbUnitTask" loaderref="dbunit"/>
<typedef name="dbunit" classpath="$\{dbunit.jar\}"
classname="org.dbunit.ant.QuerySet" loaderref="dbunit"/>

Since queryset can be defined as a reference outside of a dbunit 
task, it must have its own typedef, and the loaderref attribute must 
be specified to ensure that the classes are loaded into same JVM. If 
you forget to specify the loaderref, you get ugly 
ClassCastExceptions.

The only thing was that the row for queryset in the generated 
anttask.html did not have the alternating color when I viewed it. I'm 
assuming that has something to do with style sheets or XSLT, but 
I'm not familiar with that stuff.

Thanks,
Lenny Marks
American Physical Society

posts.subject: #98 unable to delete rows from tables with fk's to each other
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Hm, I think you will get a CyclicTablesDependencyException from dbunit if you have two tables referencing each other via FKs if you use the "DatabaseSequenceFilter\#sortTableNames\(\)" \(at least in 2.4 releases\).

Did you try to surround your DELETE operation with a transaction \(DatabaseOperation.TRANSACTION\)? I assume that your problem is that the "autocommit=true" is set by "DbUnitTask\#createConnection\(\)". You may solve your problem by setting the attribute "transaction to true:

<operation type="DELETE" transaction="true">


I will set this item to pending waiting for a response.

regards,
mat

in DatabaseSequenceFilter.  I am almost positive that
this is because the reprocess flag never gets set to
false in this situation.

tickets.summary: Accept that "" and NULL are the same on Oracle
tickets.description:
We have input/output datasets \(as XML\) which we're
using to validate stored procs against both SQL Server
and Oracle.

When the result on SQL Server is an empty string, it's
null on Oracle. Unfortunately, I can't see any way of
representing both of these states of affairs in an XML
file.

It would be really helpful if there were some way of
either making the Oracle connection automatically
replace nulls with empty strings \(optionally, of
course\) or provide a flag to the dataset comparison to
make it allow nulls and empty values to be compared as
equal.

tickets.summary: When a table has no primary key, use all columns for sorting
tickets.description:
We have some "bag" tables - there is no primary key for
these tables at all.

In order to correctly compare datasets, we need a
reliable sort order. Just using all columns as sort
columns works fine, and is a simple change to
DatabaseDataSet.java. Just insert:

// If there isn't a primary key, sort by all
columns
if \(primaryKeys.length==0\)
\{
primaryKeys=columns;
\}

into getSelectStatement\(\).

That's what we've got in our local copy of DbUnit now,
but it would be nice to have in the main line too :\) Of
course, I could see that this should probably be
configurable...

tickets.summary: Configuration for size of statement batches
tickets.description:
As dbunit is so handy for import/export, we're using it
for large tests as well as system test.

By using streaming datasets, we've had no problem
exporting the data. However, importing it using batched
statements, the system blows up with an
OutOfMemoryException.

Turning off batched statements would, I believe \(but
haven't tested, admittedly\) slow things down hugely. A
good intermediate state, however, would be to use
batched statements but only batch a certain number.

For the moment I've got a temporary hack to the source
to always execute the statement after 100 operations,
but putting this in place properly with a configuration
option would be nicer.

\(I can attempt a patch if that would help.\)

posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1803108
Originator: NO

Hi there,

thanks for your submission and sorry for the long time dbunit has been dormant.

I investigated this and found that the root cause for your problem is not the bad parsing. Instead your DTD really is invalid because the ELEMENT declarations of TABLE\_1/TABLE\_2/TABLE\_3 are missing \(in Oxygen I get only a warning which is "The content model of element "dataset" refers to the undeclared element "TABLE\_1"\).

A correct DTD would be this here:
<\!ELEMENT dataset \( \(TABLE\_1|TABLE\_2\)\* \)><\!ELEMENT TABLE\_1 EMPTY><\!ELEMENT TABLE\_2 EMPTY>

I committed a change which throws a more meaningful exception now \(SVN rev 793/trunk - upcoming 2.3 release\):
"ELEMENT/ATTRIBUTE declaration for 'TABLE\_1' is missing."

So the root cause is problably in the FlatDtdWriter. Could you please provide a testcase that reproduces the creation of your broken DTD?

Thanks and regards,
mat

posts.text:
I have been able to reproduce the problem using The MySqlConnector/J versions 5.1.6 and 3.0.17. The driver reports the SQL type "Types.BIT" for a "TINYINT\(1\) UNSIGNED" column. This is why the DatabaseTableMetaData has the wrong DataType set for the column which is the dbunit DataType.BIT.

I added the exceptional type "TINYINT UNSIGNED" as type name to the MySqlDataTypeFactory \(SVN rev. 829/trunk\). Should work in the next release \(probably 2.4\)

Thanks and regards,
mat

posts.author: gommma

tickets.summary: ITable uses getFloat() when it should use getDouble()
tickets.description:
ITable.getValue\("column"\) uses getFloat\(\) when
retrieving a value from a float, but it should use the
recommended getDouble\(\)

Database: mysql 4.1.14
JDBC: mysql-connector-3.1.11

Recommendation:
http://java.sun.com/docs/books/tutorial/jdbc/basics/retrieving.html

This is not just a recommendation, as I have a Unit
Test example that shows that a Float object is being
returned by getValue, but the value returned is
different from the database. 

The database has a structure float\(10,6\), and a value
in the database of 139.714172. phpMyAdmin shows me the
proper value. Also, using getDouble\(\) via a jdbc
ResultSet gets me the proper value. However, using
getFloat\(\) via ResultSet, and ITable.getValue gets me
139.71417.

When the value is 35.648003, the getFloat\(\) and
ITable.getValue\(\) works fine as well, but just because
I got lucky. 

It would be nice if getFloat\(\) would have worked and
got me the full value, but it did not, so I believe
sticking to Sun's recommendation would be best,
however, this might break some other unit tests that
expect a Float object to be returned. 


Not Okay for 139.714172 \(float\(10.6\) in database:
dataSet.addTable\("TEST", sql\);
ITable testTable = dataSet.getTable\("TEST"\);
Float usrkObject = \(Float\) testTable.getValue\(0, "USRK"\);

Okay:
usrk = set.getDouble\("USRK"\);

posts.subject: #107 project.xml problem re jdbc-2.0.jar
posts.author: rlogiacco

tickets.description:
I tried to build from source using Maven, and I got an
error because it couldn't find 'jdbc-2.0.jar'. Thanks
to the handy <url> element \(wish more people did that\!\)
I was easily able to determine it was looking for
jdbc2\_0-stdext.jar from Sun.

Unless I'm wrong, Sun doesn't actually release anything
named jdbc-2.0.jar, so I'm wondering if it might be
better to change project.xml to use the jar name, like so:

<dependency>
<id>jdbc</id>
<version>2.0</version>
<jar>jdbc2\_0-stdext.jar</jar>
\(url omitted for formatting\)
</dependency>

Also, since dbunit is offered via ibiblio.org, I wonder
if you could make jdbc2\_0-stdext.jar avialable as a
separate artifact in the dbunit group.

\(I spent most of the day streamlining our build process
at my day job, so I must have this sort of thing on the
brain\)

Keep up the good work\!

\- mcgee

posts.subject: #108 Time objects incorrect when using IBM Net Drivers
posts.author: sf-robot

tickets.summary: Time objects incorrect when using IBM Net Drivers
tickets.description:
Read the comment below for description of the problem.
This is the code we have placed in our local version
of DBUnit 2.1 to fix the bug in the IBM drivers.  We
have modified: TimeDataType::getSqlValue\(int, ResultSet
\), adding the following code just before the last
return from the method.

	/\*
	 \* David Weiler-Thiessen
	 \* Nestle Purina Petcare
	 \* December 15, 2005
	 \* 
	 \* There is a bug in way the IBM JDBC Net
Drivers for UDB v7.2 work.  They return Time
	 \* objects for a time on Dec 31, 1969, instead
of Jan 1, 1970 as would be expected.  This means
	 \* that operations on Time, such as
equals\(Object\) and compare\(Object\) will fail, if the two 
	 \* objects were created in two different ways,
i.e. one in memory and another from the JDBC Driver.
	 \* 
	 \* We have found that this bug does not exist
with the JDBC App Drivers, however, there is another
	 \* serious problem with the App Drivers relating
to Hibernate usage.  As a result we are caught
	 \* in a catch-22.  
	 \* 
	 \* To rectify the situation, we do a simple
check on the Time object created.  If the
timeInMilliseconds
	 \* is negative, we assume it was created by the
JDBC Net Driver, and we adjust the value by one
	 \* day to move it into Jan 1, 1970.
	 \*/
	if \( value.getTime\(\) < 0 \) \{
		value.setTime\( value.getTime\(\) + 86400000 \);  
		
	\}

CSV isn't a well defined or standard format, so there's nothing to define it but conventions.  I haven't seen a convention for using comments in CSV files, but that wouldn't prevent you from creating your own.  Since the major advantage of dbunit's CSV support is to provide basic editing in Excel, I would look at how well Excel would support a comment character in one row of data.  

Since I submitted this, I've abandoned CSV files in DBUnit because of the lack of null-value support.  I don't use the XML formats either, but that's another story.
