tickets.summary: Problem in data import if all the columns are not populated
tickets.description:
Currently I have a sql table with the first two 
columns populated with data. The rest of the columns 
may or may not be populated. In one such case the xml 
file exported is as follows:

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>
<dataset>
<APP\_CHANGE SLICE="1000" ITEM\_ID="0"/>
<APP\_CHANGE SLICE="1000" ITEM\_ID="20" 
DESCRIPTION\_LONG="long desc"/>
<APP\_CHANGE SLICE="1000" ITEM\_ID="1575"/>
</dataset>

Please note that the second xml record has one extra 
attribute "Description\_Long". The reason is that the 
sql table has that column populated for the 2nd row 
only.

Now the problem is in import of the above xml. It is 
only importing the first 2 columns i.e. "SLICE" 
and "ITEM\_ID". It is not importing the value 
of "Description\_Long".

I tried debugging the code and found that it inserts 
values for the number of column attributes in the 
first xml row i.e if I move the second row with 3 
column attributes to the first position then in that 
case it will import the data for 3 columns.

I think that the number of columns to be populated 
should not be dependent on the first row of xml 
because they would be many cases where each row would 
have different columns populated.

I would appreciate any kind of help or suggestions.

Thanks in advance.

posts.subject: #115 Problem with Oracle 10g Recyclebin
posts.author: sillygit

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1671660
Originator: NO

Had the same issue and found the following reason:

Background:
Starting with Oracle 10g the database has a recycle bin storing dropped tables.
The dropped tables have special \(generated\) names starting with 'bin$' and are located in
the same schema as the dropped table.
Important: the recycle bin table names are case sensitive

Reason: DbUnit reads all table names and stores the upper case names in a map 
DbUnit assumes that the table names within one schema are unique using an case insensitive compare.
This is no longer the case taking the bin$ table names into account. 

What should be fixed?
DbUnit should ignore the bin$ tables all together because they are internal tables
until there is a JDBC Oralce driver which doesn't report these tables to the application.

Workaround:
Drop the bin$ table with the same case insensitive name with the pruge command.

Patch:
I'll check if I can provide a patch.

tickets.description:
Well it looks like someone beat me to it, but I didn't
check these lists until AFTER I'd done it myself. I've
added XMLType support to dbunit for oracle. My way is a
different approach though, so I thought I'd post my
files anyway.

There's only two:
org\dbunit\ext\oracle\OracleXMLTypeDataType.java \(new file\)
org\dbunit\ext\oracle\OracleDataTypeFactory.java
\(replacement file\)

If you download these files, put them in an appropriate
place \(underneath the java dir\) and rebuild then you
should have support for XMLTypes. See the FAQ for
information on how to use oracle data types.

The XMLTypes are stored as binary data in the xml flat
files produced.

Hi aalmiray,

Your class seems very interesting and it would be a great addition for dbunit 
2.2.

However, I'd like to ask a few improvements \(not in the class itself, but in the 
'infrastructure\):

\- could you refactor the test case to not use Spring? I know Spring is great 
and it facilitate the development, but in this case it would get in the way, as it 
is not used in the current tests and hence can cause confusion on future 
maintenance

\- the current DatabaseTestCase could be refactor to delegate it's job to the 
new class - this way, we would have a better reuse. More than that, you could 
create an IDatabaseTester interface \(implemented by DatabaseTester\), which 
in turn would be a parameter for the DatabaseTest constructors \(of course, 
the current constructors would use DatabaseTester to keep compatibility\).

\- it would also be important to update the docs as well, otherwise your 
efforts could be wasted \(in other words, it's necessary to spread the word 
about this enhancement ;-\). In fact, you could use the Spring integration in 
the documentation as well.

\- finally, could you provide a cvs-style patch \(for instance, created from 'cvs 
diff -u' or with Eclipse\) of these changes?

Regards,

\-- Felipe

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1192026

I've been trying to access de cvs server today but it keeps
failing with a timeout. I'll try tomorrow from another computer.

I have stumbled upon a design issue; if DatabaseTestcase
uses a DatabaseTester behind the scenes the following
changes must happen:

1 mark getConnection as deprecated
2 add protected abstract DataSource getDataSource\(\)
3 add protected IDatabaseTester getDatabaseTester\(\) \{ return
new DatabaseTester\(\); \}
4 in setUp\(\) wire IDatabaseTester and Datasource before any
operations

In that way DatabaseTestcase subclasses can use the default
DatabaseTester or provide ther own, but adds a new method
that will break backward compatibility.

Another aproach would be to create a parallel
DatabaseTestcase with this code, and deal with the backward
imcompatibility in a later release.

\- Andres
posts.subject: #54 Enable TestCase compositions
posts.author: aalmiray

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=129356

Hi again,

I think you're trying the old server \(cvs.sourceforge.net\) and not the current 
one \(dbunit.cvs.sourceforge.net\) - SF has changed the CVS setup a couple of 
weeks ago and dbunit's site does not have the current information \(sorry\).

Regarding the design issues, we cannot count with a DataSource - what if the 
users are using 'traditional' JDBC connections, obtained through 
DriverManager? So, I would take the route where the DatabaseTester is an 
abstract class with an abstract getConnection\(\) method; you could then 
extend it by a DSDatabaseTester, which would get the connection through 
DataSource \(you could even go one step further and also provide a 
NamedDSDatabaseTester, which would take the DataSource from JNDI\).

So, answering your questions:

> 1 mark getConnection as deprecated

Nope, it would simply call dbTester.getConnection\(\)

> 2 add protected abstract DataSource getDataSource\(\)

DataSource would be a specific characteristic of the DatabaseTester sub-
class; DatabaseTestCase has nothing to do with it \(although it could have a 
sub-class to do so\).

> 3 add protected IDatabaseTester getDatabaseTester\(\) \{ return
>new DatabaseTester\(\); \}

Yep.

>4 in setUp\(\) wire IDatabaseTester and Datasource before any
>operations
I don't think it's necessary - you should just use getDatabaseTester\(\) 
everywhere it's needed. What setUp could do is to call a protected method 
that checks if getDatabaseTeste\(\) does not return null.

I've followed your suggestions and I coded the following:
IDatabaseTester
\\-> AbstractDatabaseTester
|-> DMDatabaseTester \(DriverManager\)

|-> DSDatabaseTester \(Datasource\)

tickets.description:
Althogh DbUnit is a tool to test database access and
components closely related to the database \(i.e, DAO
layer\) it is also good at establishing a very specific
database state for integration testing. The drawback is
that each integration TestCase must extend
DatabaseTestCase in order to harness that
functionality. What then, if the integration TestCase
has already another class hierarchy ?

The solution would be to create a DatabaseTester \(like
WebTester on jWebUnit\).

I have already coded such solution and used it in
conjuction with the depedencyInjection testCases
bundled with Spring \(tested against 1.2.6 and 2.0M1\)
and would like to contribute it.

posts.subject: #116 Version 2.2-dev, Java 1.4 compatibility issue
posts.author: felipeal

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1725448
Originator: NO

I've had a similar problem \(see stack trace below\) and have managed to get a working version from the checked out source.  I'm not entirely sure that it has fixed all of the problems but it has worked for me so far.  

It appears that there is an issue with the m2 compiler.  Since m2 requires a 1.4 jdk the compiler  generates 1.3 compatible code but compiles against 1.4 libs \(or 1.5 or 1.6 depending on what you are running\).  So in the following code:

public class NumberDataType extends AbstractDataType
\{
private static final Number TRUE = new BigDecimal\(1\);
private static final Number FALSE = new BigDecimal\(0\);

With 1.5 libs we get a call to public BigDecimal\(int val\) which does not exist in jdk 1.3 or 1.4.  If we were to compile against the 1.4 libs though we would compile against public BigDecimal\(double val\).

A discussion of this can be found at http://www.mail-archive.com/users@maven.apache.org/msg21609.html

A work around exists where you may either cast to a double or explicitly make the number a decimal by adding a decimal point.

public class NumberDataType extends AbstractDataType
\{
private static final Number TRUE = new BigDecimal\(\(double\) 1\);
private static final Number FALSE = new BigDecimal\(0.0\);

There may very well be other instances like this in the code but I've not exercised DbUnit enough in my project to throw any other errors.

I'll leave it to you to decide how best to manage this issue in the code base \(i.e. I'm not entirely sure how you get your automated tests to catch these types of errors\).

Jason Tholstrup

posts.subject: #56 Expanded Refresh Operations
posts.author: rlogiacco

tickets.description:
I've been using DBUnit to manage the reference data for
our integration/development database and it's been very
useful thus far.  However I have some ideas that may
make it even more useful.  \(I've started to implement
some of them, but I wanted to capture them so others
can comment on it\)

First of all, I've written my own foreign key database
filter since the default was too slow.  \(There may have
been some improvements in cvs, but the version I was
using was really slow\)  Basically I get all the foreign
keys at once since it seems to take the same amount of
time to get all vs. get a single one on the database
I'm working with.  That solves part of my problem.

The next issue I've faced is data failing when trying
to refresh it.  The 2 main scenarios are: unique
constraint violations and foreign key violations \(even
with FK filters, you still run into situations where
you're missing necessary data to satisfy FK's\)  My
proposed solution for the unique index issue is to
create an operation that will delete offending data
based on indices for the record you are inserting.  For
the FK issues I've faces, I'd like to insert the
necessary data \(i.e. create random records\) to satisfy
the foreign key constraints.

My ultimate goal is to be able to insert any data into
any database and just have it work with minimal impact
on the existing data.  Obiously this wouldn't be
something for production data, but for JUnits and
integration testing database, this seems reasonable to
me.  I've begun work on some of this, but don't really
have the time to implement all of this.

I appreciate any comments, for or against this proposed
operation.
Tom

tickets.description:
Problem:
When a MySQL 5.0 export is performed and a column of
the type bit\(1\) is encountered than the following
method invocation occurs:
IDataTypeFactory.createDatatype\(1111, "bit"\). This
datatype is currently not recognized and therefore the
export fails.

Solution:
The attached patch adds recognition of this datatype to
MySqlDataTypeFactory.createDataType\(int, String\).

Don't forget to set the correct datatype factory with
the following property:
datatypeFactory="org.dbunit.ext.mysql.MySqlDataTypeFactory"

Bas Cancrinus
www.iprofs.nl

posts.subject: #118 datatypeFactory documentation note needed
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
This note should be added to the documentation \(it
would have saved me the time of tracing into the source
code to track down a problem\!\):

On the "Ant Integration" page
\(http://www.dbunit.org/anttask.html\), under
"datatypeFactory" attribute, and/or on the
"Configurable features and properties" page, under the
link http://www.dbunit.org/properties.html\#typefactory:

\---------------------------
If you specify a database specific datatype factory,
make sure that the database specific driver jar is in
your classpath.  For example, if you are using the
INSERT, CLEAN\_INSERT or UPDATE operation in an Ant task
to load BLOB's into an Oracle database and you set    

datatypeFactory="org.dbunit.ext.oracle.OracleDataTypeFactory"
, then you must have one of the Oracle JDBC driver
JAR's \(for example, ojdbc14.jar\) in your Ant classpath
\(in <ANT\_HOME>/lib\).  Otherwise Ant will produce the
error:  "org.dbunit.dataset.datatype.TypeCas
tException: Error casting value for table '<TABLE
NAME>' and column '<COLUMN NAME>'.
\---------------------------

It would also have helped if the real error mentioned
above was not masked/translated by the Ant task.  In
other words, if it was more descriptive, for example,
"Could not load class for "oracle.sql.BLOB".

posts.author: dhartford

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=2027376

I get this as well. Looks like its due to the StreamingDataSet only allowing on Iterator to be created, ever. This causes problems when running two DatabaseOperations on the same StreaminDataSet instance, as is the case with CLEAN\_INSERT \(which is a Composite operation\). Running the two DatabaseOperations separately fixes the issue... though with the overhead of having to delete with one StreamingDataSet and insert with another StreamingDataSet.
posts.subject: #121 StreamingDataSet - Only one iterator allowed!
posts.author: mrrove

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=57511
Originator: NO

I think this is correct as a stream can be read only once by definition: imagine the stream comes across network, if you wish to traverse it twice you should request it twice or cache it on your computer, which turns it to a file-based dataset.

Sounds correct to you?

posts.subject: #121 StreamingDataSet - Only one iterator allowed!
posts.author: sf-robot

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=2027376
Originator: NO

I don't thinks so. Other IDataSets are referenced multiple times using composite DatabaseOperations, and since DatabaseOperation takes in the IDataSet interface, it seems to break the contract of abstraction where DatabaseOperation should work on all IDataSets. By someone using the API and passing in a StreamingDataSet to a composite DatabaseOperation, they're explicitly accepting the fact that the stream will be read more than once.

The choice to use a particular method or api based on performance can only be made by the end user who understands their environment. In my case, its far more efficient to stream twice than load an entire database into memory, and I can't imagine anyone else who's using a StreamingDataSet trading memory consumption for network speed while unit testing.

If this \_is\_ the final decision, there should be a check on the composite DatabaseOperation that throws an IllegalArgumentException when passing in the StreamingDataSet indicating why it doesn't work. I imagine, however, that there will be many people wondering why in the world it doesn't work, and will request it to work.

Besides, all you're doing is forcing people to do the long work-around I proposed that essentially does the same thing as creating a second iterator.

posts.subject: #122 possibly i18n problem
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
I was able to reproduce your problem by writing a new test DatabaseTableMetaDataTest\#testCaseInsensitiveAndI18n\(\). I fixed the bug by changing "tableName.toUpperCase\(\)" to "tableName.toUpperCase\(Locale.ENGLISH\)" in method "OrderedTableNameMap\#getTableName\(String tableName\)" \(which provides the table name depending on the case-sensitivity settings\).

It will be available with the 2.4.3 release \(commit is SVN rev 935\).

Thanks and regards,
matthias

posts.subject: #59 Patch for feature request 1482990
posts.author: felipeal

tickets.description:
I could not find a way to attach this patch to feature
request 1482990, so I'm creating a new feature request
for it.

The patch includes configuration for the Maven 2
changes plugin.

There are three things to note:

1\. The Maven 2 changes plugin does not handle html code
within an action, so some actions look weird.

2\. Because of the way SourceForge constructs the URLs
to the tracker, some URLs to issues goes to an error
page that includes a link to the correct page. This is
because bugs and feature requests use a different
"atid" in their URLs.

3\. Ideally the changes.xml file should be moved out of
the xdoc directory. As it is now a changes.html file is
created by the site plugin for this file. Moving the
changes.xml file to its default location
$\{basedir\}/src/changes/changes.xml and changing the pom
accordingly solves this issue.

posts.subject: #129 $ in db names corrupts the xml parser
posts.author: felipeal

posts.text:
Hi there,

the reason for this problem is that the "$" sign is not allowed for XML element names. To address this issue we should think about:

1\. Use a proper API to create/read XML \(I like XOM from www.xom.nu for example\) so that it cannot happen that an invalid XML document is created
2\. Create a dbunit interface to map database table names to XML element names back and forth \(similar to the ant mapper type, but simpler\). The following interface should be sufficient and could be configured via the DatabaseConfig \(just a non-compiling proposal\):

public interface ITableNameMapper \{
/\*\* returns the mapped name for the given table name, 
\* example: input="my$table", result="my\_table"
\*/
public String getMappedName\(String tableName\);
/\*\* returns the table name for the given mapped name, 
\* example: input="my\_table", result="my$table"
\*/
public String getTableName\(String mappedName\);
\}

To all others: please let me know what you think about these changes.

Regards,
mat
posts.subject: #129 $ in db names corrupts the xml parser
posts.author: gommma

posts.text: Another idea to solve this could be to use the full-blown XML format of dbunit without a DTD. The column names are specified as XML values instead of attributes.
posts.subject: #129 $ in db names corrupts the xml parser
posts.author: gommma

posts.text: Another possible solution may be to use a ReplacementDataSet.
posts.subject: #129 $ in db names corrupts the xml parser

tickets.summary: $ in db names corrupts the xml parser
tickets.description:
When xml reading datadumps using a DTD as the second argument fails, if any table in the DTD contains a $ sign. I've tried replacing the sign with a "html code" but with no luck.

To reproduce the error:

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=498804
Originator: YES

This request came from the need for the test setup to create objects from dbUnit data.  dbUnit makes ITables from 2 main sources: the database and the data files \(e.g. flat xml\).  We could have reused production code that created objects from dbUnit ResultSets if dbUnit ITable supported that.

"Testing the database" is one use case - we have many unit tests that test the database, but that is only part of the testing use of dbUnit.

"Testing with the database" is probably more than half of what our tests are - we have lots of automated functional tests that test scenarios with the database, vs mocking it.  This use case is the source of the request.

Using dbUnit for automated functional tests is the use case - something needed to inflate objects from test seed data, not just leave them in ITables.  dbUnit is a good, easy way to do so; even easier if ITable supported ResultSet.
posts.subject: #61 Have ITable implement ResultSet
posts.author: jeffjensen

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1803108
Originator: NO

If I got that right you wanted to have a seamless way to create your DAOs both using dbunit ITables and the plain JDBC ResultSet. If you look at the ResultSet interface, this means that we would have a big amount of methods to implement in ITable which would clutter the code extremely. That is why I have the same opinion as felipe: a decorator/adapter would be the best solution.

It would be very nice if you could provide a patch for that.

Regards,
mat
posts.subject: #61 Have ITable implement ResultSet
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=498804
Originator: YES

Yes, that was the general idea.

Agreed, having ITable implement ResultSet would add many many methods to it; that is usually undesirable.  Decorator is a fine approach. :-\)

As with others' old requests, I no longer work on that codebase.  As long as there is committer buy-in, I am still happy to do it though.  I will put this item on my todo list; I may be able to work it in sometime in the future.  It is not a hard or complex change, just making time to do it.  It seems that perhaps we were about the only ones interested in that feature, so its priority is low  :-\).

Thank you all for the comments on this small idea.  And I really appreciate the current committers reviving dbUnit...it is a very useful test framework\!
posts.subject: #61 Have ITable implement ResultSet

If ITable \(and it's implementing classes\) implemented ResultSet, then a system's prod code that manipulates ResultSets would work great\!
\(current system I'm working on has "makeXxxObject" methods for domain objects from ResultSets; with dbUnit usage, we make dupe methods for this - one for prod code, one for test code\).

For starters, would only really need to implement the ResultSet.get\* methods; others could be UnsupportedOperationExceptions.

posts.subject: #64 [PATCH] ReplacementTable - allow fail if value not replaced
posts.author: gommma

tickets.summary: [PATCH] ReplacementTable - allow fail if value not replaced
tickets.description:
I would like DbUnit to allow a fail-fast when a value is marked \(delimited\) for replacement, but no replacement is found.  For example, if I have a value "$\{test-id\}-1" that should get a replacement, but I accidentally added a replacement for "Test-Id" \(instead of "test-id"\) then the value won't get replaced, when I'm expecting it to.  The reason for the fail-fast is it makes debugging much easier - the developer can immediately fix the test data, rather than debugging his own code that he thinks is failing.

The solution I put together is very simple, and is backwards compatible.  I just added a new property "strictReplacement" to the ReplacementTable and ReplacementDataSet classes.  When set to true \(default is false\), the fail-fast behavior is used.

The patch I submitted includes a test case which I added to ReplacementTableTest.

Thanks a lot in advance,

posts.author: cdubet

posts.text: - **status**: pending --> open
posts.subject: #140 short BLOB are not correctly managed
posts.author: cdubet

posts.text:
Hi there,

thanks for your test. I can now reproduce your problem. It is because the BytesDataType\#typeCast\(\) method invokes a "Base64.decode\(\(String\)value\)" on your string because it assumes that your String is a Base64 String. You could try to use the StringDataType for saving your data, but I'd rather recommend to use a CLOB if you only want to store character data in your DB. I do not yet know how to solve this, do you have a proposal?

Best regards,
matthias
posts.subject: #140 short BLOB are not correctly managed
posts.author: gommma

posts.subject: #140 short BLOB are not correctly managed
posts.author: cdubet

posts.text: - **status**: open --> closed-fixed
posts.subject: #140 short BLOB are not correctly managed
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Hi there,

thanks for your suggestions. After some thoughts I did the following steps:

\- updated javadocs in BytesDataType\#typeCast\(\) and BinaryStreamDataType\#setSqlValue\(\)
\- added check for <null> in BinaryStreamDataType\#setSqlValue\(\) to invoke statement.setNull\(\) instead of statement.setBinaryStream\(\)
\- added info log message when the given String is interpreted as Base64 because it is neither a URL nor a File

I deliberately did not add your code since it is - at least from my point of view - not correct to assume that if the "byte\[\].length==0" we can be sure that the conversion failed. There may be cases in which this is okay. If you do not agree please let me know your reasons why this assumption is correct.

I committed my change to rev. 940/trunk for the upcoming 2.4.3 bugfix release.

Regards,
matthias

posts.subject: #145 Table order failure
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
Hi,
the DatabaseSequenceFilter orders tables checking if the current table primary key is being refered by any database constraint. I have a database where a foreign key refers to a column which is just unique, but not primary key. The result is constraint exception when issueing a DELETE\_ALL. The DatabaseSequenceFilter should not only check constrains on table primary keys, but also constraints on columns which are unique.

Cheers,
filippo

tickets.summary: Document whitespace handling in XML serialization
tickets.description:
By editing and reformating the XML files produced by dbunit, it is possible that whitespace at the beginning and at the end of strings is introduced.

These mistakes can be a problem, for example I've been locked out of a webapp because the password hash in the bootstrap data now had whitespace around it.

dbunit should make clear whether it considers xml whitespace to be of the significant kind, or not significant. For columns where it is significant, embedding it into a CDATA could be nice. A comment at the top of files serialized by unitdb would also be helpful.

posts.subject: #146 Export fails with DataSetException ORA-01424
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
As I read at http://www.mail-archive.com/ddlutils-dev@db.apache.org/msg01246.html the reason for this problem might be that you are using a pre-oracle10 jdbc driver. The cause seems to be the oracle recycle bin as I read at http://www.talendforge.org/forum/viewtopic.php?pid=8769.
To avoid this exception you can set the "skipOracleRecycleBin" property in the dbunit ant task to true \(this prop is available since dbunit 2.2.1\):

...
<dbunit skipOracleRecycleBinTables="true" ...>
...
</dbunit>
...

This might be in item for the FAQs.

Regards,
mat

Hi,

the problem with DBUnit in our case is the handling of schema names. Normally,
there are multiple schmemas within a single database. So we were kind of wondering
why you have to specify a schema name when obtaining a db connection. Can you elaborate on
this concept please?
Due to the rather complicated schema handling in full qualified mode, the method
getDependentTables did not work in our case. The problem is that even though we
enabled "full qualified name support" we did not get the full qualified table name
in AbstractMetaDataBasedSearchCallback.getNodes. This is why we added the additional code.
We are running DB2 8.1.14.

posts.subject: #147 getAllDependentTables didn't work with full qualified names
posts.author: jmlozano

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1803108
Originator: NO

Hi all,

closing the result set is implemented \(see bug \#2014884\) for release 2.3.0.

I also did some major changes \(including unit tests for this bugfix\) to consolidate the "qualified table name" functionality which is needed in several places. Therefore I introduced a new class "QualifiedTableName" which also provides the "qualified-table-name" methods that have formerly been in DataSetUtils \(changes are committed in rev. 712/trunk\).

I would appreciate if someone of you \(Roberto, Sebastien\) could review this latest commit and tell me what you think about my latest commit. I intended to make the code cleaner and to avoid duplicate code.

As an explanation for the below mentioned fact that the schema name can be given to the DatabaseConnection constructor I think that the original authors designed this as kind of a "default" schema for all selects. This is especially useful for performance optimisation \(especially if your db has a huge amount of schemas around\) within dbunit.

Thanks and regards,
matthias

tickets.description:
When using the following code:

OutputStream outputStream = new FileOutputStream\("src/test/resources/schema.dtd"\);
FlatDtdWriter writer = new FlatDtdWriter\(new OutputStreamWriter\(outputStream\)\);
writer.setContentModel\(FlatDtdWriter.CHOICE\);
writer.write\(dataSet\);

DBUnit crashes with a NullPointerException.

This is the stack trace:

java.lang.NullPointerException
	at org.dbunit.dataset.xml.FlatDtdProducer.endDTD\(FlatDtdProducer.java:224\)
	at org.apache.xerces.parsers.AbstractSAXParser.endDTD\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.dtd.XMLDTDProcessor.endDTD\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLDTDScannerImpl.endEntity\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLEntityManager.endEntity\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLEntityScanner.load\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLEntityScanner.skipSpaces\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLDTDScannerImpl.skipSeparator\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLDTDScannerImpl.scanDecls\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLDTDScannerImpl.scanDTDExternalSubset\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLDocumentScannerImpl$DTDDispatcher.dispatch\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLDocumentFragmentScannerImpl.scanDocument\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.parsers.XML11Configuration.parse\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.parsers.XML11Configuration.parse\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.parsers.XMLParser.parse\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.apache.xerces.parsers.AbstractSAXParser.parse\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.xml.FlatXmlProducer.produce\(FlatXmlProducer.java:145\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.CachedDataSet.<init>\(CachedDataSet.java:71\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.xml.FlatXmlDataSet.<init>\(FlatXmlDataSet.java:125\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.xml.FlatXmlDataSet.<init>\(FlatXmlDataSet.java:101\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.xml.FlatXmlDataSet.<init>\(FlatXmlDataSet.java:88\)
	at nl..................BaseTestCase.getDataSet\(BaseTestCase.java:53\)
	at org.dbunit.DatabaseTestCase.setUp\(DatabaseTestCase.java:119\)
	at nl..................BaseTestCase.setUp\(BaseTestCase.java:32\)
	at junit.framework.TestCase.runBare\(TestCase.java:128\)
	at junit.framework.TestResult$1.protect\(TestResult.java:110\)
	at junit.framework.TestResult.runProtected\(TestResult.java:128\)
	at junit.framework.TestResult.run\(TestResult.java:113\)
	at junit.framework.TestCase.run\(TestCase.java:120\)
	at junit.framework.TestSuite.runTest\(TestSuite.java:228\)
	at junit.framework.TestSuite.run\(TestSuite.java:223\)
	at org.junit.internal.runners.OldTestClassRunner.run\(OldTestClassRunner.java:35\)
	at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.junit4.runner.JUnit4TestReference.run\(JUnit4TestReference.java:38\)
	at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.junit.runner.TestExecution.run\(TestExecution.java:38\)
	at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.junit.runner.RemoteTestRunner.runTests\(RemoteTestRunner.java:460\)
	at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.junit.runner.RemoteTestRunner.runTests\(RemoteTestRunner.java:673\)
	at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.junit.runner.RemoteTestRunner.run\(RemoteTestRunner.java:386\)
	at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.junit.runner.RemoteTestRunner.main\(RemoteTestRunner.java:196\)


The problem is that in the while loop, there are checks on the characters:  '\*', '?' and '+', but not on '\(', which is necessary for the first table.

Possible solution:

Change this:
String rootModel = \_rootModel.substring\(1, \_rootModel.length\(\) - 1\);

Into something like this:
String rootModel = \_rootModel.substring\(1, \_rootModel.length\(\) - 1\);
if \(rootModel.startsWith\("\("\)\) \{
rootModel = rootModel.substring\(1, rootModel.length\(\) - 2\);
\}

tickets.summary: Database schema with escape pattern doesn\'t work
tickets.description:
I found a problem using qualified table names \(support for schemas\) in combination with escaping pattern: the escape pattern is applied to the composite name instead of the single names.

As an example if I wish to use a qualified name of AUTH.USER with an escaping pattern of "?" I get "AUTH.USER" instead of "AUTH"."USER" which is what I need \(and I suppose is what others need\).

Attached is a patch to DataSetUtils to resolve the problem.

Hi jondi, Roberto,

I successfully tested it on an oracle XE having the schema name set in the DatabaseConnection constructor \("new DatabaseConnection\(jdbcConn, "MYSCHEMA"\). When I omit the schema name I can see the same exception as you do \("org.dbunit.dataset.NoColumnsFoundException: XDB$ACL"\). 
Moreover I can see a dbunit warning now: "WARNING - XDB$ACL.SYS\_NC\_ROWINFO$ data type \(1111, 'XMLTYPE'\) not recognized and will be ignored. See FAQ for more information."

So I think you can solve this by specifying the schema name. Please let me know if that works for you. I will look at the "NoColumnsFoundException" so that the export does not fail when a table does not have any columns. Perhaps we need some discussions about the "NoColumnsFoundException" and if this is a proper way to tell the user that dbunit did not find any columns for a table...

Regards,
mat

posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1803108
Originator: NO

I looked into this behaviour and found out that the "ITableMetaData.getColumns\(\)" implementation in "DatabaseTableMetaData.getColumns\(\)" does not behave consistently to all of the other implementations of this method. It throws a "NoColumnsFoundException" instead of just returning an empty array of columns. So basically the contract of this method is broken \(whereas the contract's behaviour is not documented at all in the interface...\). Does anybody know why this exception is thrown? It is in the explicitly checked in the unit test so there must have been a reason for this \(I guess\). 

Nevertheless I propose to change the implementation of "DatabaseTableMetaData.getColumns\(\)" to simply return an empty array of columns if no column has been found \(that is supported by dbunit\). I would also like to add this information to the javadocs of the interface so that the contract is clearly defined.

Please let me know your opinions on this.

regards,
mat

posts.text:

Hi all,

I committed the bugfix \(rev. 720/trunk\) for this issue and changed the behaviour of method "DatabaseTableMetaData.getColumns\(\)" which now returns an empty "Column\[\]" array if no columns have been detected for a table.

Please let me know if you think that this might impact some other functionality that was based on the exception that was formerly thrown.

Regards,
mat

posts.subject: #153 Multiple inserts on the same table with optional values
posts.author: jigarzon

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=671569
Originator: NO

This is a known limitation, cf. http://dbunit.sourceforge.net/apidocs/org/dbunit/dataset/xml/FlatXmlDataSet.html

Without a DTD, DBunit uses the first occurrence of a line to infer the schema... So if your first line does not have one of the attribute, then this attribute will not be used.

You either have to have to use a DTD or to use a ReplacementDataset to define some special value that'd have to be replaced with null. We use the second approach, since it makes it clearer in the dataset that the column is to be inserted with null value and does not force us to define the dtd \(which is difficult in our case\). For example, we use \[NULL\], but you could obviously use whatever you want. See this page http://dbunit.sourceforge.net/components.html for more infos about ReplacementDataset

So, I understand that this is a known behavior and there are workarounds. At the same time, I don't think that makes it any less of a bug.  

The behavior as it stands is confusing and \(if you don't look at the way it is implemented\) completely irrational.  The workaround requires either dynamic DTD generation \(why couldn't FlatXmlDataSet do the equivalent introspection itself?\) or redundant entries of replacement null values.

I've been using dbunit for years, and this problem still sends me down a blind alley from time to time.  If there's no plan to fix it right away, I can understand that \(I've been wanting to give it a go for a while, but have nto had time\), but it should at least be acknowledged as a bug.

Hi all,

I think there is a bit confusion around here. The "AmbiguousTableNameException: AAADIFF\_COLS1" probably has nothing to do with the oracle recycle bin tables. The oldest of the comments here is most appropriate I think: dbunit doesn't like to have 2 tables with the same name but different case in the same schema.
So if you do the following on oracle:

create table "aa" \(COL1 varchar2\(10\)\);
create table "AA" \(COL1 varchar2\(10\)\);

If you do a

select object\_name from user\_objects;

you will see that there are 2 tables called "aa" and "AA". This leads to a conflict when dbunit tries to build up the dataSet because the table-names are all "toUpperCase\(\)"-ed.

One possible solution for this would be to introduce a new dbunit property that lets the user configure whether or not table names should all be "toUpperCase\(\)"-ed or not.

Please let me know your opinion.

Regards,
mat

posts.author: rlogiacco

tickets.description:
I am using a embeded Derby database to run unit testing for my code and I ran into the following problem.  Within  a table that I am working with there is a column that is defined as follows:

LONG\_TEXT CLOB NOT NULL

Data is then taken from a XmlDataFile \(not flat - Flat causes other problems\).  When the data is loaded from the XML file it runs through the class org.dbunit.dataset.datatype.ClobDataType.  In this class the string from the XML file is sent to the DB as a "longvarchar" and then derby throws an error.  the solution is to not use "DataType.LONGVARCHAR.getSqlType\(\)" but just use "getSqlType\(\)" in the "setSqlValue" function.

posts.subject: #74 Create H2 data factory
posts.author: felipeal

tickets.description: H2 has the same BOOLEAN issue as HSQLDB...

posts.text:
Hi Felipe,

are the H2 and HSQLDB implementations related to each other? AFAIK they are not and it probably is by chance that the DataTypeFactories are equal. This is why I would not create a common superclass for those two. What do you think?

Regards,
mat
posts.subject: #163 Improvements on HxxxxDataFactory
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Hi Mat,

Technically speaking, they are not related, as pointed out in \(http://www.h2database.com/html/history.html\):

"The development of H2 was started in May 2004, but it was first published on December 14th 2005. The author of H2, Thomas Mueller, is also the original developer of Hypersonic SQL. In 2001, he joined PointBase Inc. where he created PointBase Micro. At that point, he had to discontinue Hypersonic SQL, but then the HSQLDB Group was formed to continued to work on the Hypersonic SQL codebase. The name H2 stands for Hypersonic 2; however H2 does not share any code with Hypersonic SQL or HSQLDB. H2 is built from scratch."

Pragmatically speaking, though, I think they are very similar, particularly from the point of view of client code \(I used both within Hibernate test cases, and they behave exactly the same\).

So, my suggestion is that we have an abstract super-class \(some thing like HxxxDataTypeFactory\) with the common code \(and explain in the Javadoc that they are similar, but not exactly related\), then extend it to the existing H2 and Hsqldb types.

\-- Felipe
posts.subject: #163 Improvements on HxxxxDataFactory
posts.author: felipeal

posts.text:
Aha. Thanks for this detailed information. I see your point. But this one sentence

"however H2 does not share any code with Hypersonic SQL or HSQLDB"

is the key fact to me for keeping those 2 dbunit factories completely separated.

Another indicator making me afraid of creating this superclass is that we would need to describe this fact in detail in the javadocs \(as you already suggested\).
posts.subject: #163 Improvements on HxxxxDataFactory
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Yes, I saw that too. 

Anyway, I think both approaches have their advantages and disadvantages. If you prefer to keep as is, I'm fine with that, but please remove the TODO from the Javadocs before closing this ticket.
posts.subject: #163 Improvements on HxxxxDataFactory
posts.author: felipeal

posts.text: - **status**: open --> closed-wont-fix
posts.subject: #163 Improvements on HxxxxDataFactory
posts.author: gommma

posts.text: I removed the TODOs from the code \(svn rev. 883\). Thanks for your goodwill :-\)
posts.subject: #163 Improvements on HxxxxDataFactory
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
Both H2DataTypeFactory and HsqldbDataTypeFactory are identical \(which makes sense, as the DB are related\), and hence should derive from a common superclass.
Also, there is something wrong with either the test case or the implementation, as the tests pass even if the custom code to handle the BINARY type is removed from the factory.
Finally, it would be nice to have a real rest case, that uses an embedded database to test such factories.

posts.text:
I'd go even a step further and would say the dbunit could provide more information on an assertion failure. For example all data of all tables could be compared and all differences could be reported instead of a failure when the first difference was detected. Therefore we could introduce "Assertion.compare\(\)" methods in addition to the "Assertion.assertEquals\(\)" methods which simply return some kind of a difference object.

Basically it would be great to have an own dbunit framework for reportion assertion failures. The classes I found that currently depend on junit are:
\- org.dbunit.util.FileAsserts
\- org.dbunit.Assertion
\- org.dbunit.DatabaseTestCase

I removed the dependencies from
\- org.dbunit.Assertion
\- org.dbunit.util.FileAsserts

by improving the FailureHandler interface and adding an implementation for JUnit. This JUnit implementation is used by default now in the org.dbunit.Assertion methods, but can be passed in as an additional argument \(if you like to use TestNG, for example\).

Perhaps we could also remove the dependency to JUnit from the DatabaseTestCase class using the FailureHandler interface.

Please feel free to improve this \(for example reading the FailureHandler dynamically from a SystemProperty\) or to add some comments on this.

My commit is svn rev 864/trunk.

Regards,
mat
posts.subject: #164 Remove JUnit dependency
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Hi Mathias,

First of all, thanks for the effort making the project cleaner.

Now, some comments:

> Perhaps we could also remove the dependency to JUnit from the
> DatabaseTestCase class using the FailureHandler interface.

I think that's not necessary, as DatabaseTestCase is meant for JUnit - it relies on setup\(\) and teardown\(\), for instance. What we could do in the future is to split the project in sub-projects with its own jars \(dbunit-core, dbunit-junit, dbunit-testng, dbunit-all.jar, etc...\), so these framework-specific classes would belong to the proper sub-project.

> Therefore we could introduce "Assertion.compare\(\)" methods in addition to the
> "Assertion.assertEquals\(\)"

Adding such a method could cause confusion, because calling these methods assertXXX is kind of an idiom on test frameworks. OTOH, the current assertEquals\(\) method is not flexible enough, so we could do some refactoring in this method \(I will talk more about it in the dev's list\).

\[\]s,

\-- Felipe
posts.subject: #164 Remove JUnit dependency
posts.author: felipeal

posts.text:
Hi Felipe,

here my comments on this:

> I think that's not necessary, as DatabaseTestCase is meant for JUnit - it
> relies on setup\(\) and teardown\(\), for instance. What we could do in the
> future is to split the project in sub-projects with its own jars
> \(dbunit-core, dbunit-junit, dbunit-testng, dbunit-all.jar, etc...\)

You are right. Since there are only 2 classes that depend on JUnit directly at the moment I am not sure if it is worth the effort creating an extra jar for them. \(These classes are JUnitFailureHandler and DatabaseTestCase\)

>> Therefore we could introduce "Assertion.compare\(\)" methods in addition 
>> to the "Assertion.assertEquals\(\)"

> Adding such a method could cause confusion, because calling these methods
> assertXXX is kind of an idiom on test frameworks.

Do you know xmlunit? It is a very good example for the case we are facing in dbunit as well. A dbunit assertion is not kind of a "single unit of work" assertion as is a normal JUnit test. Instead it compares a whole bunch of data. For evaluating the cause of a test failure it might be helpful if one could retrieve all rows/columns that did not match instead of only the first one. \(This is the same way xmlunit compares two xml strings - you can retrieve the diff for the whole document\). I will think about a potential solution and will keep you in the loop. I will open a new request for this.

Regards,
mat
posts.subject: #164 Remove JUnit dependency
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Hi Felipe,

I have another suggestion for clarifying the dependencies dbunit currently has on JUnit. What about creating a package "org.dbunit.junit" which contains all JUnit specific stuff? I think these are currently the following:

\- JUnitFailureFactory
\- DatabaseTestCase \(+ inherited DBTestCase + inherited classes DataSourceBasedDBTestCase/JdbcBasedDBTestCase/JndiBasedDBTestCase\)
\- ant.DbUnitTask

The only problem I currently see is the DbUnitAssert which uses the JUnitFailureFactory for backwards compatibility reasons.

Regards,
mat
posts.subject: #164 Remove JUnit dependency
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
I think there are two solutions:

* More modules: for some classes it is to much effort. 
* Mark JUnit dependency as optional. And it is a good practice to move the things depend on JUnit to a separate package.

tickets.summary: Public constructors for default data type

Only a few default data types provide public constructors \(the others are package protected\).  
Is it possible to generalize this characteristic to all data type? 
That would allow more flexibility and ease to overload existing data types.
Example: to overload the DateDataType class, I had to create a PublicDateDataType in the same package, with a public constructor. Then I overloaded this class to create my own type.

posts.subject: #166 getTable fails with RowOutOfBoundsException using Oracle 10
posts.author: rlogiacco

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1803108
Originator: NO

Hi all,

I installed the new 2.2.2 release today hoping that this exception goes away but it didn't. I browsed a bit through the sources and finally came to the conclusion that this bug is not a duplicate of \#1925603. The log message is written in class

http://dbunit.svn.sourceforge.net/svnroot/dbunit/tags/release-2.2.2/src/java/org/dbunit/dataset/DefaultTable.java

method "addTableRows\(ITable table\)" in the catch block before the comment "end of table".

A quick fix would be \(equal to \#1925603\) to change the log level from "error" to "debug".


From my point of view, the clean \(and more time-consuming\) implementation would be one of the following:
A\) Do not throw an exception from org/dbunit/database/ForwardOnlyResultSetTable.getValue\(\) when the end of the table was reached \(\_eot=true\). Instead return a constantly defined EOT object that must be checked by the invoker or introduce a method "boolean endOfTableReached\(\)".
B\) Throw a specific "EotException" which can be explicitly handled.

I want to suggest to reopen this bug and start a little discussion if needed/wished.

Thanks and regards,
mat
posts.subject: #166 getTable fails with RowOutOfBoundsException using Oracle 10
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=57511
Originator: NO

Hi gomma,
actually we missed one logging statement when we patched the source to avoid this logs clutter the console so I still consider this issue a duplicate one.
Anyway both me and Sebastien are welcome to any proposal about removing that "unclean" handling of this condition, but we were focusing on a quick release so we applied the quickfix.

Hi Roberto,

Another thing I want to mention with respect to logging \(also concerns bug \#dbunit-Bugs-1963062\) is that I strongly suspect the new logging to be responsible for the heavy performance decrease from 2.2 to 2.2.2 \(mentioned in \#\#dbunit-Bugs-1963062\). Normally the methods "logger.isDebugEnabled\(\)" etc. should be used before logging to avoid the performance overhead of string-concatenation or parameterized logging statements. See http://www.slf4j.org/faq.html\#logging\_performance. I think this is what Roberto queued as feature request with the ID \#1959825.

Thanks,
mat

tickets.description:
Hello there,

I'm having trouble testing tables with the Datatype TIME when they have values larger than 24:00:00.

This is what I found on the Mysql Site concerning the datatype TIME:
MySQL retrieves and displays TIME values in 'HH:MM:SS' format \(or 'HHH:MM:SS' format for large hours values\). TIME values may range from '-838:59:59' to '838:59:59'. The hours part may be so large because the TIME type can be used not only to represent a time of day \(which must be less than 24 hours\), but also elapsed time or a time interval between two events \(which may be much greater than 24 hours, or even negative\).

I let DBUnit insert the data and instead of inserting 24:01:00, it inserts 00:01:00. It won't allow larger values than 24:00

I tried a mysql and a hsql database, same result.

Am I doing something wrong?? 

Thanx for the help
greetz
Marco

posts.subject: #170 Schema not used in getTableMetaData()
posts.author: tkelley353

tickets.description:
In the DatabaseDataSet class, the getTableMetaData\(tableName\) method attempts to look up the table name from the internal collection named \_tableMap.

When using a schema, this \_tableMap holds the correct FQ table name, however the table name passed in is never updated with the current schema. As a result, the seek for the table name fails and a NoSuchTableException ensues.

posts.author: rlogiacco

tickets.summary: End of table name cut off when bracketed in FlatDtdProducer
tickets.description:
Related to 1849219:

In a DTD with grouped elements \(tables grouped together with brackets\), the last character of the name of the first table of the group is trimmed off along with the opening bracket.

Example: "\(Table" becomes "Tabl".

This causes a NullPointerException on line 274 of FlatDtdProducer.java, in endDtd\(\), since obviously a column list can't be found for Tabl\! :\)

From what I can tell, this is an off-by-one error in the substring operation on line 265- the length param is one short. In fact, this parameter can be omitted altogether, since we want to grab the whole string after the bracket.

Fixed file attached\!

posts.subject: #172 Memory leak
posts.author: rlogiacco

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=2076978
Originator: YES

Am I absolutely sure no tests would break?  No, and I don't think I can be.  I can only assure you that none of ours broke, and we have quite a few.  I can also tell you that given the behavior I've seen from test runners, it seems highly unlikely that it would cause any problems.

In the test runners I've used \(Eclipse and Surefire\), the test class is instantiated anew for every test.  So if my class FooTest has 10 tests in it, by the time I'm done running them, I have 10 instances of FooTest on the heap.  Since the tester variable is an instance variable, it's going to be re-instantiated with every test anyway, regardless of what you did with the previous test's instance.  Since that's the case, it makes sense to let the garbage collector grab the "old" instances, especially since they can hold quite a bit of memory \(if your DB has many tables, as ours does\).

The only time I can foresee this being an issue is if there's a test runner that reuses the test class instance for each test.  I have no idea if such a test runner exists, or if it's even legal in the JUnit paradigm.

I was running this on both IBM's and Sun's 1.4.2 JDK.  I received the error on both.  I profiled it with JProfiler to track down the problem.

posts.subject: #76 Add DatabaseConnection methods to support PreparedStatements
posts.author: sf-robot

tickets.summary: Add DatabaseConnection methods to support PreparedStatements
tickets.description:
Basically for us the change is needed to setup for testing a batch job's "fetcher SQL" - proving that its query works correctly.  The core issue is the use of prepared statements and their parameters.  dbUnit currently does not support that; it only supports SQL strings.

We subclassed DatabaseConnection and added the two methods in the attached file to support testing like this:

String sql = batch.getFetcherSql\(\);

DatabaseConnectionCustom conn = \(DatabaseConnectionCustom\) getConnection\(\);
PreparedStatement stmt = conn.createPreparedStatement\(sql\);
batch.setFetcherSQLParameters\(stmt\);
ITable actualTable = conn.createQueryTable\("Generated\_SQL", stmt\);


The general explanation of the above code is:
1\. Get the fetcher SQL from the batch job class
2\. Create a prepared statement using the dbUnit connection with the SQL
\(using the new createPreparedStatement\)
3\. Pass the statement to the batch class for it to set the parameters.
4\. Execute the prepared statement query \(using the new createQueryTable\).
5\. The rest would be normal dbUnit stuff...


In summary, we can't do those tests without those methods.  We can keep our
custom implementation, of course.  However I think dbUnit should support
prepared statements too, and wonder that others could use this feature.

posts.subject: #77 Support RowFilter on ITable
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
It would be quite useful to have a common usable row filter \(based on arbitrary columns\) for the ITable. So you would be able to load a whole bunch of data from the db into memory and could filter those out that are not relevant for the assertion you want to do currently.

There is already such an implementation which unfortunately only works for primary keys \(org/dbunit/database/PrimaryKeyFilteredTableWrapper.java\).

I appended two proposals based on the PKFilteredTableWrapper:

1\.
RowFilterTableBySingleColumn.java
-> convenient way if you only want to filter rows using a single column

2\.
RowFilterTableByMultipleColumns.java
IRowValueProvider.java
-> to filter rows using arbitrary columns


I would appreciate any comments on this and - of course - if this would finally be included into a release :-\)

Regards,
mat

posts.author: sf-robot

tickets.summary: support for tolerated delta in floating-point comparisons
tickets.description:
This is what JUnit provides for floating-point operations by method
assertEquals\(double val1, double val2, double delta\)

I added a patch as a first proposal. By implementing the new interface IDataTypeFactory2 you can create instances of DataType for specific table.column values.
Using the NumberTolerantDataType object provides the comparison methods needed for the DBUnit Assertion then. As an example on how to use it I also added a sample implementation of IDataTypeFactory2 called DeltaDataTypeFactory.

I introduced the new interface IDataTypeFactory2 so that the old API is not broken.

If this proposal makes sense to some of you we should discuss if the existing implementations of the original IDataTypeFactory should also implement the interface IDataTypeFactory2.

Looking forward to your comments.

Thanks and regards,
mat

posts.subject: #80 Assertion.equals to provide more info about failure
posts.author: gommma

tickets.summary: Assertion.equals to provide more info about failure
tickets.description:
We compare two tables from two different database schemas with each other having several thousands of rows in each. In order to make the dbunit-assertion work we use the SortedTable which reorders the result rows. 
If one cell does not match dbunit writes a beautiful assertion that only contains the in-memory row number for which the comparison failed. Here an example \(row=1 identifies the in-memory row index\):

junit.framework.AssertionFailedError: value \(table=TEST\_TABLE, row=1, col=COLUMN2\): expected:<row 1 col 2> but was:<wrong value>

Using this ID it is very painful to detect the physical database rows \(expected,actual\) for which the comparison failed. I suggest to introduce an equals method that allows the user to define additional columns to be reported when a comparison fails. These can be used to identify the rows easily on the DB.

The method could look as follows:

Assertion.assertEquals\(ITable expectedTable, ITable actualTable, Column\[\] additionalColumnInfo\)

The failure message could look as follows:

junit.framework.AssertionFailedError: value \(table=TEST\_TABLE, row=1, col=COLUMN2\): expected:<row 1 col 2> but was:<wrong value>
Additional row info: \(col 'MY\_PK\_COLUMN' values: expected=<4728374>, actual=<4728375>\)

Having the additional information about the PK-column it is possible to identify the rows that differ very easily.

The patch including a UnitTest is appended.

Since this change is a very small I would be happy if you could introduce it in the near future.

Thanks in advance and regards,
mat

-1 for me.

I don't think this is in DbUnit scope.  This type of requirements opens a whole lot of issues that are not DbUnit's responsibility and IMO, shouldn't be as a unit test library -- like exporting from one Rdbms to import into another \(I'm thinking of Oracle to Db2, for example\), etc.  Having dealt with sql exports and cross-database issues in the past, I know this is particularly tricky stuff, and very hard to maintain too because it requires skills in the various RDMSes.  Come to think of it, most issues we have in DbUnit today are to do with the db-specific part...

The original choice of using XML as the primary format was just to avoid this type of concerns which, again, I think are out of the scope of a unit test library.

posts.text:
Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1803108
Originator: YES

Hi Sebastien,

I am very sorry, I did not run the whole suite so I did not recognise that a test broke.

The problem in org.dbunit.operation.InsertOperation\#testExecute is a mismatch of the Column types in the "afterTables" \(those Metadata contain correct types like VARCHAR, NUMBER, ...\) and the "dataSet" Column types which are "UNKNOWN". I suppose it is ok to change the unit tests so that they use the metadata of the table they belong to. Please correct me if this breaks some of the business logic I am not aware of. 
So I basically changed the following line in org.dbunit.operation.InsertOperation\#testExecute\(\) as follows:
OLD
<code>SortedTable expectedTable = new SortedTable\(dataSet.getTable\(name\), table.getTableMetaData\(\)\);</code>
NEW 
<code>SortedTable expectedTable = new SortedTable\(dataSet.getTable\(name\), dataSet.getTable\(name\).getTableMetaData\(\)\);</code>

The whole patch is attached \(including SortedTable patch\). 

The root problem for the test failure is that the Column.equals\(\) method does not return "true" for Columns with the same name but different column types. If you do not agree with my change to the test case I could also provide a modification for SortedTable that uses only the columnNames instead of the whole Column object for the existence check.

Sebastien, thanks for your quick reply. I really like the work all of you are doing.

Regards,
mat

tickets.summary: SortedTable throws NPE when a column is unknown
tickets.description:
I use the SortedTable defining my sort-columns in the constructor as follows:

String\[\] sortColumnNames = new String\[\] \{"COLUMN2", "COLUMNXY\_UNDEFINED"\};
SortedTable sortedTable = new SortedTable\(iTable, sortColumnNames\);

Accidentally it happened that I passed in a column that does not exist in the given table.
This causes a NPE without any detailed information about the cause when the data is accessed the first time \(line 171 in SortedTable, as of release 2.2.3\).

I propose to add a check to the constructor that shows up the exact cause of the underlying problem \(NoSuchColumn\). 

A patch including unit tests is attached.

Hope this comes with the next release.

Thanks and regards,
mat

posts.author: bustuila

tickets.description:
Since version 2.2.2, the insert operation fails with clobs and Oracle:

java.lang.ClassCastException: java.lang.String cannot be cast to oracle.sql.CLOB
at oracle.jdbc.driver.OraclePreparedStatement.setObjectCritical\(OraclePreparedStatement.java:9229\)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.OraclePreparedStatement.setObjectInternal\(OraclePreparedStatement.java:8843\)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.OraclePreparedStatement.setObject\(OraclePreparedStatement.java:9316\)
at org.dbunit.dataset.datatype.ClobDataType.setSqlValue\(ClobDataType.java:67\)
at org.dbunit.database.statement.SimplePreparedStatement.addValue\(SimplePreparedStatement.java:73\)
at org.dbunit.database.statement.AutomaticPreparedBatchStatement.addValue\(AutomaticPreparedBatchStatement.java:63\)
at org.dbunit.operation.AbstractBatchOperation.execute\(AbstractBatchOperation.java:201\)
at org.dbunit.ant.Operation.execute\(Operation.java:212\)

The change that triggers this problem seems to come from this:

ClobDataType.getSqlValue\(int column, ResultSet resultSet\) contains
statement.setObject\(column, typeCast\(value\), getSqlType\(\)\);

where getSqlType\(\) returns Types.CLOB -> 2005

In version 2.2.1, where it works, it was:
statement.setObject\(column, typeCast\(value\),           DataType.LONGVARCHAR.getSqlType\(\)\);

where DataType.LONGVARCHAR.getSqlType\(\) -> -1
the same as Types.LONGVARCHAR

Oracle driver: 10.2.0.2.0 and 10.2.0.4.0
Oracle Database 10g Enterprise Edition Release 10.2.0.1.0 - 64bit Production

posts.text:

user\_id=1803108

Hi Sebastien,

Regarding your 2 comments:

\- "as long as the AbstractTableMetaData is immutable with respect to the
"Column\[\]" object"  What do you mean by that?  Isn't that the point of rev.
643, i.e. modifying Column\[\] as new columns are being discovered \(I
actually hadn't seen that change before\)? -> That is no contradiction since the discovery of new columns is handled by a buffer and the final DatabaseMetaData is created AFTER all Column\[\] information has been gathered - so the Column\[\] array is still final in the resulting DatabaseMetaData \(passed in once in the constructor and never changed later\)
\- Can we use a HashMap rather than Hashtable? -> I don't mind using a HashMap instead. But the Hashtable does not allow null values which I find advantageous in this special case.

If you wish we could continue the conversation on the dev-lists.

Regards,
mat

posts.subject: #83 Performance optimization getColumnIndex()
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
Hi Roberto/Sebastien/community

I had a deeper look into the code with respect to performance. I found that the method AbstractTable.getColumnIndex\(String colName\) is invoked for every ForwardOnlyResultSet.getValue\(\). The implementation of "getColumnIndex" loops every time through all columns to find the index of
the current column. This causes a heavy impact on performance for me since my test table has aprox. 3200 rows.
In the patch I attached I introduced a new method getColumnIndex in the AbstractTableMetaData which is accessiblevia the interface ITableMetaData. It uses a hashtable internally and the performance results are really amazing.
I measured the time that the call "Assertion.assertEquals\(ITable expectedTable, ITable actualTable\)" takes with the following results:

old \(2.2.3\) version:   1130 secs
my patch applied:      299 secs
so the improvement factor is approx. >3.5 which is really significant to me\!

I would like to ask you to review it \(especially Roberto/Sebastien\) and if you agree I would commit it to the repository. 
At this time I do not see any drawbacks of this solution, as long as the AbstractTableMetaData is immutable with respect to the "Column\[\]" objects. The only ugliness is that the new method invokes the "getColumns\(\)" from the interface which is not implemented in the AbstractTableMetaData itself but only by the concrete implementations. But I do not see a problem with that. Please let me know what you think.

Thanks a lot
mat

tickets.description:
While trying to create a dependent dataset from a Oracle 10g database, I found the following bug:

I get:
1\. getDataset\(IDatabaseConnection connection, java.lang.String rootTable, java.util.Set allowedIds\) without prodiving the schema name results in an org.dbunit.dataset.NoSuchTableException when calling write\(IDataSet dataSet, java.io.OutputStream out\).
2\. getDataset\(IDatabaseConnection connection, java.lang.String rootTable, java.util.Set allowedIds\) with prodiving the schema name works, but doesn't include the dependent tables in the final xml file.

It's no difference whether I use System.setProperty\( PropertiesBasedJdbcDatabaseTester.DBUNIT\_SCHEMA, "MYSCHEMA" \) or not.


I also noticed the following:
1\. getDependentTables\(IDatabaseConnection connection, java.lang.String rootTable\) without providing the schema name gives me the correct dependent table names.
2\. getDependentTables\(IDatabaseConnection connection, java.lang.String rootTable\) with providing the schema name gives me only the root table itself.

So when I don't provide the schame name, I can find all the dependent tables, but the method which should create my dataset fails.
If I do not provide it, I can create a dataset, but without the dependent tables.

posts.author: slecallonnec

tickets.summary: BytesDataType.typeCast() makes too much noise in the log
tickets.description:
When a binary value represented with a string is converted, first it's checked to be an URL, then to be a file name, and finally it's decoded using Base64.
Both attempts \(URL and file\) are logging ERRORs. So when we execute a CLEAN\_INSERT operation, we have log full of these messages which are absolutely non-informative.
I suggest to lower their log level \(for instance, to DEBUG\).

I'm talking about dbunit-2.2.3

tickets.summary: Alternative support for CLOB and BLOB in Oracle 11 JDBC
tickets.description:
I have some problems with CLOBs and BLObs while using Oracle JDBC 11.1.0.6 with Oracle Database 10.2.0.4.0.

First I tried to use OraclaDataTypeFactory, but it requires access to Oracle native connection, which was impossible for me, because my connection is wrapped by C3P0, hibernate and spring.

Also I would like not to use oracle.sql.CLOB and BLOB Types \(I use org.hibernate.lob.ClobImpl and BlobImple instead\).

I've read:
\* http://www.oracle.com/technology/sample\_code/tech/java/codesnippet/jdbc/clob10g/handlingclobsinoraclejdbc10g.html 
\* http://jasonbherald.com/2008/05/27/oracle-blob-jdbc-how-to/
and write new DataTypeFactory \(see attachment\).

It uses new ClobAsStringDataType class for handling Clobs using standard jdbc ResultSet.getString and ResultSet.setString methods \(contrary to oracle custom api\).

For Blobs, uses BinaryStreamDataType.

It works great, even for clobs bigger than 1 MB.

Probably it will work with Oracle 10.x JDBC drivers.

posts.subject: #186 ClassCastException when using oracle.sql.CLOB
posts.author: slecallonnec

tickets.description:
Hi,

Recently, while using DBUnit with Oracle 10g, I attempted to insert a CLOB object into a table and received the exception:
java.lang.ClassCastException: java.lang.String cannot be cast to oracle.sql.CLOB
at oracle.jdbc.driver.OraclePreparedStatement.setObjectCritical

I have pinned this down to a change mode since version 2.2.2 in support of Derby.

The class in question is:
org.dbunit.dataset.datatype.ClobDataType.

The method in question is:
setSqlValue\(\)

I managed to work around the issue for Oracle by changing this line:
statement.setObject\(column, typeCast\(value\), getSqlType\(\)\);

to this:
statement.setObject\(column, typeCast\(value\), 
DataType.LONGVARCHAR.getSqlType\(\)\);

In the process, I had to disable some unit tests.

Logged In: YES 
user\_id=1803108
Originator: NO

I created a patch on the current trunk \(see attachment below\) that contains the following changes that imply some design simplifications \(including some API changes, so I would be happy if as many people as possible could review it\):

o JdbcDatabaseTester only accepts connection parameters in constructor -> removed setters to ensure that a usable/valid object is there after constructor invocation
o Method DatabaseTestCase.newDatabaseTester\(\) is abstract now so that implementors have to provide a IDatabaseTester
o Method DatabaseTestCase.getConnection\(\) is final now and concretely implemented in DatabaseTestCase, always returning the connection from the IDatabaseTester
o new method "DatabaseTestCase.setUpConnectionConfig\(DatabaseConfig config\)" that can be overridden in order to easily specify config features/properties

@Sebastien: could you please let me know what you think about this?
@Mike: could you please   
1\. svn checkout the trunk
2\. apply the patch \(e.g. tortoiseSVN function "apply patch"\)
3\. execute "mvn package" in the trunk folder \(maven must be installed before\) and include the new "dbunit-..-SNAPSHOT.jar" into your project
4\. simply extend DBTestCase.setUpConnectionConfig\(DatabaseConfig c\) and set your config parameters
5\. please let me know the results :-\)

Thanks and regards,
mat

tickets.summary: Simplify use of custom datatype factory for DBTestCase
tickets.description:
This comes from bug 1215495: getting DBUnit to recognize the time portion of Oracle DATE columns.  It is easy enough to do if you can set the PROPERTY\_DATATYPE\_FACTORY on the IDatabaseConnection, but to get that work with DBTestCase seems complicated \(I created a subclass of PropertiesBasedJdbcDatabaseTester, maybe there's a better way\).

So my request is to make it easier to use the DBTestCase and JdbcDatabaseTester classes when RDBMS-specific tweaks are required.

Thanks\!
Mike

posts.text:
P6Spy has a serious bug that causes it to mix up fields that have the same name, e.g. if you join two tables which both have a 'title' column, P6Spy will mix them up and the values end up switched over. 

So I can't \(and many others won't be able\) to use P6Spy to log dbunit SQL statements. 

This bug in P6Spy is in their issue tracking - I saw it earlier this year, however at this point in time, their issue tracking site has lost its domain name. I have no idea if this is just temporary or due to the fact that p6spy has not seen any activity for years. 

So I'd like to vote for this feature request to be given higher priority.

posts.text: Instead of using a separate logger, you could use an SLF4J Marker \(http://www.slf4j.org/apidocs/org/slf4j/Marker.html\) for all logged SQL statements, allowing users of Marker-aware SLF4J implementations additional flexibility in seeing what is logged \(see examples for Logback at http://logback.qos.ch/manual/filters.html, particularly http://logback.qos.ch/manual/filters.html\#TurboFilter\)

The currently used version of POI is a bit outdated \(2.5.1\). Check if upgrade is possible to latest version at 
http://repo1.maven.org/maven2/poi/poi/3.1-FINAL/

I did a quick check and found that only one test fails with the following exception:

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: The workbook already contains a sheet of this name
	at org.apache.poi.hssf.usermodel.HSSFWorkbook.createSheet\(HSSFWorkbook.java:578\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.excel.XlsDataSet.write\(XlsDataSet.java:91\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.excel.XlsDataSetTest.testDuplicateWrite\(XlsDataSetTest.java:124\)


I also noticed that the input document "trunk\src\xml\dataSetDuplicateTest.xls" is detected to be corrupt by M$ Excel because it contains two sheets with identical names \("DUPLICATE\_TABLE"\). So this error fits to the one reported by POI-3.1.

Since this worked with the old dbunit version the behaviour will change a bit - but I think that the unit test which fails with the new POI version should be changed. It should not be possible to have two tables with equal names in the same dataset/excel file.

DatabaseOperation.REFRESH does not insert non-existing row\(s\).

According to the documentation, DatabaseOperation.REFRESH should be the same as running DatabaseOperation.DELETE then DatabaseOperation.INSERT.

posts.text:
Hi there,

I suppose the reason for the exception is that the "Column.equals\(\)" method in dbunit returns false in your case which is why the exception is thrown. The reason why it returns "false" is probably because "Column.equals\(\)" compares the attributes "columnName"/"dataType"/"nullable"/"sqlTypeName" of the column and it is likely that your "expectedTable" has a different table metadata \(perhaps coming from a FlatXml file\) that the actual DB.
This validation of the Columns is new with 2.3. I recommend you to simply omit the "expected.getTableMetaData\(\)" and write the code as follows:

...
// Compare the tables after sorting them by the same column
Assertion.assertEquals\(new SortedTable\(expectedTable\), new SortedTable\(filteredActualTable\)\);
...

This should work since you already filtered out all unneeded columns through the "filteredActualTable".

By the way, the same problem/exception will occur if you use the constructor "SortedTable\(ITable table, Column\[\] columns\)" because this one also checks for "Column" equality instead of column name equality. If you want to sort by column names you can use the constructor "SortedTable\(ITable table, String\[\] columnNames\)".

Regards,
mat

posts.author: felipeal

posts.text:
Hi Matija,

first of all thanks for your feedback. Having a look at the code I must suppose that the QueryDataSet/QueryTable is actually designed to re-query the database every time it is invoked. The best thing you can do is to wrap your QueryDataSet with a CachedDataSet in order to cache all of your data once only.

Nevertheless the the behaviour of the QueryTableIterator is a bit weird to me as well. If you do not specify a query for your QueryDataSet's table \(i.e. if you use "QueryDataSet.addTable\(tableName\)" without the query param\) the method "QueryTableIterator.getTable\(\)" always recreates the whole database dataset when a single \(current\) table is requested. In my eyes this is definitely an overhead. It would be sufficient to query the table currently being iterated. I committed this change right now \(svn rev. 814/trunk\). I would be glad if you could retest this without the CachedDataSet to check if the number of db-queries has been reduced now. You can retrieve the latest jar-file from parabuild at http://parabuild.viewtier.com:8080/parabuild/build/result/30/2869/dbunit-2.3.1-SNAPSHOT.jar

Thanks in advance,
mat

tickets.summary: QueryDataSet does not cache query results
tickets.description:
I have two data sets that I want to compare to each other. The expected results are in an instance of XmlDataSet, the actual results are in an instance of QueryDataSet.

Here is the problem: Assertion.assertEquals\(IDataSet, IDataSet\) iterates over all of the expected tables, and for each such iteration, it also iterates over every table of the supplied QueryDataSet AND pulls the data out of the database for that table.

The result of this behaviour is that for my dataset, which has 15 tables \(SQL statements in the case of QueryDataSet\), those 15 queries are retrieved from the database 225 \(=15^2\) times\!

QueryDataSet's iterator retrieves the table from the database every time its element is accessed, instead of only once and then caching the result. Is this really correct behaviour? If so, then why is it necessary for the comparison method to iterate over the data set 225 times, when I only have 15 queries? 

Am I missing something obvious?

posts.subject: #199 Wrong error condition causes RowOutOfBoundsException thrown.
posts.author: jzhou_092008

posts.text:
Hi there,

I am not sure if I understood you correctly. From my point of view the scenario "currentRow == rowCount" must always be invalid since the "currentRow" count starts at index zero. I also think that it has been invalid in older dbunit releases. The fix for 1712122 \(http://sourceforge.net/tracker/index.php?func=detail&aid=1712122&group\_id=47439&atid=449491\) did not actually change the condition when the exception is thrown but only the exception text.

Just a quick example to show my understand of this \(whether wrong or not\) where the first column shows the Excel "rowIndex".

Excel-Sheet:
0 | MY\_COL1 | MY\_COL2
1 | 12345   | valueX 
2 | 23456   | valueY 

so the rowCount here is 2 \(for excel-sheets the header is handled as meta-information row being ignored when accessing to data via dbunit\).

Valid row indexes for dbunit are: 0 \(mapped to excel-row-index 1\) and 1 \(mapped to excel-row-index 2\).

Please correct me if I am wrong.

Best for all of us would be if you could provide a small test case \(just attach it to this tracker item\) so that we can reproduce it and ensure that we are talking about the same thing ;-\)

Thanks,
mat

posts.text:
If I did not get it wrong you are doing the following in your testcase2:

Excel-Sheet:
0 | MY\_COL1 | MY\_COL2
1 | 12345   | valueX 

You now want to do a 

int rowIndex = 1;
Object value = t.getValue\(rowIndex, "MY\_COL1"\);

which obviously cannot work because the table has only one data row which is at rowIndex=0. The first row with the column names will always be ignored.

I think best would be if you could provide a JUnit test for this so that we do talk about the same thing.

tickets.summary: Wrong error condition causes RowOutOfBoundsException thrown.
tickets.description:
The condition in the method org.dbunit.dataset.AbstractTable\#assertValidRowIndex\(int row, int rowCount\) which excludes "row == rowCount" as valid scenario is wrong - consider spreadsheet with total rows of two where the second row is a data row, because the row number indexes from zero and the row number for second row is "1"; the value of the rowCount is calculated by calling getRowCount\(\) which returns \_sheet.getLastRowNum\(\) as "1", in this case "row == rowCount" is valid and should not throw an RowOutOfBoundsException.

If everyone agrees on the above fix, the fix for Artifact ID "1712122" needs to be reversed - the error message passed to RowOutOfBoundsException is originally correct.
