regards,
mat

posts.text:
I did the following work on this which is committed in svn rev. 815/trunk:

=> the AmbiguousTableNameException is thrown by the new class "OrderedTableNameMap" which also handles case sensitivity.
=> it is now not possible anymore to create a FlatDtdDataSet/FlatXmlDataSet/DatabaseDataSet/... with two tables having equal names \(fail-fast implemented by "OrderedTableNameMap" class\) - in older versions this was possible and the AmbiguousTableNameException was thrown in "getTable\(\)" invocation on IDataSet
=> removed some of the "testXxxDuplicateTablesXxx\(\)" tests \(e.g. "testDuplicateWrite" from XxxDataSetTest\) since "duplicate-table DataSets cannot be created anymore - central location where this is tested is in AbstractDataSetTest and its implementors during the creation of a DataSet.
posts.subject: #97 Fail-Fast if AmbiguousTableNames found
posts.author: gommma

posts.text: - **status**: open --> closed-fixed
posts.subject: #97 Fail-Fast if AmbiguousTableNames found
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
As already discussed on the developers list \(around 20. Sept 2008\) it would be nice to have a fail-fast mechanism which throws the AmbiguousTableNameException immediately during the construction of an IDataSet and not only in the invocation of the "IDataSet.getTable\(String tabName\)".
So if you have an IDataSet instance you can always be sure that no duplicate table names are inside.

This is also a preparation for \#1214252 which is about case-sensitive table names \(as they were until 1.5\).

When an export under xml file was make \(for is from postgreSQL, but the trouble is the same under mysql database\), if a field contain no data, is not write in the line of the record.
For example, if you have table "USERS" whith 4 field : "ID","LOGIN","PASS","EMAIL" and the fiel email is empty, the  line write is :
<users id=3 login=deazdz pass=dakdzo>
For this, is not trouble, but id after this line under the xml file you have an other record like this
<users id=4 login=daedze pass=dezpdkz email=dae@dze.de>
the field "email" is not insert during the dataset step.
A temporaly solution is to write the record with email before the other record.
But i think, it could be have a solution under the developement of dbunit in the next version.

posts.text:
I see. By default the "Compare" task uses the flat XML format. In the sources I saw that the Compare-task does not implement the XLS format at all. So unfortunately we have to concentrate on your first example. What is your problem there? I copied your example and tried it out against oracle and it works fine for me - after some adaptions \(namely writing a correct db query\). I am working on the current trunk. One problem that already occurred with the 2.3 release is that the SortedTable threw an Exception when the wrong metadata has been passed in \(column validation failure has been reported\). To workaround this use the single-argument constructor 

new SortedTable\(filteredTable\)

instead of 

new SortedTable\(filteredTable,expectedTable.getTableMetaData\(\)\)

The reason for this is described in \#2119648.

Regards,
mat

One thing you could do is to create a "normal" JUnit test which contains the dbunit assertions \(for example code you wrote below\) and then invoke it as a simple JUnit test case. Another option would be to use the dbunit maven plugin \(http://maven-plugins.sourceforge.net/maven-dbunit-plugin/index.html\) - I do not know how it works but I would expect that maven generates a report for the tests.
If you are willing to contribute an extension that allows the generation of a report from an ant-task I would be fond of putting it into the dbunit repository for a future release. And thanks a lot for your motivating sidenode :-\)

mat

tickets.summary: Additional column info also for filtered columns
tickets.description:
With respect to \#1966517 \(additional column info on failure\) another useful feature popped up. I compare tables which have a surrogate key. The surrogate key is filtered out in the assertions. If a failure occurs it would be helpful to print out the value of this surrogate key so that the failed db-row can be identified by this key. Since the key column is filtered out in the table-metadata it cannot be displayed when passing it to the 

Assertion.assertEquals\(ITable expectedTable, ITable actualTable, Column\[\] additionalColumnInfo\)

method. It should be extended so that filtered columns can also be displayed.

posts.subject: #102 Generated DTD should use #IMPLIED if a column has a default
posts.author: gommma

tickets.summary: Generated DTD should use #IMPLIED if a column has a default
tickets.description:
Most of the tables in our \(Sybase\) database include a couple of audit columns, Last\_Updated\_At & Last\_Updated\_By, which store the timestamp and user's login ID when rows are inserted or updated.  Because these are NOT NULL columns, the generated DTD had CDATA \#REQUIRED for all these attributes.  However, the columns have defaults bound to them \(using getdate\(\) and suser\_name\(\) respectively\) so values don't need to be supplied for these when inserting rows and \#IMPLIED would therefore be more appropriate.
The DatabaseMetaData.getColumns\(\) javadocs include "COLUMN\_DEF String => default value \(may be null\)" in the returned column description, which I'm guessing could be used to determine this?

posts.text:
I updated to apache poi 3.2 in revision 875/trunk. Therefore I also changed the code so that Date objects are stored as numbers to XLS sheets as well as Date values read from an XLS sheet are provided as numbers in dbunit ITable objects. This was necessary since POI's handling of Date objects is not consistent. I.e. writing a java.util.Date and afterwards reading it again via HSSFCell\#getDateCellValue\(\) provides non-equal Date objects. If this leads to any kind of problems please let me know.

Regards,

posts.subject: #108 Bundle required slf4j components with dbunit
posts.author: rlogiacco

posts.text:
Hi there,

please have a look at \#1798149 \( https://sourceforge.net/tracker/index.php?func=detail&aid=1798149&group\_id=47439&atid=449494 \).
It has been a strategic decision for dbunit to switch from JCL to slf4j \(so we have been using JCL before\). For a detailed description on the usage of slf4j see www.slf4j.org. It is a very good alternative to commons-logging IMO.
So from my side -1 for this.

The second thing with the bundling is an option which should be discussed on the developer's list \(licensing questions and so on\). Please feel free to start a discussion about this. I think it would be great for users to download a single zip/tar file which includes all jars \(mandatory/optional\) which dbunit uses. With respect to the dependencies you currently need to have a look at http://www.dbunit.org/dependencies.html and download the necessary jars from the corresponding project site \(or simply get them via maven\).

Regards,
mat

posts.author: sf-robot

tickets.summary: Bundle required slf4j components with dbunit
tickets.description:
Per the DBUnit Wiki: "As of version 2.2.1, DbUnit uses the SLF4J library for logging purposes \(See dependencies\). This allows DbUnit users to use their favourite logging library \(log4j, JCL, etc.\).

When using DbUnit, you must therefore download SLF4J and include slf4j-api-\*.jar and the implementation library of your choice in the classpath: for example, slf4j-log4j12-\*.jar for Log4J, or slf4j-jcl-\*.jar for commons-logging. However, make sure there is only one implementation library in your classpath, as failing to do so might cause some issues."

It seems like the more user friendly route would be to pick  logging library \(I vote for commons logging\) and then provide instructions on how to adapt for others.  Alternatively, maybe bundle all of slf4j and place somewhere outside of the classpath.  This would make it easier for users to switch to a different logging library since all the needed slf4j jars would be distributed with DBUnit.

posts.subject: #208 Enable sensing not working (FlatXmlDataSet)
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
When enabling sensing \(see http://www.dbunit.org/faq.html\#differentcolumnnumber\) the dataset is not correctly read from the xml file. Rows read from a table also occur in the set of rows of the following table in the dataset.

I could fix this problem for my use case by modifying org.dbunit.dataset.stream.BufferedConsumer. In the function "endTable\(\)" the \_allRows variable wasn't cleared. So, I added

this.\_allRows.clear\(\);

at the end of this method.

posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Currently the default setting is that no escape pattern is used at all. I think this should stay as it is to keep backwards compatilbility \(and to keep the default behaviour as simple as possible\).
Nevertheless I agree with you and I would also intuitively set the escape pattern \(or maybe escape character?\) to simply "\"". So I committed a little improvement which checks if the given escapePattern has the length of 1 and if so it is used to surround the table name on the left and right. \(committed to rev. 906/trunk for upcoming 2.4.2\).

To everyone: please let me know if you have a veto for this change.

posts.author: gommma

tickets.summary: Cannot extract contents of table with a column named "AS"
tickets.description:
The following code:

IDataSet dataSet = conn.createDataSet\("MYTABLE"\);
FlatXmlDataSet.write\(dataSet, xmlOut, "UTF-8"\);

Causes the following exception:

org.dbunit.dataset.DataSetException: java.sql.SQLException: ORA-00936: missing expression
	at org.dbunit.database.DatabaseDataSet.getTable\(DatabaseDataSet.java:284\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.filter.SequenceTableIterator.getTable\(SequenceTableIterator.java:77\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.stream.DataSetProducerAdapter.produce\(DataSetProducerAdapter.java:83\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.xml.FlatXmlWriter.write\(FlatXmlWriter.java:124\)
	at org.dbunit.dataset.xml.FlatXmlDataSet.write\(FlatXmlDataSet.java:317\)

The MYTABLE table contains columns called AA, AB...AZ, including a column called AS.  I believe this is confusing the SQL parser.  I looked in the code and believe I found the problem in org.dbunit.database.AbstractResultSetTable line 89:

String selectStatement = getSelectStatement\(schema, metaData, escapePattern\);

The value of "selectStatement" for MYTABLE is:

select AA, AB, AC, AD, AE, AF, AG, AH, AI, AJ, AK, AL, AM, AN, AO, AP, AQ, AR, AS, AT, AU, AV, AW, AX, AY, AZ from MYSCHEMA.MYTABLE

... which causes the same error as above when I execute it directly using SQL Developer.  By enclosing each column name, the schema name, and the table name in quotes, like this:

select "AA", "AB", "AC", "AD", "AE", "AF", "AG", "AH", "AI", "AJ", "AK", "AL", "AM", "AN", "AO", "AP", "AQ", "AR", "AS", "AT", "AU", "AV", "AW", "AX", "AY", "AZ" from "MYSCHEMA"."MYTABLE"

... the error is eliminated.  More generally, every time a column name, schema name, or table name is referenced in a SQL query, it should be surrounded in double quotes.

Version: 2.4.0
Environment: Windows XP, JDK 1.4.2\_14 SUN 32-bit, Oracle 10g database \(10.2.0.2.0\) running on linux, OCI driver

tickets.summary: Cannot insert table data with self-reference in 2.4.0
tickets.description:
I tried to implement a database schema of employees. An employee can have a supervisor. The supervisor also an employee so there is a reference from a row in the employees table to another row in employees table.

How to reproduce:
\* Unpack the files in attached zip file to a directory.
\* Mysql test: Change Mysql username and password. Create the database "test"
\* H2 test: It may be necessary to manually download the H2 1.0.79-jar file and deploy in your local maven repository
\* Run "mvn test"

Actual:
Both the Mysql and H2 test fails \(but a bit differently\)

In the Mysql case no data is inserted what so ever. An exception is raised:
org.dbunit.dataset.NoSuchColumnException: EMPLOYEES.SSN -  \(Non-uppercase input column: ssn\) in ColumnNameToIndexes cache map. Note that the map's column names are NOT case sensitive.
	at org.dbunit.dataset.AbstractTableMetaData.getColumnIndex\(AbstractTableMetaData.java:128\)
	at org.dbunit.operation.AbstractOperation.getOperationMetaData\(AbstractOperation.java:89\)
	at org.dbunit.operation.AbstractBatchOperation.execute\(AbstractBatchOperation.java:140\)
	at org.dbunit.operation.CompositeOperation.execute\(CompositeOperation.java:79\)
	at se.lesc.dbunit\_ref\_insert\_problem.InsertMysqlTest.testInsertRef\(InsertMysqlTest.java:61\)

In the H2 case only 2 rows are inserted

Expected:
It should be possible to populate the database even if there is a self-reference in the table.

Source code snippets:
String createEmployeesTableSql =
"CREATE TABLE employees \(" +
"    ssn varchar\(255\) NOT NULL, " +
"    firstName varchar\(255\) default NULL, " +
"    initial varchar\(255\) default NULL, " +
"    lastName varchar\(255\) default NULL, " +
"    salary bigint\(20\) default NULL, " +
"    supervisor varchar\(255\) default NULL, " +
"    PRIMARY KEY  \(ssn\), " +
"    FOREIGN KEY \(supervisor\) REFERENCES employees \(ssn\)" +
"\)";


<dataset>
<employees ssn="730403" firstname="Niklas" lastname="Berglind" />
<employees ssn="720202" firstname="Bjorn" lastname="Julander" />
<employees ssn="710101" firstname="Petter" lastname="Edblom" />

<employees ssn="790630" firstname="Lennart" lastname="Schedin" supervisor="730403" />
<employees ssn="760503" firstname="Johan" lastname="Fransson" supervisor="790630" />
</dataset>


DatabaseConnection iDatabaseConnection = new DatabaseConnection\(connection\);
IDataSet dataSet = new FlatXmlDataSet\(getClass\(\).getResource\("/employees.xml"\), false, true\);
DatabaseOperation.CLEAN\_INSERT.execute\(iDatabaseConnection, dataSet\);



Reproducible in: Dbunit 2.4.0 \(Windows XP\)
Not reproducible in: Dbunit 2.3.0 \(Windows XP\)

posts.subject: #213 FEATURE_CASE_SENSITIVE_TABLE_NAMES doesn't work completely
posts.author: j_zimmermann

posts.text: I found 2 copy/paste mistakes that I made using the wrong feature...I fixed them and got the thing running on my oracle \(note that I also had to set an escape pattern on the DatabaseConfig\). The latest build should now also work for you. Sorry again for the inconvenience...
posts.subject: #213 FEATURE_CASE_SENSITIVE_TABLE_NAMES doesn't work completely
posts.author: gommma

posts.text: I re-tested my stuff using revision 913: everything works fine. Thanks a lot\!
posts.subject: #213 FEATURE_CASE_SENSITIVE_TABLE_NAMES doesn't work completely
posts.author: j_zimmermann

tickets.description:
My code which was working fine with DbUnit 2.3 and PostgreSQL 8.3.5. Upgrading to DbUnit 2.4.1 fails due to case sensitive problems.

According to Bug-ID 2382766 I downloaded http://parabuild.viewtier.com:8080/parabuild/build/result/30/3187/dbunit-2.4.1.jar and extended my code as follows:

Connection jdbcConn = DriverManager.getConnection\(URL, USERNAME, PASSWORD\);
IDatabaseConnection dbunitConn = new DatabaseConnection\(jdbcConn, SCHEMA\);
		
DatabaseConfig config = dbunitConn.getConfig\(\);  // new
config.setFeature\(DatabaseConfig.FEATURE\_CASE\_SENSITIVE\_TABLE\_NAMES, true\); // new
		
InputStream inputStream = new BufferedInputStream\(new FileInputStream\(XML\_FLAT\_DATASET\)\);
IDataSet xmlDataset = new FlatXmlDataSet\(inputStream\);
		
CLEAN\_INSERT.execute\(dbunitConn, xmlDataset\);

The flat xml file contains the correct table names \(being case sensitive\) and looks as follows:
<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>
<\!DOCTYPE dataset SYSTEM "db.dtd">
<dataset>
...
<bestellung\_lieferung bestellung\_fk="251" lieferung\_fk="71"/>
...
</dataset>

Here is the resulting stacktrace when running the Java code above:
org.dbunit.dataset.NoSuchTableException: Did not find table 'BESTELLUNG\_LIEFERUNG' in schema 'hskajboss'
	at org.dbunit.database.DatabaseTableMetaData.<init>\(DatabaseTableMetaData.java:126\)
	at org.dbunit.database.DatabaseDataSet.getTableMetaData\(DatabaseDataSet.java:267\)
	at org.dbunit.operation.DeleteAllOperation.execute\(DeleteAllOperation.java:109\)
	at org.dbunit.operation.CompositeOperation.execute\(CompositeOperation.java:79\)
	at de.hska.test.util.DbUtil.reload\(DbUtil.java:189\)
	at de.hska.test.util.DeployUtil.redeploy\(DeployUtil.java:91\)
	at de.hska.test.KundenverwaltungTest.setupLoginAndProxy\(KundenverwaltungTest.java:107\)
	at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0\(Native Method\)
	at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke\(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:39\)
	at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke\(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:25\)

posts.text:
Hi there,

the change in the XLS handling has been introduced when updating to apache POI-3.2. The dbunit tests had problems with restoring the original Date values when writing and re-reading an XlsDataSet. So I changed the handling to store and provide XLS Date values as Java BigDecimals. You can temporarily fix the behaviour for your use case by overwriting the method "XlsTable.getDateValue" to return a java.util.Date instead of a BigDecimal.

I would welcome very much if you could make a proposal for fixing this annoying issue. As you can see in the XlsTable.getDateValue and XlsDataSetWriter.setDateCell I tried several possibilities - none of them succeeded unfortunately \(i.e. having all unit tests green\)...

regards,
mat

posts.subject: #219 problem when verifying against an XmlDataSet
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Hi Erik,

I am now able to reproduce the problem. So thanks a lot for your new testcase.

I debugged through this and found that something weird is happening in the maven-call which 
is not happening when executing the test within eclipse's junit runner. The "AbstractDataSet\#getTableNames\(\)" is
invoked before the XML actually has been parsed into the CachedDataSet. So the "AbstractDataSet\#\_orderedTableNameMap"
is initialized emptily and all later invocations will use this lazily initialized "\_orderedTableNameMap" \(this is the reason why it is always empty in your case\).
After some more debugging I found out that the "AbstractDataSet\#toString\(\)" method invokes the "AbstractDataSet\#getTableNames\(\)" which
was responsible for the premature initialization of the "\_orderedTableNameMap". This happened only when invoked via maven invoked because \(in my case at least\) a logger was configured with the level DEBUG.

I created item \#2475264 in the tracker for handling this bug \(I took the freedom to copy/paste our mail correspondence there\).
I already committed the bugfix in to SVN revision 930/trunk. Please feel free to build it on your own or to retrieve the latest snapshot build from parabuild \( http://parabuild.viewtier.com:8080/parabuild/build/results.htm?buildrunid=11264 \).

Best regards,
Matthias

tickets.summary: Doesn't load table correctly when different columns included
tickets.description:
I have a table that I am trying to load with data that includes different columns.  It appears that dbUnit gets confused and tries to load the data using previously used column names.

The data for table t\_setting in the snapshot is as follows:

<t\_setting hid="162" id="RssUrl" source="2" fk\_to\_settingdefinition="157" fk\_to\_widgetdef="159" setting\_position="1"/>
<t\_setting hid="218" id="scan1\_Preview" fk\_to\_settingdefinition="110" fk\_to\_task="217"/>

You'll see that the first row sets data in fk\_to\_widgetdef - the 2nd in fk\_to\_task.

However, the sql generated \(from P6Spy\) is:

1231189497656|31|1|statement|insert into t\_setting \(hid, id, source, fk\_to\_settingdefinition, fk\_to\_widgetdef, setting\_position\) values \(?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?\)|insert into t\_setting \(hid, id, source, fk\_to\_settingdefinition, fk\_to\_widgetdef, setting\_position\) values \(162, 'RssUrl', 2, 157, 159, 1\)
1231189497656|0|1|statement|insert into t\_setting \(hid, id, source, fk\_to\_settingdefinition, fk\_to\_widgetdef, setting\_position\) values \(?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?\)|insert into t\_setting \(hid, id, source, fk\_to\_settingdefinition, fk\_to\_widgetdef, setting\_position\) values \(218, 'scan1\_Preview', '', 110, '', ''\)

dbUnit does not try to insert into fk\_to\_task in the 2nd statement, but rather into fk\_to\_widgetdef again.

cheers,

David

NOTE: this is against version 2.1.  I tries to upgrade to latest version, but couldn't get it to run with maven.

posts.text:
Some additional information:

1\) The change in behavior was introduced between 2.3.0 and 2.4.0.
2\) This specific error only seems to happen with MySQL under Windows.
3\) Interestingly, both of the tests in the aforementioned sample project fail when run against MySQL running under Linux.  Note that both tests pass regardless of db server platform with 2.3.0.

Hi Christian,

First of all thanks a lot for your great bug report\! I wished everybody provided such good work\!
I investigated this and I currently think that you made use of a corner case - the iterator \(internally used for your use case\) worked in 2.3 because no duplicate check was performed. This changed with 2.4.

Did you try to invoke flatXmlDataSet.getTable\("user\_roles"\) on your DataSet? I'm quite sure that it throws an AmbiguousTableNameException.

This needs to be discussed on the developer forums. I think the following question should be looked at:
If the same table occurs twice having any different tables inbetween, should it be added to the Java-Object representing the first table or should a new ITable object be created every time \(as it was in your "corner"-case\)?

Best regards,
mat

posts.author: jhattat

posts.text:
Hi there,

the default behaviour is to ignore case which is why every tablename is the "toUpperCase\(\)"ed in dbunit. You can probably fix your problem by setting the feature "http://www.dbunit.org/features/caseSensitiveTableNames" to "true" on the DatabaseConfig or by invoking a constructor having set the "caseSensitiveTableNames=true" \(for example "public DatabaseDataSet\(IDatabaseConnection connection, boolean caseSensitiveTableNames\)"\).

Regards,
matthias

posts.text:
I think I'm running into the same problem.  When MySQL is running on a Windows box, everything works fine.  When MySQL runs on a Linux box, NoSuchTableException is thrown for every table reference because DBUnit is looking for capitalized table names.

This \(broken\) behavior happens consistently with versions \[2.4.0, 2.4.3\].  v2.3.0 \(and all previous versions\) work correctly with respect to table name capitalization.

I tried the FEATURE\_CASE\_SENSITIVE\_TABLE\_NAMES option but it didn't seem to have any effect.  I supplied both the literal true and the string "true", neither changed the outcome.

As Matthias already knows, I'm an advocate for retaining previous behavior, especially when that behavior worked out of the box without requiring additional configuration.

I've updated my previous test application to reflect this problem.  It can be found at: http://svn.carbonfive.com/public/christian/dbunit-mysql-issue/trunk/
posts.subject: #224 Mysql Linux metatdata UpperCase Comparison
posts.author: xianpants

posts.text:
Hi Christian,

sorry for the long delay. 
I think you made one mistake in your testcase: the caseSensitiveTableNames is a feature and not a property \(and features are always primitive booleans\). So please try the following and let me know if it works:

tester.getConnection\(\).getConfig\(\).setFeature\(DatabaseConfig.FEATURE\_CASE\_SENSITIVE\_TABLE\_NAMES, true\);

nevertheless I will investigate this in detail.

Regards,
matthias
posts.subject: #224 Mysql Linux metatdata UpperCase Comparison
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Matthias,

You're right... that did the trick.

Personally, I'd like to see it work out the box once again.  While I think it's a good idea in general, I'm also a little selfish because I have a open source test support project that uses dbunit \(http://blog.carbonfive.com/2008/07/testing/database-testing-with-spring-25-and-dbunit\) and supports a variety of db types.  The previous functionality seemed to work out of the box across the supported db types across platforms.  While I can fix MySQL on Linux by specifying this feature, it's better when it just works.

What instigated the change with 2.4 that altered the previous behavior?

Christian

posts.text:
Hi there,

I committed the bugfix \(SVN rev. 953/trunk\).

I introduced a new property named DatabaseConfig.PROPERTY\_METADATA\_HANDLER. You can use the "MySqlConnection" or configure it manually using the "DatabaseConfig.setProperty\(DatabaseConfig.PROPERTY\_METADATA\_HANDLER, new MySqlMetadataHandler\(\)\)".

Could you please test it and let me know if it now works for you? \(latest build results are on parabuild server at http://parabuild.viewtier.com:8080/parabuild/index.htm?view=detailed&buildid=30 \)

Best regards
matthias

tickets.description:
With 2.4.3, when Using the FEATURE\_QUALIFIED\_TABLE\_NAMES, the next warning appears :
WARN  DatabaseTableMetaData - No columns found for table 'XXX' that are supported by dbunit. Will return an empty column list

The DatabaseTableMetaData.getColumns\(\) is called and uses
ResultSet resultSet = databaseMetaData.getColumns\(null, schemaName, tableName, "%"\);

This method retrieve the metatda, but, as mysql don't manage schema but catalog, the resultset schema and catalog are null, so the 
SQLHelper.matches return false\!

Hi,

I have created a "performance pack" which provides:
\- a FastInsertOperation, which is fairly quick for larger datasets.
\- a CachedDatabaseOperation, which records the batched statements in an operation, and can replay them on a different connection - useful for repetitive data loading for unit tests.

Please apply dbunit's standard software license \(whatever that is\) to the attached code. I don't have the time to join this project as a developer, nor do I have the time to write test cases for what I have written... but you are welcome to use it :-\)

Cheers,
Anton.

posts.text:
Hi Armand,

could you please provide a testcase that lets me reproduce your failure? If you have a look at "DBTestCase\#getConnection\(\)" you can see that the "DatabaseTestCase.setUpDatabaseConfig\(DatabaseConfig config\)" is also invoked there. Also if you look into the DBTestCaseTest you can see that this functionality is tested and works. The only thing I can imagine that might cause your problem is the inconsistent behavior of the DatabaseTester implementations \(the DefaultDatabaseTester\#getConnection\(\) returns always the same connection whereas the PropertiesBasedDatabaseTester\#getConnection\(\) always provides a new Connection\)

Regards,
matthias

- **status**: open --> closed-invalid
posts.subject: #234 Oracle 10g  and AmbiguousTableNameException
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
I got this exception while running tests:

BIN$YZK/P05ONR/GQAB/AQBGPQ==$0  
org.dbunit.database.AmbiguousTableNameException: BIN$YZK/P05ONR/GQAB/AQBGPQ==$0 
at org.dbunit.dataset.OrderedTableNameMap.add\(OrderedTableNameMap.java:198\) 
at org.dbunit.database.DatabaseDataSet.initialize\(DatabaseDataSet.java:225\) 
at org.dbunit.database.DatabaseDataSet.getTableMetaData\(DatabaseDataSet.java:273\) 
at org.dbunit.operation.DeleteAllOperation.execute\(DeleteAllOperation.java:109\) 
at org.dbunit.operation.CompositeOperation.execute\(CompositeOperation.java:79\) 
at org.dbunit.operation.TransactionOperation.execute\(TransactionOperation.java:78\) 
at org.dbunit.AbstractDatabaseTester.executeOperation\(AbstractDatabaseTester.java:166\) 
at org.dbunit.AbstractDatabaseTester.onSetup\(AbstractDatabaseTester.java:87\) 
at org.dbunit.DatabaseTestCase.setUp\(DatabaseTestCase.java:151\) 
at my.stuff.MyTest.setUp\(MyTest.java:40\) 
at junit.framework.TestSuite.runTest\(TestSuite.java:232\) 

Reason for this is following:

Oracle keeps backups of deleted table schemas. They are basically tables with random name like this: BIN$YZK/P05ONR/gQaB/AqBGpq

In my case oracle created two tables with otherwise identical names, but case of letters was different. \(ie. BIN$YZK/P05ONR/gQaB/AqBGpq and BIN$YZK/P05oNR/gQaB/AqBGpq\). 

When DbUnit is checking for ambiguous table name, it first uppercases table names which will cause this AmbiguousTableName exception.

See http://www.orafaq.com/node/968 for details about oracle's table schema backup.

posts.subject: #120 Support schema and table create / drop
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
A separate module, that's an idea.  I was actually thinking of introducing something like the Hibernate Dialects into dbunit \(part of it's already there in the form of data types, but there's no SQL for specific databases\) and determining the database at runtime off of the metadata \(and ansi compliance if it's an unsupported dialect, at least we can do ansi sql, or throw an exception if neither of those two work\) from the JDBC Connection.  I'd add four new operations \(create schema, create table, drop schema, and drop table\).  The hard part would be either modifying the current XML format or creating a new format for the DDL generation.  If it were a module, would it be a good place to store it within dbunit \(maybe under a contrib, extras, modules folder\) or a new project?

I completely agree about staying backwards compatible, and I don't want to introduce new methods into interfaces or base classes that would mess this up.  Fortunately it looks like the code coverage for the tests is pretty good so they should catch the bulk of things if this were to introduce a change that would affect compatibility.  I saw Jailer, but didn't really look into it much.
posts.subject: #120 Support schema and table create / drop
posts.author: lightguard

posts.text:
At this point in time a separate module would mean to just create a new subpackage, e.g. org.dbunit.ddl.

We are currently discussing the migration of the dbunit-maven-plugin into dbunit \(see \#2215029\) which would then allow us to define modules as separate maven projects with an own pom.xml. In my eyes this would be the cleanest solution. But this is currently future sound...

For your implementation you may also consider to use the dbunit DTD \(or perhaps an XML schema, but this is not supported yet by dbunit\) to retrieve the information you need for the DDL. It becomes a bit more difficult if somebody wants to use the Excel XLS format to create a DDL from...

tickets.summary: specify the schema via the api
tickets.description:
I'm using DataSourceDatabaseTester and I'm getting the following warning:

2009-03-10 15:24:17.233 PDT,  WARN: org.dbunit.AbstractDatabaseTester.setSchema.123: setSchema\(\) should not be used anymore

The api docs for org.dbunit.IDatabaseTester say for setSchema\(\): "Deprecated. since 2.4.3 Should not be used anymore. Every concrete IDatabaseTester implementation that needs a schema has the possibility to set it somehow in the constructor".

Of the 5 concrete classes that implement IDatabaseTester; DataSourceDatabaseTester, DefaultDatabaseTester, JdbcDatabaseTester, JndiDatabaseTester, PropertiesBasedJdbcDatabaseTester, only JdbcDatabaseTester provides a constructor that allows you to specify the schema, and PropertiesBasedJdbcDatabaseTester provides a way to set the schema via a system property.

tickets.description:
I have a table with several fields - ..., pin2, puk2, ... - and when I load the xml using

IDataSet iDataSet = new FlatXmlDataSet\(new File\("data.xml"\)\);

the returned dataset has the second field ok but the first has it's initial part overritten with the contents of the second. It only happend with this fields and if I change the order the currupted field is always the first appearing in the xml. The size of the corrupted field does not change

Example - source
...
<info fase="6" pin2="PIN2 x" puk2="PUK2 yaya" />
...

loaded data
...
<info fase="6" pin2="PUK2 y" puk2="PUK2 yaya" />
...

posts.text:
Hi Sebastian,

I see two options for implementing this:

\- check in setUp/tearDown if the DatabaseOperation.NONE is set and skip if so
\- only once invoke the "setDataSet" method when the databaseTester is created \(in getDatabaseTester method\) and then in the setUp/tearDown only invoke the "tester.onSetUp"/"tester.onTearDown" methods

I implemented both versions and at least one unit test failed afterwards...do you have another idea on how to implement this? You could also simple override the setUp/tearDown methods for your special test case where the dataset creation impacts performance that much.

regards,
matthias
posts.subject: #244 DatabaseTestCase calls getDataSet() in tearDown
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
Hi Matthias\!

Thanks for the advice. I've overwritten tearDown in all my testcases to do nothing.
Interestingly this did not improve the performance at all - thats maybe because we are already caching our XmlDataSet and just provide a copy of it using new CachedDataSet\(ourCachedXmlDataSet\).

Seems as if we've already done everything to boost setUp / tearDown performance - so not calling getDataSet\(\) in tearDown does not help in our case. Nevertheless it could be helpful if the dataset was not cached.

Thanks again,
Sebastian
posts.subject: #244 DatabaseTestCase calls getDataSet() in tearDown
posts.author: sebidietrich

posts.text:
Hi Sebastian,

I agree with you with respect to the creation overhead of the DataSets but I think backwards compatibility is more important in this case. So we will leave it up to the user to cache DataSets wherever it makes sense.

I will close this issue for now. BTW, if you find a place where dbunit suffers from a bad performance please let me know. I'm always interested in improving performance, provided that backwards compatibility isn't broken.

regards,
matthias

This is a performance problem for me since getDataSet\(\) takes a while \(since we need a lot of test-data to fetch from an XML file\). And with getTearDownOperation set to NONE it is not necessary

posts.subject: #246 DefaultDataSet is incompatible w/ AbstractDataSet's IDataSet
posts.author: gommma

tickets.summary: DefaultDataSet is incompatible w/ AbstractDataSet's IDataSet
tickets.description:
DefaultDataSet's implementation is incompatible w/ AbstractDataSet's implementation of getTable, getTables, and getTableName methods.

AbstractDataSet uses "private OrderedTableNameMap \_orderedTableNameMap;" for table management.

DefaultDataSet uses "private final OrderedTableNameMap \_tableMap;" for
table management.

That works fine so long as DefaultDataSet overrides every method of
AbstractDataSet, but it fails to override the getTable\(String\),
getTables\(\), and getTableNames\(\) methods, which is half of the
IDataSet interface.

What this means is that if you construct a DefaultDataSet using the
DefaultDataSet methods, your constructed data set is stored in
\_tableMap, but when you then refetch those values using the getters,
you're fetching out of the empty  \_orderedTableNameMap.

Ie, the following method fails because the previously-added tables are
not found, yet when we try to re-add the tables, we get an exception to that
effect.

public static DefaultTable getOrCreateTable\(DefaultDataSet
aDataSet, String tableName\) throws DataSetException,
FileNotFoundException, IOException
\{
DefaultTable table = null;
try
\{
table = \(DefaultTable\)aDataSet.getTable\(tableName\);
\}
catch \(NoSuchTableException e\)
\{
ITableMetaData userTableMetaData =
BaseDBunit.getMetaData\(\).getTableMetaData\(tableName\);
table = new DefaultTable\(userTableMetaData, new ArrayList\(\)\);
aDataSet.addTable\(table\);
\}

return table;
\}

The solution would be to either override every method of
AbstractDataSet or to make  \_orderedTableNameMap protected and allow
subclasses to use it.  Ie, replace  \_tableMap in DefaultDataSet with
\_orderedTableNameMap.

I've created a patch to fix this that passes all 2.4.4 unit tests.  I have not created a unit test that demonstrates the problem, however.

I have changed AbstractDataSet's \_orderedTableNameMap. to protected.
I have replaced references with \_tableMap with \_orderedTableNameMap.
I have called initialize\(\) at the appropriate places, and provided a dumbed-down implementation of initialize \(since the one in AbstractDataSet does work that is unnecessary for DefaultDataSet\).

posts.subject: #248 Postgresql and schema
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
I have different schemas on Postgresql 8.3 using the latest dbunit-2.4.5. I cannot delete table entries by DatabaseOperation.DELETE\_ALL.execute\(...\)

IDatabaseConnection contains the schema \(in this case BI, but it does taken into account\)

The problem is:
In the DatabaseOperation.DELETE\_ALL

a\) I put in a table with name: schema.tablename: BI.APP\_ROLE\_ALL\_CHILDREN
->  org.dbunit.dataset.NoSuchTableException: BI.APP\_ROLE\_ALL\_CHILDREN
-> Table 'BI.APP\_ROLE\_ALL\_CHILDREN' not found in tableMap==org.dbunit.dataset.OrderedTableNameMap\[\_tableNames=\[app\_role\_all\_children, ...\]

So the check does not take the schema into account.

b\) I put in a table without a schema: tablename: APP\_ROLE\_ALL\_CHILDREN
-> postgres likes to execute this sql: select \* from APP\_ROLE\_ALL\_CHILDREN without the schema which throws an:
org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: Error Relation »app\_role\_all\_children« does not exist

Which is right as well, because the sql should be: select \* from BI.APP\_ROLE\_ALL\_CHILDREN

So both ways don't work. I expected a behavior that takes the given schema in the IDatabaseConnection into account so that I can put in the \(same\) schema or leave it away. None of these both cases above should generate an exception.

+1 Vote

Dbunit is a nice, powerful tool, but it is difficult to learn with quirks like that.

tickets.description:
Hello.

I found that around dbunit 2.1/2.2 Ant's Export task was switched from ForwardOnlyResultSetTableFactory to CachedResultSetTableFactory factory. That decision leaded to impossibility export databases that exceed amount of JVM Heap size \(i.e. -Xmx option\) and natively limited by overall RAM size. 
It will be nice if you can restore the functionality of version 2.1 and prior. 

I try to do a patch by myself \(i.e. org.dbunit.ant.getExportDataSet. IDataSet dataset = getDatabaseDataSet\(connection, this.\_tables, true\); true - means use ForwardOnlyResultSetTableFactory instead of CachedResultSetTableFactory. It works nice with "plain" table export, but leads to NPE on Export + Query and fails some tests as well.

Hope the feature will be useful for other users as well.

Thanks.

posts.text:

please try to create your flat XML ds as follows:

new FlatXmlDataSet\(Test.class.getResource\("temptable.dbunit.xml"\), false, true, false\)

the second parameter enables the column sensing which will ensure that new columns that did not appear in previous rows will be dynamically added to the DataSet. Probably your last paragraph is the problem you are facing.

regards,
matthias

http://www.dbunit.org/properties/primaryKeyFilter is only respected if it is set before the metadata is loaded for the first time in DatabaseTableMetadata. 
I'm not sure if this is by design.

If it is by design, I think that it should be explicitly stated on the documentation.
If not, I think that in DatabaseTableMetaData.getPrimaryKeys\(\), in addition to checking if \_primaryKeys is null, one should also check if the primaryKeysFilter property has been changed since its last invocation.

posts.subject: #258 DB2 problem with schema names
posts.author: matthieus

posts.text:
I found something, but there might be a deeper reason for what's happenning.

The fix actually solve part of the issue. The remaining issue is in the "DefaultMetadataHandler.matches\(...\) ", when "areEqualIgnoreNull\(catalog, catalogName, caseSensitive\)" is called.
"areEqualIgnoreNull" actually ignore a null argument, but only if the null argument is the first value, not the second one. And in the DB2 case, it is "catalogName" which is null \(the second one\).

The same could happen for the schema name, as the schema name can be null.
posts.subject: #258 DB2 problem with schema names

Hmm, I do not believe that the DefaultMetaDataHandler.matches\(\) method is the problem. Did you play around with the DatabaseConfig features/properties \(e.g. qualifiedTableNames - see http://www.dbunit.org/properties.html\)

If this does not help you can create your own MetaDataHandler using the DefaultMetaDataHandler as template and change the code so that it works for you \(configure your custom IMetaDataHandler via the DatabaseConfig object\). I'm curious what your problem might be.

tickets.description:
This bug might be linked to "DB2 problem after 2.4 - ID: 2549683". I actually got the same error.
I'm runnning 2.4.4 and also got the same issue with 2.4.5.

The exact error I got is actually a warning when I call "getTable". This happens only with DB2.

The stacktrace is:
"
WARNING: Cannot find column from ResultSetMetaData info via DatabaseMetaData. Returning null. Even if this is expected to never happen it probably happened due to a JDBC driver bug. To get around this you may want to configure a user defined interface org.dbunit.database.IMetadataHandler 
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Did not find column 'CASEID' for <schema.table> 'DWHTEST .TRANSFERERROR\_FACT' in catalog 'DWHTEST' because names do not exactly match.
at org.dbunit.database.ResultSetTableMetaData.scrollTo\(ResultSetTableMetaData.java:301\)
at org.dbunit.database.ResultSetTableMetaData.createColumnFromDbMetaData\(ResultSetTableMetaData.java:262\)
at org.dbunit.database.ResultSetTableMetaData.createMetaData\(ResultSetTableMetaData.java:154\)
at org.dbunit.database.ResultSetTableMetaData.createMetaData\(ResultSetTableMetaData.java:131\)
at org.dbunit.database.ResultSetTableMetaData.<init>\(ResultSetTableMetaData.java:97\)
at org.dbunit.database.AbstractResultSetTable.<init>\(AbstractResultSetTable.java:84\)
at org.dbunit.database.AbstractResultSetTable.<init>\(AbstractResultSetTable.java:63\)
at org.dbunit.database.ForwardOnlyResultSetTable.<init>\(ForwardOnlyResultSetTable.java:65\)
at org.dbunit.database.CachedResultSetTableFactory.createTable\(CachedResultSetTableFactory.java:52\)
at org.dbunit.database.QueryTableIterator.getTable\(QueryTableIterator.java:151\)
at org.dbunit.dataset.AbstractDataSet.initialize\(AbstractDataSet.java:111\)
at org.dbunit.dataset.AbstractDataSet.getTable\(AbstractDataSet.java:161\)
"

The origin of the error seems to be that there is a spare space in "'DWHTEST .TRANSFERERROR\_FACT'" compared to "'DWHTEST'".
\(Actually if you look at the stacktrace in the bug "DB2 problem after 2.4 - ID: 2549683", you can see that there is also a spare space for the schema name ...\)

When debugging the code I realized that at the line 227 in ResultSetTableMetaData:
"
String schemaName = rsMetaData.getSchemaName\(rsIndex\);
"
"schemaName" is returned with a spare space.

This seems to be a DB2 driver bug, but maybe dbunit could do a workaround to solve this issue, what do you think?

posts.subject: #259 DBUnit Batch statements not implemented properly
posts.author: porkchop009

tickets.summary: DBUnit Batch statements not implemented properly
tickets.description:
Batch statements do not appear to be implemented properly for refresh operations \(this probably applies to other ones as well\).

In RefreshStatement.execute, it uses the iterator to retrieve each ITable, one by one.
For each ITable, and for each row it tries to perform an 'update', and failing that, performs an 'insert'

the underlying statement that is used is SimplePreparedStatement

In RefreshStatement.UpdateRowOperation.execute, all the columns for a given row are collected, SimplePreparedStatement.addBatch is executed \(which executes the statement\) and then SimplePreparedStatement.executeBatch is called \(which deceptively enough, does not actually execute the batch\).

This isn't exactly a batch as the documentation makes it out to be \(if it is it's just 'batches' of one statement each\). The same goes for RefreshStatement.UpdateRowOperation

Throughout this process, there are neither references to DatabaseConfig.PROPERTY\_BATCH\_SIZE nor FEATURE\_BATCHED\_STATEMENTS. It seems as if those are only respected if IPreparedStatementFactory is used.



I hope that I did not miss any external mechanisms for keeping track of batch sizes

tickets.summary: Date and Time Tokens and Time-zones
tickets.description:
Date and time comparisons would be more practical if there were support for time-zones like UTF offset and tokens like "\[current-date\]" or "\[current-timestamp\]".

Tokens may be even more valuable if one can specify the date or time parts in a form like "\[yyyyMM\]" for the current year and month as "200909". Since such tokens may appear in non-date/time data there should probably be an option to enable them \(per element?\) which is disabled by default.

posts.subject: #261 Sybace
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
Version 2.4.5 Can not be used with Sybase JConnect \(jconn3.jar or jconn2.jar\) \(where 2.4.4 could\)
This is because org.dbunit.util.SQLHelper.getDatabaseInfo\(SQLHelper.java:279\) calls getDatabaseMajorVersion\(\).
Wrapping the logger.info call in a if\(logger.isInfoEnabled\(\)\) block would let us work around this by switching this logging for this class to FATAL.
This could also be a work around for other drivers that throw UnimplementedOperations for this log messages \(info doesn't appear to be used elsewhere\).

com.sybase.jdbc3.utils.UnimplementedOperationException: The method com.sybase.jdbc3.jdbc.SybDatabaseMetaData.getDatabaseMajorVersion\(\) has not been completed and should not be called.
	at com.sybase.jdbc3.jdbc.ErrorMessage.raiseRuntimeException\(Unknown Source\)
	at com.sybase.jdbc3.utils.Debug.notImplemented\(Unknown Source\)
	at com.sybase.jdbc3.jdbc.SybDatabaseMetaData.getDatabaseMajorVersion\(Unknown Source\)
	at org.dbunit.util.SQLHelper.getDatabaseInfo\(SQLHelper.java:279\)
	at org.dbunit.database.DatabaseDataSet.initialize\(DatabaseDataSet.java:201\)
	at org.dbunit.database.DatabaseDataSet.getTableMetaData\(DatabaseDataSet.java:275\)

posts.subject: #262 AbstractTableMetaData hardcoded dependency on IDataTypeFacto
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description:
AbstractTableMetaData has a hardcoded dependency on IDataTypeFactory implementation classes, e.g. OracleDataTypeFactory.

There should not be a hard dependency -- this design is not extensible. \(Users cannot provide their own IDataTypeFactory and have it work with the validation in AbstractTableMetaData.\)

As discussed on mailing list, perhaps better to add an interface IDbProductRelatable and implement the validation via that interface.

posts.text: - **status**: open --> closed-wont-fix
posts.subject: #137 Separate Maven module for dbunit-oracle
posts.author: jbhurst

posts.text:
We decided that rather than add a module for Oracle, we will include the Oracle JDBC driver as a dependency in the main module. Anyone who wants to build dbunit will need to download this dependency manually and add it to his local Maven repo.

I will raise another ticket to work on the static typing in the Oracle data type support and add instructions for adding the Oracle driver to the local Maven repo.
posts.subject: #137 Separate Maven module for dbunit-oracle
posts.author: jbhurst

tickets.description:
Currently the OracleDataTypeFactory and related classes are coded using reflection, to avoid a compile-time dependency on ojdbc\(14|6\).jar.

Split the Maven project into multiple modules so that Oracle-specific code can be in an optional module. Developers can add the ojdbc jar to their local Maven repos manually if they need to build the Oracle code.

I and Zdenek Vrablik have coded a patch for this. See http://github.com/johnbhurst/dbunit/tree/maven-multimodule/patch for the work in progress. \(You can download the current source tree for my working branch.\)

If this new module structure is accepted into DbUnit, I will rework and extend the OracleDataTypeFactory and related classes.

posts.subject: #263 Numbers with >19 digits cannot be saved in a BIGINT column
posts.author: gommma

posts.text:
The problem is that dbunit uses java.lang.Long to store data into SQL Types.BIGINT columns \(which is the recommended way to do this if you look into the jdbc APIs\). This does not work for numbers with >19 digits. I changed the dbunit DefaultDataTypeFactory to use the new BigIntegerDataType and committed the fix to SVN rev 1032/trunk.

Just checkout the latest source from SVN and it should work.

rgds,
matthias

tickets.description:

Hi, all
I have a problem to load a big integer value from xml file.

I created a table as the following:
CREATE TABLE statistic \(
big\_numeric\_value BIGINT UNSIGNED,
big\_delta\_value BIGINT UNSIGNED,
\);

In my dbunit xml file, I have the following two lines:
<statistic big\_numeric\_value="7446744073709551630" big\_delta\_value="1000000000000000005" />

When I load the testing data into database using the following codes:

ReplacementDataSet dataSet = new ReplacementDataSet\(new FlatXmlDataSet\(inputStream\)\);
DatabaseOperation.CLEAN\_INSERT.execute\(conn, dataSet\);

I got the following message:
Data truncation: Out of range value adjusted for column 'big\_numeric\_value' at row 1

I checked my database, the first line of dbunit xml file was loaded successfully. But the second line was not loaded, it seems the value is too big.

I can input that value by using Tora, but I can not load from dbunit.

Is there a way to load a big integer value from dbunit?

Thanks a lot.

\(Addendum: I was able to reproduce this on mysql\)

if it works via JUnit but not via ant you probably misconfigured or forgot to configure the IDataTypeFactory in the ant xml. Did you have a look at http://www.dbunit.org/anttask.html to check whether everything is properly set?
If you are still stuck please attach a simple testcase that lets me reproduce your problem.
rgds,
matthias

We havent faced this issue with Oracle 10g weblogic 9.2 and JDK 1.5. And i am not sure it could be the problem with the database. Please help me out on this.

posts.text:
Hi Roberto,

I did a somewhat bigger change now on the trunk rev. 1048 and introduced the FlatXmlDataSetBuilder which should now be used to create FlatXmlDataSet instances. I set the FlatXmlDataSet constructors to deprecated - there were just too many of them and adding some more would degrade readability and maintenability even more. I'm quite happy with the new solution using the builder pattern. Please have a look at it and tell me what you think. Was quite a bit more work than I expected...
If you happy with it we'll ship it with the 2.4.7 release

posts.subject: #138 Add support for dynamic replacements
posts.author: kylefdyer

posts.text:
I think if we put any of this kind of generic replacement dataset into the sources \(the proposed one or Johns\)  we should also rewrite the internals of the old ReplacementDataSet to use this new functionality. This avoids redundant code and improves reusability.
I'd vote +1 for this feature.
rgds,
matthias
posts.subject: #138 Add support for dynamic replacements
posts.author: gommma

tickets.description: I extended the replacement data set to allow for dynamic replacement.  Instead of replacing keys in the data with values from a Map this passes values in the dataset to a function \(registered with that table and column\) and replaces the values with the results of the function.  The function is passed the original value and a reference to the ITable object so it can grab values from other columns.  I use this to replace dates in my dataset with new dates that are timeshifted to new point in time.  Thanks for keeping up the good work. Hope this helps someone else.  I'll upload my code.  It is all new files with no changes to existing files.

posts.subject: #139 Fix "integration tests"
posts.author: jbhurst

posts.text:
I've got the integration tests working again, with Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, HSQLDB, Apache Derby and H2.

There are numerous issues remaining however:
\- Not yet working for MS SQL-Server, Sybase
\- Different database targets not supported by CI build
\- Integration tests are not separated from unit tests; it's not clear whether any given test requires a running database or not
\- DatabaseEnvironment/Profile mechanism is quite awkward for specifying which test/features are to be done for which database targets. Current system uses a string to specify features to skip. Better to specify features to include?
\- SQL DDL not executed by Maven. Confusing/difficult manual steps to set up database schema
\- Some integration tests \(e.g. SQLHelperDomainPostgreSQLTest\) don't play well in current framework. I.e. executes its own DDL in the middle of the test
\- "Fixing" the tests introduced a few ugly hacks \(marked "TODO"\) that need to be cleaned up.

Thus, I am keeping the ticket open for now while I work on these.
posts.subject: #139 Fix "integration tests"
posts.author: jbhurst

posts.text: Jeff converted the properties to maven profiles recently. Apart from that, we've also fixed most of the tests at this time and can run them against Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, HSQLDB, Derby and H2 in custom DbUnit Docker containers. The whole thing is wired up using Concourse CI, however, Concourse CI is still buggy in our test environments, seeking long term solution yet.

posts.subject: #139 Fix "integration tests"
posts.author: quantas

tickets.description:
Some DbUnit tests run against a real database. The real database environment is configured via the profile-properties file. There are a couple of errors in this file relating to Oracle \(e.g. dbunit.profile.oracle.unsupportedFeatures = INSERT\_INDENTITY\). Besides this, several tests fail when run on Oracle.

Review the tests and get them working on most/all of the supported databases.

Possibly replace profile.properties \(hardcoded URLs/user/passwords\) with Maven profiles/settings to make the build more flexible.

Possibly move integration tests from XxxTest classes to XxxIT, use Maven integration-test lifecycle phase. Automatically run DDL in pre-integration-test.

posts.subject: #140 Add support for Oracle SDO_GEOMETRY datatype
posts.author: jbhurst

posts.text:
I'll attach a new implementation which has the following changes:

1\. The original implementation used oracle STRUCT objects to read and write sdo\_geometry objects to/from the database.  To do this oracle requires a Connection.  This connection is not available in DataType.typeCast\(Object\) so there were some scenarios were it was impossible to call typeCast.  The new implementation uses oracle jpub to render classes corresponding the the sdo\_geometry related types.  Theses rendered classes have embeded type information and do not require a connection.

2\. Changed string parsing in typeCast to use regular expressions and handle more scenarios.

3\. Added test scenarios for OracleSdoGeometryDataType

4\. Added basic documentation in OracleSdoGeometryDataType.java on how to use jpub to render other oracle objects into classes for use with dbunit.

5\. StringDataType.typeCast was changed to call toString for unknown object types.  This is required for custom objects that do not fit the normal java.sql.\* datatypes.  Test cases for StringDataType were adjusted accordingly.

posts.text:
I'll attach a new implementation which has the following changes:

1\. The original implementation used oracle STRUCT objects to read and write sdo\_geometry objects to/from the database.  To do this oracle requires a Connection.  This connection is not available in DataType.typeCast\(Object\) so there were some scenarios were it was impossible to call typeCast.  The new implementation uses oracle jpub to render classes corresponding the the sdo\_geometry related types.  Theses rendered classes have embeded type information and do not require a connection.

2\. Changed string parsing in typeCast to use regular expressions and handle more scenarios.

3\. Added test scenarios for OracleSdoGeometryDataType

4\. Added basic documentation in OracleSdoGeometryDataType.java on how to use jpub to render other oracle objects into classes for use with dbunit.

5\. StringDataType.typeCast was changed to call toString for unknown object types.  This is required for custom objects that do not fit the normal java.sql.\* datatypes.  Test cases for StringDataType were adjusted accordingly.

posts.author: jbhurst

tickets.summary: Replace reflection-based Oracle data types with static calls
tickets.description:
The current Oracle data type support uses awkward and error-prone reflection to call Oracle-specific classes and methods in the Oracle JDBC driver.

This has been done to avoid a compile-time dependency on the Oracle JDBC JAR, which cannot be obtained automatically from a Maven repository.

The team has decided to make the Oracle JDBC JAR a dependency of the project, and do away with the reflection-based code.

posts.text:
Try adding:
config.setProperty\(DatabaseConfig.PROPERTY\_DATATYPE\_FACTORY, new Oracle10DataTypeFactory\(\)\);
where you configure your DbUnit connection. \(Oracle10DataTypeFactory is in org.dbunit.ext.oracle.\)

Also:
Do not use both classes12.jar and ojdbc14.jar. These are alternative Oracle JDBC driver implementations -- you should pick one, presumably the ojdbc14.jar version.

If you use ojdbc14.jar, the correct driver class is oracle.jdbc.OracleDriver. 

JH

posts.subject: #269 CLEAN_INSERT doesn't restart sequence value
posts.author: fcduarte83

posts.text:
Hi Felipe,
I think dbunit cannot be aware of the existence of the sequence and hence cannot reset or recreate it \(I only know this for sure with oracle where sequences are not linked with a table column\).
2 questions come to my mind right now:
1\. do you really need a specific sequence number which your testcase has to verify or could you also exclude it from the asserted columns \(if it is just needed for the ordering e.g.\)?
2\. one possible solution might be to drop and re-create the sequence after each testrun. I know this is not very nice and might also break if 2 unit tests are concurrently executed which need this one sequence. But actually it is not a good way to do so because the sequence is a DB synchronisation point which exists to let multiple threads retrieve unique values for an insert.

posts.author: gommma

posts.subject: #269 CLEAN_INSERT doesn't restart sequence value
posts.author: fcduarte83

posts.text:
Hi Felipe,
I'm still not sure what your testcase looks like but as far as I see you want to insert 2 objects into your 2 tables that are related to each other. So you should also specify the PK and FK in both XML dataset tables to establish the link properly:

<IMPOSTO\_RENDA DT\_INICIO\_VIGENCIA="2009-01-01" ID\_IMPOSTO\_RENDA="1"/>
<ALIQUOTAS\_IMPOSTO\_RENDA VL\_FAIXA\_INICIAL\_BASE\_CALC="0.01"
ID\_IMPOSTO\_RENDA="1" />

If the insert fails because of ConstraintViolation you might want to use negative sequence IDs in the XML to avoid a collision with the usually positive sequence number generated by the db:

<IMPOSTO\_RENDA DT\_INICIO\_VIGENCIA="2009-01-01" ID\_IMPOSTO\_RENDA="-1"/>
<ALIQUOTAS\_IMPOSTO\_RENDA VL\_FAIXA\_INICIAL\_BASE\_CALC="0.01"
ID\_IMPOSTO\_RENDA="-1" />

If you want to recreated the db sequence you have to do this via plain JDBC and issue the "drop sequence <sname>" and "create sequence <sname>" commands.

Further reading about sequences:
http://srivaths.blogspot.com/2007/04/dbunit-sequence-numbers-foreign-keys.html

rgds,
matthias

posts.text:
I agree that trace and the entry methods are the better choices.  For tracing, my favorite option is AOP, as it prevents the manual entry of all those method calls where needed \(and when you need them, they are never there\!\) and is very easy to turn on and off.  Of course, this requires an AOP framework, such as AspectJ or Spring AOP...
Additionally, if your app uses one of those, then your app requires no trace logging messages in dbUnit, as your AOP will add them.  In this case, it is best if dbUnit had no trace messages as the debug ones\!

Christopher, it would be great if you do a simple setup and convert a couple of classes and attach the files as a clear exhibit of your preferred suggested approach.
posts.subject: #145 Use SLF4J XLogger for execution tracing
posts.author: jeffjensen

posts.text:
I think two considerations has to be take in account:
1\. dbunit is a unit testing framework, not an application so the usage of AOP for logging could have some cons we need to take in consideration: another runtime dependency, more complex startup even if you only need INFO level \(the default\), etc...
2\. we introduced the logging statements to ease bug identification and resolution, allowing our users to give more detailed informations on what is happening on their machines and why their test are failing unexpectedly, not to print out the statements we are going to run on the database \(P6Spy is one option in this case\) nor to debug your unit tests
3\. the DEBUG level should be disabled by default and enabled only when problems arises INSIDE dbunit, or whenever you think the problem could be INSIDE dbunit, that's why we didn't make all so differences in logging statements: if you want to get our help in solving your problem the more informations we can have the faster we can get to the point.

With all this clear in mind do you really think it's worth to spend all the needed effort to refine all those logging statements \(by the way, I used log4e and I've added all logging with 1 click only...\)? Do you really think it is needed? Consider we have logging in dbunit since 2.1, the framework didn't had logging at all for many years and it has got a lot of attention by the community....

I often think I did a mistake adding the logging statements...
posts.subject: #145 Use SLF4J XLogger for execution tracing
posts.author: rlogiacco

posts.text:
In general, AOP for this kind of tracing stuff is nice, though I agree that's probably too large a hammer to be using on this particular problem.  A nice thing about this approach is that you don't need any new dependencies; you've already got SLF4J, and that's all you need.

I wasn't aware of log4e until now... looks like that probably mitigates concern \#2 to some degree.  Given your description of your intention regarding these logging statements, this issue probably all boils down to personal preferences to some degree.  That being said, I'll see if I can whip up some samples of what I was talking about in the next few days or so and put it out there, as Jeff suggested.

Thanks
posts.subject: #145 Use SLF4J XLogger for execution tracing
posts.author: *anonymous

posts.text:
Sorry, it must not be not clear in my first comments - the AOP influence would be from the application setup/tests using dbUnit, not dbUnit adding something, and mainly for TRACE level logging.  dbUnit need change nothing for users to have AOP handling.

I think the adding of the logging statements is a very good thing, and maybe even more are needed and/or just some adjustments\!

Christopher, your examples will clarify intentions and help influence direction, if any is taken to make changes \(as Roberto mentioned, it takes someone willing to take the time to do the changes.  Which means, as usual, "patches are welcome", but I do hate saying that... ;-\).

I use MSSQL Server 2008 DB and DBUnit 2.4.7. I set the MsSqlDataTypeFactory in the DatabaseConfig using

config.setProperty\(DatabaseConfig.PROPERTY\_DATATYPE\_FACTORY, new MsSqlDataTypeFactory\(\)\);

When I run the test I get this error message, which says the DefaultDataTypeFactory is still used instead of the MsSqlDataTypeFactory:

tickets.description:
I noted a compilation error with the current code base. This is related to org.dbunit.dataset.datatype.TestBlob referencing java.sql.SQLFeatureNotSupportedException which was only added in JDK 1.6 and is therefore not recognized by JDK 1.4.

I recommend replacing all references to SQLFeatureNotSupportedException with its ancestor class SQLException which is available in JDK 1.4.

posts.subject: #149 Add timezone support for timestamp data type
posts.author: jbhurst

tickets.summary: Add timezone support for timestamp data type
tickets.description:
Request:
Timezone support for the timestamp data type \(and probably for the date data type and the time data type as well\) would be very useful for some DbUnit users.



Analysis:
Currently, timezones are not supported since the parsing effort is being delegated to java.sql.Timestamp.valueOf\(\) which cannot handle timezones. Actually, Timestamp.valueOf\(\) silently uses the default timezone. This can lead to surprising results. Consider the following code and the two different outputs, depending on the default timezone:

System.out.println\( "jvm's timezone: " + TimeZone.getDefault\(\).getID\(\) \);
String input = "2007-12-05 10:00:00.000000000";
Timestamp ts = Timestamp.valueOf\( input \);
System.out.println\( input + "'s  is " + ts.getTime\(\) + " milliseconds since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 GMT" \);

\- with the default timezone of my system:

jvm's timezone: Europe/Zurich
2007-12-05 10:00:00.000000000 is parsed to be 1196845200000 milliseconds since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 GMT

\- in UTC:

jvm's timezone: UTC
2007-12-05 10:00:00.000000000 is parsed to be 1196848800000 milliseconds since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 GMT

\(credit to Guillaume Cottenceau, see http://archives.postgresql.org/pgsql-jdbc/2007-12/msg00014.php\)



Solution:
Use java.text.DateFormat.parse\(\) instead. See attached patch.

We need to decide what to do about the database products supported in DefaultDataTypeFactory. posts.subject: #150 General Logging Message Change Requests
posts.author: jbhurst

tickets.description:
Hi, I work for a company that uses DBunit to test on multiple databases and when we're using it to insert data into various tables for testing purposes, we have always encountered a lot of logging messages that we feel should probably only occur under debug logging, but these always show up under general info logging. I'm fairly new at this also, so please excuse my ignorance if I used a "dumb" fix or it sounds like I don't know what I'm talking about, because I probably don't, lol.

I personally went in and made my own changes, then rebuilt dbunit, and everything works the way we need it to. However, I don't want to contribute this because I'm not sure if everyone would want it this way or not. Thus, I'm just going to list here what I did and if you want to make these changes also you can.

\#1

Action: Changed BytesDataType.class to remove INFO logging and replaced it with DEBUG logging for the warning "Assuming given string to be Base64 and not a URI or File".

Reason: To prevent a warning message from displaying during every instance of an insert statement containing Byte values. This was coming up in our logs during every insert line containing a Byte value, making the log fairly huge and annoying.

\#2

Action: Changed DatabaseTableMetaData.class to comment out a logging message that was set for table name case correction.

Reason: To prevent an info logging from occurring during insert on any table using MySQL as the database. I had to comment this one out entirely for now \(it didn't have a debug/info option\) until we figure out the actual problem. It says it's for table name case correction, but everything looks correct from what we can tell, no case correction should be needed, and we just haven't had time to investigate further.

\#3

Action: Changed DefaultDataTypeFactory.class to include "Teradata", "Adaptive Server Enterprise", and "Netezza NPS" in the DATABASE\_PRODUCTS Collection. 

Reason: To prevent a warning message from displaying during the refresh for every insert into a table in regards to TeraData, Sybase, and Netezza being incompatible with the selected DataTypeFactory. The driver and/or version of the database we use for these must operate differently than the norm, as the naming convention being fetched wasn't matching what already existed in the class file. Thus, I added more. 

So yeah, like I said, I'm not especially savvy when it comes to this stuff, so maybe there were easier ways to get around all of this, but oh well, this is what I did and I'm just letting you all know in case you want to add it or make a better correction for me and include it in the next release.

THANK YOU IN ADVANCE\!\!

posts.subject: #277 I18N issue w/ Turkish database
posts.author: wrylpm5ltq

tickets.description:
A fix for i18n issue 1537894 was included in release 2.4.3. The fix is sufficient to resolve the issue if the underlying database has English-like collation, but isn't sufficient if the database is set to Turkish collation.

I'm using SQL Server 2k5, English windows regional setting, database set to Turkish collation, and DbUnit configured to be case insensitive. I'm attempting to use the DeleteAllOperation to wipe the database, and it's failing to find the table "\_Denied" \(lowercase "i"\) -- I get the following error "org.dbunit.dataset.NoSuchTableException: Did not find table '\_DENIED' in schema 'DBO'". Works fine if the underlying database is set to English collation.

The notes for issue 1537894 contain further background on the issue.

The current ReplacementDataSet is too limited. It's too hardcoded around strings and maps of replacements.

The concepts of wrapping dataset/tables to replace values in them, and actually replacing values, should be separated.

More interesting replacements are possible too:
\- Replace value with current time
\- Replace value prefixed "blob:" with BLOB content read from file/URL
\- Replace value prefixed "clob:" with text read from file/URL
\- Replace value prefixed "script:" with result of scripting function

... etc.

tickets.description:
dbunit version 2.4.7 - Oracle JDBC jar ojdbc14-10.2.0.1.0 

Restoring SDO\_GEOMETRY returns the following stacktrace:

...
Caused by: java.sql.SQLException: Internal Error: Inconsistent catalog view
	at oracle.jdbc.driver.DatabaseError.throwSqlException\(DatabaseError.java:158\)
	at oracle.jdbc.driver.DatabaseError.throwSqlException\(DatabaseError.java:206\)
	at oracle.sql.StructDescriptor.initMetaData1\_9\_0\(StructDescriptor.java:2095\)
	at oracle.sql.StructDescriptor.initMetaData1\(StructDescriptor.java:2034\)
	at oracle.sql.StructDescriptor.isInstantiable\(StructDescriptor.java:1212\)
	at oracle.sql.STRUCT.<init>\(STRUCT.java:142\)
	at org.dbunit.ext.oracle.OracleSdoGeometryDataType.typeCast\(OracleSdoGeometryDataType.java:346\)
	... 7 more

This problem can be fixed by qualifying the SDO\_GEOMETRY when using createDescriptor.

In other words, editing OracleSdoGeometryDataType:343 from:
StructDescriptor geometryDescriptor = StructDescriptor.createDescriptor\(SDO\_GEOMETRY, connection\);
to:
StructDescriptor geometryDescriptor = StructDescriptor.createDescriptor\("MDSYS." + SDO\_GEOMETRY, connection\);

fixes the issue. The other call on line 336 should also be changed, but I do not have a test case for this one.

posts.subject: #154 Plain text data set
posts.author: achelis

tickets.description:
It would be nice to have a simpler way of writing datasets and to have datasets in an easier to read format. My personal opinion is that XML files can get a bit bloated at times.

An example syntax could be:
\*\*authors\*\*
id | name
\-----------
0  | Iain M Banks
1  | Stephen King

\*\*books\*\*
id | category
\-------------
0  | Fantasy
1  | Sci-Fi 

While this format will have some restrictions compared to XML - lack of foreign/primary keys etc. I think it can still be useful. The idea is to provide something with a very simple, easy to read syntax, which can be used for the majority of db-tests.

A sample implementation has been attached.

posts.subject: #155 Implement DBUnit connection pooling/caching
posts.author: gerald_quintana

tickets.description:
As described in DBUnit FAQ, to improve performance, it is very important to keep DBUnit connection opened as long as possible to avoid metadata information loading. Yet no class is provided by DBUnit to achieve this.

For a real project I developped a PoolingDatabaseConnectionProvider which I find very useful \(see attached patch\) and brought use to reduce by 30% unit test duration. The main idea is to keep it with a singleton and reuse it in all test methods. See the ProviderDBTestCaseIT to see how it works.

About connection closing, as I keep DBUnit connections opened as long as possible it's difficult to determine when to close them, here are sev- eral ideas:
\- Add a fake EndTestCase in each TestSuite
\- Implement finalize method in provider --> done
\- Register shutdown hook
\- Declare the provider singleton with Spring Fwk, use DI to get it in test case and destroy-method to close connectiosn --> my favorite solution

posts.author: paulp3210

posts.text:
Paul,

Which DataTypeFactory were you using for the case producing your stack trace?

Your stack trace indicates the exception is thrown in org.dbunit.dataset.datatype.ClobDataType.setSqlValue\(ClobDataType.java:71\).

The OracleDataTypeFactory creates a OracleClobDataType for handling CLOBs. The Oracle10DataTypeFactory creates a StringDataType for handling CLOBs. Neither matches your stack trace.

I have been working to expand the DbUnit integration tests so that we can test as much DbUnit functionality on as many different databases as possible. I have been running tests against 11gR1, but am hoping to set up an 11gR2 environment soon. Also, I hope to expand the test support for CLOB and XMLType to make it more robust.

Certainly I would like to reproduce your problem and fix it, and I also hope to have a closer look at code changes between 2.2.1 and now soon.

John Hurst

I have used the latest svn version to import/export data from Oracle 10g.
If I have XMLTYPE fields in my tables, the import fails with ORA-03113 \(with OCI protocol\) error.

The export works perfectly.

I have found the problem into OracleXMLTypeDataType. This class defines the XMLTYPE as a BLOB type, but Oracle use CLOB as default storage type for XMLTYPE field.

I have changed the class from Blob to Clob and correct the setSqlValue method

daniele

For a FlatXmlDataSet the following:

<table column1="abc" column2=""/>

where column2 is a varchar not null, causes a SQLException with a constraint violation that an attempt has been made to insert a null value in a non-nullable column. An empty string should be passed instead, as null can easily be represented by omitting attributes.

posts.author: dfghi

posts.text: Newer versions of dbcp may like better this.
posts.subject: #290 Add support for Postgresql 8 Blobs.
posts.author: dfghi

posts.text:
The version of PostgresqlBlobDataType in the zip file should be used with dbcp version 1.2, newer versions should use the attached one, but that requires to set the property "accessToUnderlyingConnectionAllowed" to true in the data source.
A good solution would be to get an entirely new connection in another way, getting the datasource from the factory \(if possible\) or from some other source that should be clearly accessible from the PostgresqlBlobDataType class.
posts.subject: #290 Add support for Postgresql 8 Blobs.
posts.author: dfghi

tickets.description:
Recent versions of Postgresql prefer to handle blobs \(large objects or "lo", as posgresql documentation calls them\) in a separate system table, leaving only a reference \(oid\) in the original table. This means that it's not possible to use DbUnit to insert Blobs directly in tables. The attached file provides a patch to the existing DataTypeFactory and a new DataType for Postgresql Blobs.
It's a draft because I'm not totally sure of a couple of things, but it's better than nothing.

posts.author: gommma
